Skilled with the sword, warriors specialize in physical attacks and\nboast high defenses. In time, they can grow to master a variety of\noffensive abilities.
Healers who concoct medicines to treat the sick and injured.\nThough apothecaries excel at restoring HP and preventing status\nailments, they can also go on the offensive with their axes.

Ruffians who mastered the art of stealing to survive, thieves can take\nall manner of things from their targets, including items, HP, and SP.\nExtremely nimble, they are fleeter of foot than anyone else.
Ever in the pursuit of knowledge, scholars specialize in powerful\nelemental attacks. Given time, they can harness multiple elements,\nallowing them to skillfully exploit their foes' weaknesses.
Servants of the Order of the Sacred Flame. They can use the magic\npowers granted to them by the heavens to safeguard allies and\nvanquish evil foes.
Expert trackers who never lose their prey, hunters are adept at\nphysical attacks and inflicting foes with status ailments. They use\ntheir inherent agility to make quick work of their targets.
Responsible for distributing goods all across Orsterra, merchants\nearn leaves and JP in a variety of ways. They support their allies\nwith polearm or bow in hand.
Dancers are entertainers skilled in both song and dance. Their cheers\nbolster ally stats, and they can use their fan to elegantly dispose of\nenemies.
Select a weapon and deal damage to a foe 2 time(s).
Grant HP recovery to an ally and restore SP by 10%.
Lower Phys. Atk. and Phys. Def. of a foe for 2 turn(s).
You wanted to learn how to fight as a member of the watch.\nSo your father, the watch captain, taught you how to strike\ndown enemies.
You wanted to learn how to heal the injured. So your mother,\na former cleric, taught you how to ease the pain of others.
You showed a great interest in architecture. So the local carpenter\ntaught you about everything you needed to know about structures.

A grilled beef dish that fills you with strength.
A light snack that gives you a boost of energy.
A simmered lamb dish that bolsters your strength in a pinch.
A chicken dish that enhances your concentration.
A noodle dish that can be slurped down quickly.
A stewed vegetable dish that leaves you invigorated.
A grilled fish dish that clears your head.
A key engraved with the crest of a dagger.
A fragment of a letter. Its content cannot be deciphered.
Grants the following effects for 15 turns:\n- Elem. Def. +20%/Phys. Def. +15%\n- Fire/Ice/Lightning/Wind Resistance +15%
A mushroom bed that can be maintained to yield a harvest of\nmushrooms at a later date.
A bracelet you received several years back on the Day of Reverence.\nIt was made by your childhood friend, Stia, who has one just like it.
A wristband given to you by senior watchman Phenn. Apparently,\nyou're supposed to wear it when you want to get fired up.
A talisman gifted to you by Deacon Laurana after you fell and\ninjured yourself. It carries the blessing of the Sacred Flame.
A key you picked up in the town square when you were a child.\nRumor has it that a certain warrior is looking for this key.
You vaguely remember receiving this from a mysterious kitten\nwhen you were very young.
A dish favored by a once-famous actor whose whereabouts are\ncurrently unknown.
Special nuts given to you by the vegetable-loving Basil.\nThey were simply too precious for you to eat.
A bed of mushrooms entrusted to you by a traveler from Emberglow\nlong ago. Apparently, they're a local specialty.
Raise the amount restored by the equipped character's HP/SP recovery skills.
The equipped character will restore 2 BP when near death.
You devised this skill to fight back against tyranny while remembering\nthe carnage from that day.
You learned all you could about healing, and acquired ways to\nefficiently rejuvenate both body and spirit.
While scrounging for food in the forest, you learned ways to survive\nin even the harshest conditions.
ただ歩くだけ 他には何も望まない
この暗がりの道を 照らす灯りさえあれば
通り過ぎた町と人は 色褪せても消えず
旅路が途切れることはない
踏みしめる道は 戦いの傷で覆われて
白く降り積もった 痛みと悲しみを
この手で掬う その先に……
幾千の時を越えて あなたに巡り会った
この胸の静寂に 温かな声が聴こえる
“何が為に生まれた？” 私を抱く手はもうない
ただ歩く 声のする方へ
幾千の時を越えて それでもあなたに会いたい
その場所は 歩くには遠すぎる
私を呼び続ける声 ただ歩く
人は光持って生まれ 底知れぬ谷へ堕ちていく
“何が為に生まれた？”　“この手は何を掴む？”
答えの無い螺旋の中で 手繰り寄せられて
ふたりの摩擦が生んだのは 新たな灯火
ただ歩くだけ 他には何も要らない
ここにある灯りが 私の道を照らすから
There's naught else I long for—
But as long as I hold
A dim lamp for this road
All the souls I've walked past
All the pain and the sorrow
I can hear a warm voice
From a breast that is stilled:
Though the place where you are
Is much too far to walk.
To where I know truth lies.
Tell me why we're put here;
What can we hope to grasp?
Devoid of answers we draw closer,
Light the road that sustains me...
In memory and honor of Asuma san.

エネミーテスト（アメリカ）

Ring of the Prince of Thieves
Ring of the Lady of Grace
Woman Who Is Stern to Strangers
Bard Who Sings of a Dragon Slayer

A cleric dispatched from the church's headquarters. She cares for Rinyuu, a woman from a distant land, and tends to her every need. After witnessing the way people live under both the protection and dominion of the Scarlet Wings, she has begun to rethink what the blessing of the Sacred Flame truly is.
Make a face all you want.\nI still won't give it to you!
Okay. But take good care of it!
Is there something you wish to\nspeak about?

An archbishop with jurisdiction over the Frostlands. He was entrusted with the great responsibility to perform the Kindling ceremony in order to spread the Sacred Flame's power over twenty years ago. While the task was an arduous one, the trust the people placed in him as a result was well worth the effort.
I'm afraid I cannot give you that.
If I can be of use to you, then\ncertainly.
Please use this for the good of\nthe realm.

The only son of the great actress Francesca was sent away from their manor in Theatropolis to live with his mother's friend in Atlasdam. He too may one day walk the path of the thespian, for he has shown interest in stage acting, and is often seen reading difficult screenplays.
Here, take this. Now leave me alone.
I have time to talk a little.
The life of the Bargello family party who always has a joke at the ready and gets scolded for it in turn. Though his words may be crude, he has a heart of gold and loves his family more than anyone.
I got a whole lotta stories for ya!
Sorry, I'm kinda busy. Maybe later!
Guess this makes you a covetous\nchicken!

A poet who once competed with Auguste for fame. He was one of the playwright's few friends with whom he could discuss his views on art. It seems as though he found inspiration in Auguste's final work.
Unfortunately,\nI cannot give that to you.
Your life story interests me, as well.
No, I don't sense any spark from you.
Haha... Make good use of it.

A cleric who roams the land and reports his findings on each region to the pontiff. He is both intelligent and well-versed in creeds other than the Order of the Sacred Flame. He recently visited Wispermill in the Flatlands and now harbors an interest in their heretical beliefs.
Now is not the time for that.
A stray dog that lives at the town's shop. Whenever a powerful person visits the shop, he wags his tail and welcomes them with glee. His personal history is shrouded in secret.
You look like you can handle yourself.\nSure, I'll listen to what you have to say.
That's right. Tuck your tail and run.

A hunter who lives a solitary life with his dog some distance from town. His grandmother, who was an apothecary, taught him everything he knows, including the axiom, "A blessing shared is a blessing doubled," which convicts him to share his game with the townspeople when harvests are poor. Even so, he can be a bit hotheaded at times.
All right! If you beat me,\nI'll fork over everythin' I got!
I'll give it to ye if you can beat me!
Don't see no point in tellin' ye about it.
Good gods! Yer a strong one, all right!

A man who joined the family after Bargello's abilities won him over. Despite being extremely hot-blooded, his past in the mafia has made him into a loyal man of his word. His favorite "powder" is specially made with only the hottest of spices and would leave any ordinary person writhing in agony for hours...
A fight, huh? Then let me show you\nthe might of my latest powder!

The Order's hierarchy of power is this: pontiff, archbishops, bishops, priests, and then deacons. I tolerated much to finally attain the status of bishop. I will not lose my position over something like this!
I refuse to yield anything\nto the likes of you!

A shrewd syndicate member at the head of an up-and-coming family. With the backing of Leopaldi, he forcefully took over small mafia families one by one and claimed the old quarter of Victors Hollow as his own. He believed that power was everything until discovering a certain unbridled pleasure...
A man who got involved with the underworld for the thrill of it. He once stood at the top of his own small family having gained power under Leopaldi the Wealthy, but now works for Bargello.
This isn't for you. It's for her...
The only one who'll be getting\nanything from me is that lady...

A girl who sells flowers to provide for her two younger brothers. She used to sell powder, but has since washed her hands of that business. Her income isn't quite what it used to be, but now she lives respectably, and is cheerful and focused on the future.
A man of few, but chosen words who has closed off his heart because of an unspeakably dark past...or so people think. In truth, he may just be repeating his favorite phrase.
The daughter of Father Monaris. She misses him dearly and spends her idle hours gazing at the flowers he sent her.
If you really want it, then...
A priest who spends his days pondering what he can do in this town where the Scarlet Wings hold so much sway. Every year, he sends his beloved daughter flowers from each region for her birthday.
I pray it will be of use to you.
I'm afraid that just isn't possible.

The daughter of Father Monaris. She misses him dearly and spends her idle hours gazing at the flowers he sent her. Though a quiet girl, at times, her eyes convey all kinds of emotion as she stares at the blossoms before her.
An apprentice cleric of the Order of the Sacred Flame. Though once blind, she gradually regained her sight after the scarlet snow fell.
I'm sorry, but I cannot give you this.
If this will suffice, please take it...
If it's something I can help you with,\nthen of course.

Headmaster Yvon's secretary is extremely capable, assisting him in his work and research that both might proceed smoothly. Strict and demanding, she chastises anyone who breaks the rules, but many students in fact appreciate her uncompromising nature.
I trust you will not disappoint me.
Can I help you with something?

A bright young woman known as the "Sun of the Flatlands," said to be an even greater beauty than her mother, the queen. She dedicates her days to her education, in hopes of better serving her people in the future.
Please, put this to good use.
I will gladly share my story with you.
I would like to hear your story,\nnot share with you mine.

A young woman in the employ of the Royal Library of Atlasdam, she has no shortage of admirers among the scholars of the academy. Though she left the Riverlands when she was but a child, her heart still belongs to a certain someone there.
I must ask that you follow the proper\ncheckout procedures.
Have you found what you were\nsearching for?
Are you interested in my work?
I'm sorry, but I'm quite busy.

The youngest scholar ever to claim the position of headmaster. His tenure has been marked by a pronounced shift in the guiding principles of the institution: knowledge is now to be hoarded, rather than shared with the masses. While many object, none can deny that his other policies have been beneficial for the academy, and so he has the support of many scholars.
Don't underestimate me because\nI'm a scholar!
What are you doing? Leave at once!
Are you satisfied? Then take your leave.

A bishop dispatched from the Order of the Sacred Flame to oversee Emberglow in the wake of Tytos's death. He once colluded with Tytos to line his own pockets, but was exposed by the Chosen One and imprisoned. He has recently befriended a mouse that visits his cell from time to time, and has named it after Saint Graecia.
Have you come to mock me?

A man who joined the family after Bargello's character won him over. Despite being extremely hot-blooded, his past in the mafia has made him into a loyal man of his word. His favorite "powder" is specially made with only the hottest of spices and would leave any ordinary person writhing in agony for hours...

A girl who sells flowers to provide for her two younger brothers. She used to sell powder, but has since washed her hands of that business. Her income isn't quite what it used to be, but now she lives respectably, and is cheerful and focused on the future.
I'm scared that if I talk about it,\nit might vanish like a dream.
A man of few, but chosen words who has closed off his heart because of an unspeakably dark past...or so people think. In truth, he may just be repeating his favorite phrase.

A member of Bargello's family. She survived the underworld thanks to her silver tongue and bewitching good looks. According to a certain mafia family in Victors Hollow, she is "a woman who can loosen anyone's lips."
Let me check something first.\nI'll get back to you.

The sovereign of the largest nation in the Flatlands. He has a strong interest in academics and culture, and has leveraged his knowledge and political power to overcome many obstacles. His nature compels him to intervene in difficult situations, and as such, his throne is often left empty.
Hahaha, this I cannot give to you.
Hm, perhaps it is time for a chat.
Have you no respect for a king?

A subordinate of Lord Eltrix of Grandport. He was once a pirate, but after being bested by Eltrix, his friends convinced him to join her side. His laid-back way of speaking belies his superior navigation skills. He is said to have been a thorn in the Grandport navy's side during his pirating days.
Let's not be greedy, all right?
If you want it that badly, you can\nhave it.

A subordinate of Lord Eltrix of Grandport. He was undefeated as a pirate until losing a duel to Eltrix. Impressed by her strength, he then decided to join her side. He loves to fight and spends his spare time in taverns looking for trouble.
I've been itching for a fight!\nIvri, Wappa! Let's go!
Haha! You've got a long way to go!
Heh. I should've seen that coming.\nYou sure are strong.
I'm working, if you can't tell.

A subordinate of Lord Eltrix of Grandport. Once the captain of a pirate crew in the Stormy Seas, he was defeated by Eltrix and fell in love with her personality. Despite his humorous and excitable nature, his loyalty is second to none, and he will put his own life on the line to protect his lord when danger arises.
C'mon now. I'm sure you can do\nbetter than that!
Sorry, I'm kinda busy right now.

The life of the Bargello family party who always has a joke at the ready and gets scolded for it in turn. Though his words may be crude, he has a heart of gold and loves his family more than anyone. Lately, he's been heading to White Grape Hill in his free time to tell his late parents and friends about his life.
I got a whole lotta stories for ya!
Sorry, I'm kinda busy. Maybe later!
Guess this makes you a covetous\nchicken!

A man who joined the family after Bargello's abilities won him over. Despite being extremely hot-blooded, his past in the mafia has made him into a loyal man of his word. His favorite "powder" comprises a Valorian mustard base mixed with the Sunlands's legendary sufra masala. It will singe the throats of anyone but Rosso.
A fight, huh? Then let me show you\nthe might of my latest powder!

A man of few, but chosen words who has closed off his heart because of an unspeakably dark past...or so people think. In truth, he may just be repeating his favorite phrase. Despite his reticence, he is loyal to the family and trusts deeply in <chara_name> who saved Bargello.

A member of Bargello's family. She survived the underworld thanks to her silver tongue and bewitching good looks. Once a scholar, she has extensive knowledge in all fields and leverages that intelligence to help rebuild Valore.
You wanna have some fun, too?
Take this for me, will you?
Hugo, Captain of the Knights Ardante

A leader of the Knights Ardante. Blessed with intelligence and swordsmanship, his surly and disgruntled yet caring nature has earned him the respect of his men. He strives to rebuild the Knights Ardante amidst the aftermath of the chaos born from the Scarlet Wings and the church.
Do you have time for a chat?
I'm afraid I'm a little tied up.
The man who governs the Order of the Sacred Flame. To serve as a role model for those in the church, he always acts with discipline and preaches the teachings of the Sacred Flame.
This, too, is the will of the Sacred Flame.
I'm sorry, but I need this.
A good chance to hone my skills!
Did you go easy on me?
I don't have time for that right now.
Here you go. I hope you make\ngood use of it!

An adventurer who sailed the seas aboard the Queen Orsa, a magnificent ship that was crafted by a master shipwright from Rippletide. On his 88th trip, the ship was attacked and severely damaged by monsters. He managed to make his way to shore, but later discovered that the love of his life had disappeared. Now he spends his days drowning himself in alcohol, trying to forget all that he has lost.
Let me tell you about this ship...
Your hometown, eh?\nIf you have need of me, I'll go.
I'm not ready to part with the sea\njust yet.

Marjim the adventurer's love. She's spent more time waiting for her beau to return from sea than with him on land, but listening to his stories as he devours her cooking is a treasure she wouldn't trade for anything in the world.
Oh, I'm sorry. I can't give this to you.
I hope you're ready for a tale!
I'll need some time before I'm ready.
If you need it, it's yours.
An adventurer who has sailed from the Middlesea to the Outersea upon his ship, the Queen Orsa. He is excited to embark upon a new voyage around Orsterra at the behest of <chara_name>.
Apologies, but you can't have that.
I'm a little overwhelmed right now...

A young man from Wishvale. Though a little rough around the edges, he is dauntless and brave and stands up for what he believes in. In order to keep his promise to his late fiancée, Nellum, he swore to survive no matter what came his way.
We don't have time for that\nright now.
You should be ashamed of yourself\nfor trying to haggle with a child.
I guess I can tell you.
You want it that badly? Fine...
Sorry, I'm a little busy right now...
Can you give me some time?

A warrior who is traveling in order to hone his skills. While he normally possesses a genteel demeanor, he is quite ferocious in battle. He goes to great lengths to ensure he looks presentable at all times, and even mends his own clothes. Lately, he has been distressed over the loss of his favorite sewing set.

A warrior with a genteel demeanor who is traveling in order to hone his skills. He is the son of a swordplay instructor, and was taught to make himself presentable at all times. The mystic key he possesses has been passed down in his family for generations.

A warrior with a genteel demeanor who is traveling in order to hone his skills. He is the son of a royal swordplay instructor, and is also a talented sewer, so that he can ensure he looks presentable at all times. After solving the mystery of the key that had been in his family for generations, he obtained a sacred treasure, but that hasn't diminished his passion for adventure.
Apologies, but I cannot surrender\nthat to you.
By all means, please take it.
You wish to know more about me?
I was just thinking of getting myself\na new sewing set.
I am overcome with emotion. I did\nnot think it possible to obtain the\neight sacred treasures...

A warrior who hails from the distant east, where she is undefeated. She has come to Orsterra in search of worthy opponents. As a guest of Wordsworth, she has been given special treatment, but she still prefers the chicken skewers she used to eat at home over the lavish food provided for her here.

A warrior who hails from the distant east, where she is undefeated. She has come to Orsterra in search of worthy opponents. Because she escaped poverty through her skill with the blade, she values strength above all else. Currently, she wants to pursue even more rigorous training so that she may one day surpass <chara_name>.

A warrior who hails from the distant east, where she is undefeated. She has come to Orsterra in search of worthy opponents. She escaped from poverty through her skill with the blade, and is now strong enough to defeat any monster that stands before her. After fighting alongside <chara_name>, she has gotten a feel for their true strength and hopes to beat them someday.
I expected no less.\nHere, take it.
Shall we speak frankly with one\nanother?
Are these...chicken skewers!?\nThis brings back memories.
A battle to the death with monsters...\nIt was a thrilling experience, to say\nthe least.
I have no intention of becoming\nfriends with you.

A renowned merchant who largely operated in the Sunlands. Though many make light of him due to his youthful appearance, his business acumen is not to be underestimated. He sees great potential in Wishvale, but dearly misses the curry with rice he used to have in Sunshade.

A renowned merchant who largely operated in the Sunlands. Though many make light of him due to his youthful appearance, his business acumen is not to be underestimated. He has become friendly with the people of Wishvale and has lately taken to hanging around the tavern to gather information.

A renowned merchant who largely operated in the Sunlands. Though many make light of him due to his youthful appearance, his business acumen is not to be underestimated. After learning of his beloved's death, he has finally come to terms with all that happened, pledging to expand his business through honest methods in keeping with his love's desires.
Ugh... Fine, take it, ya thief!
Hey, this is curry with rice!\nMy favorite!
Thanks to you, I found out what\nhappened to Molly, and I'm glad\nI did.
Sorry, but I'm still gettin' set up.

A merchant who grew up in a small village on the outskirts of the Riverlands. She worms her way into people's hearts with her warmth and friendliness, and uses her great business sense to ensure she never misses an opportunity to make some leaves. River bean patties have been her favorite food ever since she was little.

A merchant who grew up in a small village on the outskirts of the Riverlands. Though she seems like a gentle, simple sort, she has great business sense. She has an uncle who works as an apothecary in Donescu, and is concerned about their reconstruction efforts.

A merchant who grew up in a small village on the outskirts of the Riverlands. Though she seems like a gentle, simple sort, she has great business sense. Now that the situation in Donescu has been resolved, she has decided to base herself in Wishvale and expand her business.
Whoa there! Don't you think\nyou're being a bit pushy?
Make good use of that, all right?
Donescu's on the road to recovery,\nand it's all thanks to you!
A thief who worked as a bodyguard at a tavern. His knife skills are second to none. He is extremely devoted to the Flamebringer and prays every single day. His favorite food is sautéed veal.

A thief who worked as a bodyguard at a tavern. His knife skills are second to none. He is extremely devoted to the Flamebringer and prays every single day. White hollowlilies seem to hold some special significance for him.

A thief who was once a member of a syndicate. His knife skills are second to none. Once during a squabble between the syndicates, he did something he could never take back, and ever since then, he periodically visits the flower patch by Victors Hollow.
Sorry, but I can't give this to anyone,\nnot even you.
What, you want to know my life story?
Sautéed veal, cooked just like how they\nmake it in Cragspear... You went all out.
Hollowlilies... Did you pick these\nfor me?

When she was a child, her birth father sold her off in exchange for a ring, causing her to believe that was her true worth and develop a deep obsession with rings. Though now she's more obsessed with <chara_name> and wants to try roast iridove.

When she was a child, her birth father sold her off in exchange for a ring, causing her to develop a deep obsession with rings. Now she's more obsessed with <chara_name>. Living in Wishvale has reminded her of a picture book she loved when she was a little girl.

When she was a child, her birth father sold her off in exchange for a ring, causing her to develop a deep obsession with rings. Now she's more obsessed with <chara_name>. Despite her claims that Wishvale is too peaceful for her tastes, she seems to have taken a liking to the town.
Oh? Looking for a fight, are you?
Have you gotten even stronger?\nI just can't seem to win against you.
Want to hear my story, do you?
Roast iridove, huh.\nDoes this mean I'm officially\npart of your town, then?
<i>Wish Upon the Shooting Star</>!\nDid you get this just for me?
I'm afraid my history would bore you.

An apothecary who set off on a journey in order to save as many people as possible. He greatly respects his father who was also an accomplished apothecary, and dearly misses the seafood sauté his father used to make for him. He regrets never having learned to make that special dish for himself.

An apothecary who set off on a journey in order to save as many people as possible. He greatly respects his father who was also an accomplished apothecary, and studies every day in order to live up to that legacy, but has found the road to be long and difficult at times. He'd love to hear a word of encouragement from his father if he could.

An apothecary who set off on a journey in order to save as many people as possible. He was desperate to follow in the footsteps of his father, who was also an accomplished apothecary, but it seems his father has already recognized him as an equal. Knowing this, he has resolved to take a new step forward, keeping the words of his father in his heart: "Treat all those in front of you."
This is all a part of my training!
You got me...\nAll right, here you are.
Is this...my father's seafood sauté?\nI never thought I'd get to eat it again!
This letter... It's from "Toulo"...\nMy father wrote this!?
Oh... Sorry, could you wait a moment?

An itinerant apothecary who is generally easygoing, but becomes deadly serious when it comes to combatting disease, refusing to let the matter drop until others understand that no illness can be taken lightly. She is currently searching for a nourishing root, one of many medicinal herbs.

An itinerant apothecary who is generally easygoing, but becomes deadly serious when it comes to combatting disease. She is researching nourishing roots in order to cure her younger brother's terrible affliction, but she lacks proper materials and has yet to make much progress. A foreign tome may be of use to her.

An apothecary who was traveling around in order to craft a cure for her younger brother. Thanks to the medicinal herb and book provided by <chara_name>, she was able to do just that, and has now resolved to pay them back by devoting herself to Wishvale's development.
I'm sorry, but I cannot relinquish that.
Please use this, if it will help.

A scholar who is researching the magic of ancient civilizations. On her travels with <chara_name>, she discovered the truth she sought, and plans to return to the Royal Academy of Atlasdam to write about her findings.
An actor whose goal is to spread hope and joy through his performances. After putting on an outdoor production in Wishvale, he has gained many fans who joyfully repeat the songs and lines he wrote.

A dancer known as the Songbird of Succor. Because <chara_name> is leaving on a journey, she is looking for a new dance partner who can move like a marmot, shimmy like a snow drake, and bound like a blue bull.

A warrior known as the Unbending Blade. Though he regrets being unable to protect his king and country in the past, seeing <chara_name> restore Wishvale to its former glory has inspired him to wield his blade for others once more.

A merchant with a keen eye. She believes that her good judge of character is what enabled her to encounter and journey with <chara_name>. Recently, her father has begun urging her to return home, causing her to wonder what she ought to do.

A highly skilled thief used to traveling alone. He has developed a strange concern for <chara_name>'s well-being, but plans to return to his life as a lone wolf soon and is already considering pulling off a big heist.

An apothecary from Clearbrook who decided to travel with <chara_name> in order to become more like the person who saved him in his youth. Recently, he has begun to worry about the condition of his ill mother back home.

A serious hunter who has learned from her uninhibited master, Z'aanta, what not to do. She traveled with <chara_name> at her master's behest, and through this experience has learned to trust others just as much as she trusts her partner, Linde.
After being orphaned in the war, she was adopted by Archbishop Josef and raised alongside his daughter, Lianna. She now considers <chara_name> as a member of her family, too.

A scholar who teaches at the Royal Academy of Atlasdam. His thirst for knowledge drove him to join <chara_name> in their travels, but he has recently begun to receive numerous letters from both his students and the headmaster, and his messenger crow can barely handle the load.

A dancer who works at a tavern in Sunshade. Though not one to open up to others, after traveling with <chara_name>, she has begun to consider telling them about her past and what she hopes to accomplish.
The lord of the new and improved Valore. He considers <chara_name> a true friend after all they have been through together, and trusts them fully. He has also been helping to restore Wishvale.
The queen of Edoras who, with <chara_name>'s help, slew Pardis III and drove back Empress Tatloch of G'roha. She and King Richard of Riven now have a son named Elman.
The king of Riven who is doing his best to rebuild his country after his father drove it to ruin. He and Queen Alaune of Edoras now have a son named Elman.
The king of Altinia, known for his abundant wisdom. He occasionally walks around town disguised as a merchant, but all the citizens know who he is.

The lord of Grandport and aunt of Queen Alaune of Edoras. She captains her own fleet and is not afraid to join any fray, both traits which have dazzled many a pirate into joining her crew. She becomes particularly impassioned at the Merchants' Fair, which leads to her spending more than she should.
A Knight Ardante who inherited the blue flame. Even now, he cannot forget the former Flameguard, who he held in high esteem. He walks with the flame, holding his resolve close to his heart.

The empress of the western nation of G'roha. She invaded Orsterra in an attempt to conquer it, but was thwarted by <chara_name> and their friends. Nevertheless, she still has not lost her desire to expand her territory, and plans to search for new lands to conquer instead.

A cait scholar who is researching the divine rings. His work progressed by leaps and bounds after traveling with <chara_name>, the Chosen One, and now he plans to settle in Wishvale to write a book about all he has discovered.
A former member of Wishvale's watch. Feeling guilty over his inability to protect the town, he hid in a cave in the Frostlands and devoted himself to getting stronger.
Fine... I'll return to town. I should\nvisit their graves, anyway.
I...I can't. How could I possibly\nface them?

A hunter who grew up in the deserts of the Sunlands. She takes great pride in the clan she belongs to, and refuses to change out of her light traditional outfit no matter what environment she finds herself in. She is currently hoping to test her strength in this new land.
You face Delitia of the Aqeeq!
How about a sparring match?\nI could use the exercise.
I'm a little busy right now.
That's not enough to warm me up.\nAchoo!

The most popular dancer in Sunshade. Many a traveler has been mesmerized by the striking juxtaposition between her somber expression and her passionate dances. Lately, Helgenish has been insisting that she draw in more new customers, which has caused her quite a bit of grief.
A scholar who hails from the Royal Academy of Atlasdam. She is currently journeying across the land to research ruins and collect her own thoughts.
That would be as pearls before\nan indifferent cat to you.
Hm? Has that piqued your curiosity?
Is there something you wish to know?

Oh... That dish, I crave it so...
The one that fills both belly and soul...
Among scholars, my palate it graced...
Of mild and mellow flavors it did taste...
Yes, with letter "r" its name starts out...
With "e" it ends, I have no doubt...
My heart and belly... How they quake...\nAs passion from deep within...awakes!
Please... Help...\nI cannot live without it...
A dancer who is searching for her song: someone who can dance lightly as a kingfisher, gaily as a baleful bloom, and fiercely as a fungold.
A traveling merchant who came to Cragspear. Ever since he started selling the Prince of Thieves necklace, he has been doing very well for himself.
Hey now, I need more than that!
All right, you got yourself a deal!\nHere, it's yours.
Have I got a story for you.
Maybe when I have some time.

The heir apparent to House Villeneuve of Shepherds Rock. His biggest grievance is the constant need to clean up after his father, the current lord, who seems to attract all kinds of trouble with his kind and generous nature. He has his doubts about the so-called Unbending Blade hired by his father.
Perhaps you have some wisdom\nyou may be willing to share?

[Cause of death: Burning]\nA man with blistered, melted flesh that drips with blood. A ne'er-do-well since youth, he ended his life by murdering his wife and child, setting fire to their home, and letting the flames consume him too. The desperate unwillingness to let go in his hollow eyes mesmerizes those around him.
Stay the hell away from me!!!
No one... No one leaves this place...
[Cause of death: Cut down]\nA Valore syndicate member who started under Lord Taviani, then went to work for Herminia. Unfortunately, he angered her with a minor offense and was subsequently killed.
I ain't lettin' ya through...\nThe forest is off-limits...
In the forest...what ya'll find is...

A merchant who lends money at a high interest rate, using possessions as collateral. According to rumors, he fails to trust the promises of people without money, and his products are obtained through fraudulent means. However, no one dares complain to his face because of his intimidating bodyguard.
You're a persistent one...\nHey, take them out, will ya!?
Bah! Get out of my sight!

A merchant who lends money at a high interest rate, using possessions as collateral. His aggressive methods and black-market products have earned him quite a bit of resentment, so he has hired even more bodyguards to ensure his safety. Just recently, he got his hands on an antique jewelry box.
Can't you see all the bodyguards\nI have now? This won't go the same\nway it did before.
Bah! Get out of my sight!

A young man from Wishvale. To help with the town's restoration, he spends his days gathering building materials and assisting Margot in the fields. Though a little rough around the edges, people have come to rely on his candid and trustworthy nature.
Hehe, did you really think you had\na chance?
You're a lot stronger than I remember...\nHere, take it.

A young man from Wishvale. He appears meek and unreliable, but is strong when push comes to shove. He saved Lila as they fled, but fell ill from the stress and decided to stay in Clearbrook while he recovered.

A young man who lives in Wishvale. He may seem timid, but is a fighter when push comes to shove. Ever since he rescued Lila, he has treated her like family. Though he may lack physical strength, even Granny Margot is impressed by his meticulous attention to detail.
Do you need to talk to me about\nsomething?
The daughter of Illian and Olive. She appears cheeky and acts tough, but has a kind heart. After getting separated from her parents, she was rescued by Jasper and has lived with him since.

The daughter of Illian and Olive. She appears cheeky and acts tough, but has a kind heart. People are surprised by her maturity as she often repeats phrases she's heard spoken by the adults around her.
Umm... I don't know how to explain it.
A merchant who peddles a variety of goods. He currently stocks herbs.
I refuse to sell anything to anyone who\ndoesn't recognize the value of my wares.
You don't look like a customer.
A traveler who wanders the continent as he pleases. He purchases rare items from various corners of the world not to sell, but to add to his collection.
I don't mind parting with it, but\nI can hardly give it away.
I don't have any interesting stories\nto tell.
So you want to hear what I have\nto say?

A man who worked as a farmer in Wishvale with his wife, Olive. He's a religious man who never missed a day of church, but now spends his time searching for his daughter, Lila. He prays every day and night for her safety.
A farmer who lives in Wishvale. His concern for the well-being of his daughter, Lila, has strengthened his faith. Now his days are filled with prayer as he works hard to support his family.
Hey, I've been waiting for you.
A woman who worked as a farmer in Wishvale with her husband, Illian. She deeply regrets getting separated from her daughter, Lila.
A farmer who lives in Wishvale. Though a fun-loving woman at heart, she and her husband, Illian, have become regular churchgoers out of concern for the well-being of their daughter, Lila.
I'm sorry. I'm not feeling too well...
A woman who once ran an inn in the Woodlands. She and her husband now live in his hometown of Altinia. Every so often, she gets a craving for her hometown's local cuisine.
A woman from Wishvale. Wanting to forget the attack on her hometown, she moved to Shepherds Rock with Karan. But after reuniting with <chara_name>, she has decided to return to Wishvale.
A woman who was born and raised in Wishvale. She recently returned home with her husband, Karan, and is now contemplating what she can do to help the others restore their town to what it once was.
I can't give this up. I'm sorry.
Here. Please take good care of it.
I'm sorry, but I'm a little busy right now.
Is there anything I can do to help?
Thank you. It's finally time to go home...
A man originally from Shepherds Rock. He moved to Wishvale after marrying Carabell and considers the town his second home. Despite his gentle disposition, he is a skilled hunter.
A man originally from Shepherds Rock. He moved to Wishvale, the hometown of his wife, Carabell, and joined the watch to help protect the town. Despite his gentle disposition, he is a skilled hunter.
I'll show you what I can do!
Phew... I feel like I got lucky there.
You're a lot stronger than I remember.\nYou win, fair and square.
Sorry, but I can't. I'm just about to start\nmy rounds.
That's right... Wishvale is my home.

An adventurer who sailed the seas aboard the Queen Orsa, a magnificent ship that was crafted by a master shipwright from Rippletide. The love of his life still has not returned, and so he remains stranded in a sea of despair.
After losing her true love at sea, she moved to a town far away from the coast. To this day, she still pines for her adventurous beau.
The sea is as bitter as my tears.
It's nothing you need to know.
Marjim is alive?\nPlease, bring me to him!

An apprentice to the best shipwright in all of Orsterra. His skills are on par with his mentor, who designed the beautifully crafted Queen Orsa. He hails from Rippletide, but currently resides in Emberglow to help with the construction of the Cathedral of Tytos.
Of course I'll lend you a hand!

A young man who traveled to Valore from Emberglow in the hopes of doing business. However, the wars between the syndicates make staying alive about all he can do. He worries about his father and sister back home to whom he has yet to send money.

A young man who returned to his hometown of Emberglow after failing to get his business off the ground in Valore. Since learning his father has fallen on hard times due to severe injuries to his legs, he now believes it is on him to take care of his family.
I could use someone to talk to.
Nothing ever works out for me,\nno matter where I go.
Maybe this town isn't for me...
A young man born in Donescu. He and Ensio used to play together as kids, and he's well aware of the reason his old friend became an apothecary.
Can I tell you my story?
If you think I can be of help...

An entrepreneur trying to get into the hot springs business. He once read a book on their health benefits and decided to come to Berecain in order to build a hot springs resort that would heal those injured in the war.
A sailor who hauls the island's ore to Grandport. He spends most of the month here, whiling away his time in tedium. Thinking about the adorable cat he recently saved eases his loneliness a little.
Sorry, but I have work to do.

A carved statue of one of the sacred beasts that serve Bifelgan, the Trader, god of merchants. Words engraved upon it tell of Bifelgan taking twin camels with him on his travels to protect both himself and his wares. In the pedestal is a hole the size of a coin.
You grabbed something that was\nprotruding from the sacred beast\nstatue's mouth.

A carved statue of one of the sacred beasts that serve Bifelgan, the Trader, god of merchants. Legend tells of a pair of sacred beasts, one who would guard Bifelgan's wares and one who would bite greedy travelers. But if travelers can show that they are without avarice, the beasts may lead them to riches.
An apprentice cleric of the Order of the Sacred Flame. Though once blind, she gradually regained her sight after the scarlet snow fell.
I hope I can be of help...

A cleric of the Order of the Sacred Flame. She cares for Rinyuu, a woman from a distant land, and tends to her every need. She's an avid reader and could be considered an expert on the Order's history.
Can I help you with something?
Sorry, but I am quite busy.

A cleric who roams the land and reports his findings on each region to the pontiff. He is both intelligent and well-versed in creeds other than the Order of the Sacred Flame. He recently visited Wispermill in the Flatlands and now harbors an interest in their heretical beliefs.

An archbishop with jurisdiction over the Frostlands. He was entrusted with the great responsibility to perform the Kindling ceremony in order to spread the Sacred Flame's power over twenty years ago. While the task was an arduous one, the trust the people placed in him as a result was well worth the effort.
I pray I can be of service to you.

A young man clad in vestments only bestowed upon those recognized for their unrivaled skill amongst the Knights Ardante. After extensive searching at the pontiff's decree, he's finally found the Flameguard, and it seems there was more reason to find him than to simply fulfill his duty.
I don't really know what to say...

A cleric who roams the land and reports his findings on each region to the pontiff. He is both intelligent and well-versed in creeds other than the Order of the Sacred Flame.

[Information on Miza's Disappearance]
People seen heading to the confessional are going missing.
I'd rather not speak of such things.

A member of the Scarlet Wings participating in the reconstruction of the Knights Ardante garrison. He continues to stand guard at the cathedral due to the respect he yet holds for Tytos, but he's deeply troubled over how to live his life after the tragic incident that took place here.

A member of the Scarlet Wings reconstructing the Knights Ardante garrison. He found himself at a loss after uncovering a written accusation from Velnorte to the Order buried beneath the charred rubble of the old garrison.

[Information on Miza's Disappearance]
Tarset was connected to Tytos.
Traveler and friend of the Knights Ardante,\nI place my trust in you...
...I suppose I can talk to you.

A bishop dispatched from the Order of the Sacred Flame to oversee Emberglow in the wake of Tytos's death. He originally wished to climb the ranks in Flamesgrace, where the primary chapter of the Order is located, but it seems the incident with Tytos has sorely disrupted his plans.
I would be happy to help, if I can.

A hunter who lives a solitary life with his dog some distance from town. His grandmother, who was an apothecary, taught him everything he knows, including the axiom, "A blessing shared is a blessing doubled," which convicts him to share his game with the townspeople when harvests are poor. Even so, he can be a bit hotheaded at times.
No clue what you're talkin' about.

The Order's hierarchy of power is this: pontiff, archbishops, bishops, priests, and then deacons. I tolerated much to finally attain the status of bishop. I will not lose my position over something like this!
You have a question for me?
I have nothing to say to you!
The Flameguard, charged with protecting the divine rings. For generations, the position of Flameguard has been entrusted to the strongest Knight Ardante with the purest of heart.
What do you wish to speak about?

A bishop dispatched from the Order of the Sacred Flame to oversee Emberglow in the wake of Tytos's death.

[Information on Miza's Disappearance]
He was known to have been colluding with Tytos.
That's asking a little much, I'm afraid.
I've never met a more suspicious\nperson in my life! Scarlet Wings!\nRemove them at once!
A priest who spends his days pondering what he can do to help Emberglow return to its former glory. Every year, he sends his beloved daughter flowers from each region for her birthday.
You are welcome to ask me anything.
The daughter of Father Monaris. It seems she idolized her father so much so that she followed him all the way to Flamesgrace.
A nun at the Flamesgrace Cathedral who has seen many seasons in her years of service. She wishes to spread the Order's creed, and so decided to take up post here.

A nun at the Flamesgrace Cathedral who has seen many seasons in her years of service. She wishes to spread the Order's creed, and so decided to take up post here.

[Information on Miza's Disappearance]
She was seen heading to the confession booth last night.
I'm afraid I cannot give this to you.
You think this would be of use\nto you?
I'm in the middle of my duties...
I'm afraid I have my work with\nthe Order to consider...
I shall be glad to accompany you!
What is it I can help you with?

The third daughter of the famed House Bertolini. After their parents' death, she and her sisters inherited the family's vast fortune. She's known for her philanthropy, and has been trying to use her newfound wealth to enrich the lives of everyone on the continent.

The commander of the Scarlet Wings, a subset of the Knights Ardante. He's hailed as the greatest swordsman on the continent, but humbly denies these claims and shows no interest whatsoever in wielding authoritative power. He currently devotes all his efforts to rehabilitating former criminals.

A playwright for a small theatre troupe. He lives a modest life with his wife—a former actress—and his son. Uninterested in achieving great fame, he's content to put on smaller productions, which are highly regarded by his audience.

Second-in-command at the Bargello Corporation. After coming to this town in his youth, he took a liking to it and became fast friends with Bargello and his pals. He frequently corresponds with his father, Julian, back home.
An archbishop who serves Julius, the current pontiff. Using the knowledge her scholarly grandfather imparted to her, she guides the people and spreads the teachings of the Flame across the continent.
Head of the Bargello Corporation. While running a white grape farm with his friends, he began a trading company to secure the necessary distribution channels.
A princess of Edoras. Though she sometimes hides behind her older sister, her kind and warm ways have earned her the people's favor.
In truth, he is Prince Richard of the Kingdom of Riven. He travels Orsterra on his father's orders, so that he may broaden his horizons.
In truth, he is King Solon of Altinia. In this peaceful realm, he leaves his nation in the hands of his attendant, Mendoza, and travels the continent as he pleases in a merchant's disguise.
The ruler of Grandport. She efficiently completes the necessary paperwork so that the port merchants can peddle their wares without trouble. It seems like she no longer takes to the seas these days.

A young Knight Ardante from the famed House Ravus. He grew up listening to former Knight Ardante Fried's tales of bravery, and so threw away his noble status and joined the knights himself once he was of age.
The empress of G'roha, a kingdom far to the west. She has left all administrative duties in the hands of her subordinates, and is currently touring Orsterra with her black panther, Maa'fa.
The daughter of the carpenter Lavore. She's currently helping her father with his work while studying to become a full-fledged architect herself.
Wishvale's tailor. He was originally training to be a warrior, but ended up abandoning the sword after losing to Fried, and decided to pursue his hobby of sewing instead.
A warrior who hails from the distant east. She hopes to take what she has learned during her time in the peaceful lands of Orsterra and apply it to bettering her home country.
A skilled merchant who hails from the Sunlands. He was invited to Wishvale where he fulfilled his dream of setting up his own shop.
A former merchant who was born in a small village in the Riverlands. She travels the continent, delivering items along the way.
A bodyguard who worked at Valore's tavern. Things are so peaceful that he does nothing but sharpen his blade all day.
A woman who likes to collect rings. The one she received from her parents as a child is her favorite.
A young apothecary-in-training who is trying to follow in his father's footsteps. Upon visiting Wishvale, he became friends with the children in town and has since prioritized play over work.
A capable apothecary. She settled in Wishvale after deeming it a suitable environment for her younger brother who has trouble walking.
A member of Wishvale's town watch. There isn't much for him to do in the peaceful town, so he began helping out in the fields. At this point, he's well on his way to becoming a farmer.

A member of the Aqeeq clan, one of the Sunlands' twelve clans. Upon visiting Wishvale, she fell in love with the town's comfort and has since cast aside her clan's ways in an effort to acclimate to her new environment.
A bishop of the Order of the Sacred Flame. He came to this town as Archbishop Ceraphina's attendant. He is a distant relative of <chara_name>'s mother, Yusia.
A deacon who serves Wishvale's church. She's helping her grandmother, Priestess Yaya, prepare for the Day of Reverence.

A scholar from the Royal Academy of Atlasdam. He followed the rumors of the azure flamestone to Wishvale, but over time became enamored by the town and has since quit his research and now spends his days without a care in the world.
A researcher of ancient cultures. She came to Wishvale to study legends regarding the Flamebringer.

A former playwright who held large-scale performances all over the continent. When he met Schwartz and his small troupe, he instantly resonated with the man's humble ways. He has since quit writing plays and now works as Schwartz's assistant.
A songstress who has taken the Flatlands by storm. She took a liking to Wishvale and decided to retire there.
A cait sage who manages the museum. He likes to write letters to all the lands' travelers, extolling the virtues of Wishvale.
A knight who hails from the fallen Kingdom of Hornburg. Encouraged by the hero Tytos to find meaning outside of battle, he has begun searching for a means to happiness in this peaceful town.

The only daughter of the Colzione Family Provisioners in Rippletide. When she discovered this town has everything one needs to live comfortably, she realized there is more to life than being a merchant.

A thief who is seemingly able to appear at will wherever he pleases, striking terror into the highborn and wealthy. He came to Wishvale because he heard it was a prosperous town, but abandoned any thoughts of mischief upon seeing how gentle the people were.
An apothecary who hails from Clearbrook. He initially set out wanting to save people across the continent, but since coming to Wishvale, his aim has slowly begun to change.

A hunter who shares a deep bond with her snow leopard, Linde. She once hunted ferocious monsters to protect the people from peril. However, in this peaceful town absent from such dangers, she has started to believe that a relaxed and carefree life may be the way.
A cleric who hails from Flamesgrace. Charmed by the town of Wishvale, she is contemplating settling here for good.

A professor at the Royal Academy of Atlasdam who came to Wishvale to broaden his knowledge. Despite the town's deep history and culture, he found that the people look to the future without dwelling on the past, a notion that has encouraged him to try to accept things as they are.
The most popular dancer in Sunshade. She is currently on vacation and has come to Wishvale after hearing how peaceful it is.
A cat who stands guard at the gates of the Isle of Sages. They revere the Great Sage and perform their duties with pride.
I have nothing to say to mew.
G'roha Soldier with No Fight Left
A G'roha soldier who's been hiding out on a tiny island. After seeing Tatloch drain his comrades' spirit without the slightest hesitation, he deserted the battlefield in terror.
I have nothing to give you.
I hope you can make use of it.
Please, I have to tell someone.
I don't know if I have the courage\nto speak of it.
A cat minstrel who drifts from place to place singing of all the beauty he sees. He adores the cait life, answering to no one and free to spend his time just as he pleases.
Nyahaha!\nWhat am I going to do with mew?

A mischievous kitten who was sorely scolded after playing with the Great Sage's tail. Their curiosity has since been directed at what might happen if they were to play pranks on a human, and it seems they finally have their chance to find out.
I've been waiting for a human\nto come!
I'm not gonna tell mew my weakness!
What is the world coming to when\npeople are turning to me fur help...?
A cait who professes to be the paw-tender's apprentice and hangs around in order to learn the secrets of making catsip. Their dream is to one day brew a cup that's fit for the Great Sage.

A cait who serves the Great Sage and takes great pains to protect the island from humans. They are a nihilist who has decided that human desires and the wars they cause are of no value whatsoever. Drinking catsip is the highlight of their life.
I don't have anything to give\nto a human like mew.
Here, take this. I don't need it\nanymore.
I guess I can at least listen to mew.
Mrrrow... There's an annoying bug\nbuzzing in my ears.
I admit, I am a little curious about\nyour town.
Nyao! Why should I have to go to\na human town!?

A kitten who uses its charms to get others to do its bidding. Humans instantly fall under its spell with its adorable gaze and innocent head tilt. The furry creature also makes sure to take meticulous care of its throat, which has the ability to produce the sweetest sounds known to man. Unfortunately, monsters are immune to its charm.
Fine. You can pet me if you'd like.
A gull that strayed from its flock in pursuit of faster flight. Its reason for doing so: simply because it could.
You look like you've got questions.
I've got nothin' to say to\na land dweller.
Sorry, forests aren't for me.\nI'm a sea bird.
A storm hit while it was being transported across the sea, causing it to fall overboard. Though its strong will keeps it alive on the island where it washed ashore, life can still get awful lonely.
You want to baanter with me?
Baah... I'd like it better if there\nwere other goats.
Don't you feel baad for taking\nadvantage of a poor little goat?
A cephalopod who goes wherever its many legs take it. It sometimes speaks to humans on a whim.
This is gonna hurt—eight times\nmore than usual!
So you managed to take it\nfrom me.
I wanna continue my own journey.
I got word out that you're all right.\nNow don't go making a fool of yourself.
A fierce-looking man with a rugged physique. People tend to mistake him for a syndicate member due to his looks, but in actuality he is an earnest, forest-loving lumberjack.
Afraid I can't lend an ear\nto that request.
Afraid I can't lend an ear\nto that request.
You got a question for me?
Give it up. You're better off\nnot getting involved.
It's a deal.\nCall on me anytime.
Mayor of the mafia-controlled town of Valore. While his position is little more than a title now, there remain many who love this kindhearted man.
I usually have a moment to spare\nfor travelers...but not today.
I usually have a moment to spare\nfor travelers...but not today.
I'd be glad to help you in any way\nI can.
There's nothing to gain from\nlistening to me.
I just can't say no to a traveler\nin need.
I just can't say no to a traveler\nin need.
An elderly woman who has lived in this area for a long time. She has known Taviani since he was a child and thinks fondly upon the days when he managed the town. Her favorite food is raisins.
You want to drag an old woman\nlike me around? What a hoot!

A young man who looks like a hunter, but is actually a farmer. His father was strict in teaching him the ways of hunting, but this led to him taking up agriculture in opposition to inheriting his parents' profession.
Sorry, I can't let you have it.
You want to know more about me?
I don't have anything to talk about.
It's all yours if you want it.
You want me to go to your town?\nSure.
I'd rather not leave town. Vicious\nmonsters are lying in wait out there.

A young man who issues warnings to travelers. He keeps this up day and night in the hopes of making Valore a better place. Alas, his efforts have amounted to little, as the travelers he speaks to continue to disappear one after the other.
Oho, you're gonna raise a fist\nagainst me? Everyone, over here!
Folks'd do well to keep both eyes\nopen, especially in this town.
Folks'd do well to keep both eyes\nopen, especially in this town.
No one really listens to me, so...
I give in. Guess you really can't\njudge a book by its cover, huh?
I give in. Guess you really can't\njudge a book by its cover, huh?

One of Lord Taviani's subordinates. Though few of words, he is a consummate professional. He was once a cheerful, talkative man, but ever since Taviani told him, "A man whose tongue loosens with liquor is no man at all," he has refused every drop of alcohol and never speaks a word in excess.
...I'll talk about my past. A little.
...I don't have anything to say to you.

One of Lord Taviani's subordinates. He often lightened the pockets of passersby on the street in his youth, but turned over a new leaf when Taviani took him in. Apparently, the wallet he stole from him is still quite dear to him today.
You'll need to do better than that\nif you wanna work for Taviani!
Sure, I've got time on my hands.\nWhat do you want to know?
I don't really feel like telling you.
Okay, that was a good deal.\nFor you, anyway.
Hmm... It might be time to put some\ndistance between me and Valore.
I can't just up and leave this place.

The woman with Taviani. She lost her parents at a young age and was taken in by Taviani after he found her sitting on the outskirts of Valore. She's been with him for so long that she's started to see him as her real father.
Will you listen to what I have to say?
It could be interesting workin' with\nsomeone besides Taviani for a change.
Thanks for the business.\nYou got a good deal, y'know.

A trader from Cragspear, royal capital of the Cliftlands. He heard that many moneyed aristocrats gathered in Valore and so came hoping to make a fortune. Alas, much of the money to be made finds its way to the mafia governance, and thus he spends his days escaping to the bottom of a bottle instead.
Everybody wants to buy low and sell\nhigh. But no such luck this time, pal.
Everybody wants to buy low and sell\nhigh. But no such luck this time, pal.
Couldn't hurt to take a look!
You're not a customer, are you?
I don't fight much, but I've got stamina\nif nothing else!

A mother worried about the environment in which she's raising her children. She tries to keep them indoors to safeguard them from the mafia's bad influence. She had her own wild days in her youth, making her that much more overprotective.
I'm sorry, I'm afraid that's just\nnot enough...
I'm sorry, I'm afraid that's just\nnot enough...
You seem like a nice person.
I don't feel like talking to\na complete stranger.
I can treat my baby to something\nspecial with this money.
I can treat my baby to something\nspecial with this money.
A young boy living a quiet life with his family. He secretly admires the mafia and slips out from under his mother's watchful eye every so often to hang out at their hideout.
Shut up!\nI'm not giving you anything!
Mama would get mad at me.

A girl who sells flowers to provide for her two younger brothers. She used to sell powder, but has since washed her hands of that business. Her income isn't quite what it used to be, but now she lives respectably, and is cheerful and focused on the future.
I'm sorry, this flower's for someone else.

A black market dealer who got his hands on some powder and dissolved a bit of it in honey. He sells the new product, known simply as "honeypowder," unaware of the fact that the powder he obtained was fake.
You just don't get it, do you?\nPay up or get lost.
You just don't get it, do you?\nPay up or get lost.
Hehe. Let me tell you\na little secret...
Bah! I'm not telling you anything.
A man who started a powder business by imitating what he saw. He lacked understanding of the trade and crushed up nuts, then sold them in powder form to the delight of palates all over.
You wanna take a nap, too?
Not a big deal, are ya?
I'll tell you what you wanna know.
Sure, I'll follow you.\nSee ya later, old man!
You want me to abandon this old man?\nThat's just plain cruel!

Owner of a tavern that's just barely staying afloat. All the changes around town have slowed customer traffic to a trickle, but his regulars remain ever faithful. Even now, his ears catch things that most others are not privy to.
Y'know, I used to handle folks who\ngot rowdy in the bar myself!
Some things you're better off\nnot knowing.
You know what you're doing,\nI'll grant you that.
You know what you're doing,\nI'll grant you that.

A regular at the tavern with an unrivaled love for theatre and drink. He harbors suspicions about the manor in the north of Valore and will occasionally try to approach it, but always gets chased away by monsters and ends up running home.
That ain't even enough for a beer...
That ain't even enough for a beer...
This'll be enough for me to\nwet my whistle!
This'll be enough for me to\nwet my whistle!
A dancer living strong in the mafia-controlled town. She once worked at the tavern in Sunshade, but ran away because of the manager's foul treatment of the dancers.
Hehe... Is this your first time?

A sad-eyed man standing on the outskirts of town. He warns travelers who approach Herminia's estate. In truth, he is the survivor of a tribe annihilated by the Covetous Witch, and now an unquenchable fire of hatred burns in his heart.
Don't judge a book by its cover.
Hmph. It'll take more than that\nto best a member of my tribe.
Hmph. It'll take more than that\nto best a member of my tribe.

A man who once worked as an actor onstage in Theatropolis. His natural talents helped him become something of a lead, but he was reviled by the playwright. In his frustration, he turned to powder, which cost him his reputation. It seems he's also accidentally misplaced his old stage costume.
You must project when you speak!\nYou must shake the very heart of\nyour audience!
You must project when you speak!\nYou must shake the very heart of\nyour audience!
Sorry, but I'd rather not dredge up\nthat memory.
Oho! Perhaps this is where\nmy destiny truly begins!
Why yes! Take it, by all means!

A young woman with hollow eyes. Though dressed in rags, she is quite beautiful. After having her heart broken, she turned to powder, but no longer indulges in it. She merely pretends to so she can be with the man next to her.

A veteran hunter well aware of his excellent bow skills. He once lived in the village of S'warkii, but has since moved on in search of new monsters to hunt. He's also a weapons collector, and is on good terms with the smithy.
Now to see if mine arrows fly true!
Hmm... You're not half bad,\nbut I must refuse.
Hmm... You're not half bad,\nbut I must refuse.
There's no point in telling you.
Th-that was impressive.\nMy bow is yours.
Y-your skill is true indeed.\nTake what you wish.
An extremely anxious priest. After his home was broken into, he feels compelled to check that the doors are locked four times before going out.
I'm sorry, but I'm going to pretend\nyou never asked...
I'm sorry, but I'm going to pretend\nyou never asked...
I'd prefer not to talk about it.
I...do wonder if I'm the right person\nfor this...
If anyone asks, please don't say\nyou got this from me, all right?

An elderly woman devout in her faith. She was born in the Coastlands, but married into this land. While on a journey, she was struck by the majesty of the Flamesgrace Cathedral and has not missed a day of prayer since.
Are you trying to test my faith?
You'll need to pray harder, dearie!
You'll need to pray harder, dearie!
I'm in the middle of my prayers...

An architect who always constructs buildings akin to jewelry boxes. It is reputed that those who live in his homes boast happy lives. He is quite particular about cutting down even the biggest of trees on his own and in doing so has built up exceptional strength.
I dislike conflict, but I'll not hold back\nif I'm pressed into it!
I should thank you for\nthe good exercise.
I should thank you for\nthe good exercise.
I shall tell you about what\nI refuse to compromise on.
I'll aid you if you'll have me!
I am utterly bested.\nHere, for you!

A carpenter who handles everything himself, from procuring the lumber to manufacturing. He spends most of his days in the forest nearby S'warkii, so he isn't sure how to talk to people anymore, even though he secretly wants to be friends.
An intelligent syndicate member who doesn't belong to any one syndicate, but rather acts as a consigliere, helping families that find themselves in dire financial straits.
You need to work on your\nnegotiating skills.
Is there something you wish to discuss?
Fine. I'll agree to your terms.
I have my hands full right now.\nMaybe some other time.

A renowned appraiser who lives in the Woodlands. On top of being an excellent judge of a product's value, he also sees right through people. He can be agonizingly blunt, however, incurring the displeasure of many of his customers.
Apologies, but I don't give handouts\nto third-rate customers.
Would you like my expert opinion?
Take it. A first-rate customer deserves\na first-rate product.
Your town certainly seems worth\nmoving to.
No. You have no merits to speak of.
A goat that is left to roam freely out in the pasture to graze on the weeds. This gluttonous animal will eat just about anything, including paper.
Don't interrupt me when I'm eating.

This young man lives a carefree lifestyle and is devoted to the pursuit of pleasure above all else—or at least that is how he would present himself to others. In truth, he is one of the arena promoters. Walking about in disguise allows him to surreptitiously scout for talented gladiators.
Are you interested in my story?
There's nothing for us to discuss.
Sorry, but you don't deserve this.
I have a feeling intriguing things\nwill happen if I give this to you.
Sorry, but I have to work.
I look forward to seeing you fight\nup close.

A fresh-faced merchant just setting out on a journey to the heart of capitalism. Drifting aimlessly from job to job, he's worked the bare minimum needed to pursue a life of pleasure. A discovery of a hoard of antiques in his late grandfather's storehouse gave him the bright idea to support himself by selling the stuff. He has no clue to the market value of anything, so his prices are pulled out of thin air.
Interested in my story, are ya?
Don't know if it's worth tellin'...
Tryin' to fleece me, huh!?\nGet outta here!
Heh, thanks for the business!\nStop by again anytime.
Um, excuse me?\nWhy would I bother doing that?
This antique business isn't\ngoing so well...so why not?

An arena promoter responsible for discovering many former champions. Among them is the current star gladiator, Garry. Fighters he signs on are always given a warm welcome, and anyone aiming for the top needs to earn his favor first.
Heh heh. Want to hear my story?
The strong don't divulge their secrets\nso easily.
I give you this to aid you in your battles.
Don't misunderstand me.\nI'm not a fighter.
Hmm... Very well. I've interest in\nseeing the way you fight.

A man wailing over a lost wager he made at the arena. He also lost a bet with his girlfriend, and had to swear never to gamble again. The truth is, he'd planned on proposing to her the next time he won, but now, he's starting to second-guess himself.
I don't feel like talking right now...
I just lost big, so I won't settle for\nanything but a huge pile of leaves.
Sorry, this isn't a good time... *sigh*
This might be a good chance\nto rediscover myself.

A woman celebrating a bet she won against her boyfriend. Worried about his gambling habit, she convinced him to agree to give it up for good if he lost their wager. Her misgivings about him now put to rest, she only has to wait for him to pop the question. Ironically, the adrenaline rush that victory gave her has shown her just how addictive gambling can be...
You'll listen? Great!\nWell, what happened is...
Just because I'm a gambling goddess\ndoesn't mean you can drive my price\ndown!
I'm too busy basking in my victory\nright now! Eee!
Heh heh heh... Hoping my luck\nwill rub off on you, eh?

An epicure who travels Orsterra sampling the spirits she finds along the way. She records the taste and color of every draught she quaffs, awarding them scores and publishing well-received pamphlets on her findings, then uses the income she receives from sales to fund further adventures. Craftsmen bring their prized vintages to her, hoping her seal of approval will catapult them to fame.
You want to hear my story?
I believe that to be an insulting\nassessment.
A toast to your fortunes in battle...
One must journey to new lands\nto find new spirits.
I can't just abandon my work and\ngo gallivanting off.

Moniker: Bloat Belly Peter
Height / Weight: 3 cubits, 1 span / 13 stone
Weaknesses: Dagger / Bow / Fan / Fire

Tourney champ Archibold the Grinder's #1 disciple (self-declared).
Want to hear about my master!?
Er... I-I'll show you who's the real\nfighter!
Heh, a weakling like you doesn't\ndeserve this.
I-I have to admit you're pretty tough.
You're out of your mind if you think\nyou can beat me.
Journey of physical and spiritual\nperfection! Woo! Count me in!

Moniker: Twilight Panther
Height / Weight: 3 cubits, 1 span / 7 stone
Weaknesses: Polearm / Axe / Staff / Ice

Less of a creature of the night, more of an early bird.
You want to know more about me?\nHehe.
A lady never reveals her secrets.
You'll need to try harder if you want\na favor from me.
Whatever you want, sweet thing.\nI like strong folk like you.
No one likes a violent brute.\nRemember that.
Whatever you want, sweet thing.\nI like strong folk like you.

A middle-aged nobleman who traveled here to spectate the gladiatorial matches. Targeted time and time again by bandits along the way, he nevertheless arrived unscathed. He brags that this was because of his well-concealed skill with a sword, but it was actually all thanks to the overworked and underpaid mercenary he hired as a bodyguard.
You wish to know more about me,\nyes?
Hmph. I haven't anything to say\nto the likes of you.
Heh heh heh. The time has come\nto reveal my ultimate technique.
Fwa ha ha! Quail before my power!
H-how could my ultimate technique\nfail me!?
Fwa ha ha! Quail before my power!
I would gladly lend my sword arm\nto a champion such as you!

A young girl raised alongside a crop of older brothers. Perhaps due to their influence or that of the town, she's taken to playing tag and "queen of the arena." She spends most of her days practicing the sword in hopes of one day becoming a gladiator. Her parents would like to see her grow into a lady, but she seems to have other plans.
I know how to win, but I'm not telling!
Begging a kid for stuff? You're weird!

A mercenary wielding an elaborate longsword. Once, he fought alongside the brave knights and heroes of the fallen Kingdom of Hornburg. Unable to forget the glory of those noble days, he continues to hone his skills with the blade.
Sorry. I wish not to speak of it.
I have no reason to do that.
You want me to fight for you?\nI refuse.
So I am to wield my sword\nin battle again...

A housewife from Clearbrook who married into town. A bucolic upbringing bred a distaste for violence in her, and she has yet to visit the arena. With little to do in town besides spectate, she began penning a novel about gladiators to fill her bored void. Exceptional imagination has made her stories a smashing success, and she's sold a tremendous number of copies to date.
Are you interested in my tale?
I'd rather not share my story\nwith strangers...
I feel hesitant to share with a stranger...
I wonder what sort of drama\nwill come of this.
Oh, you want me to follow you!?\nI'd love to!
Stick your neck out, and someone'll\nchop it off. Be careful out there, okay?

A curmudgeonly gladiator who once set his sights on being a champion, but soon found himself accepting tidy sums to throw fights. He's better than most with a blade and quite the actor—skills which he uses to fake a close match, only to lose by a hair when the time is right. A true virtuoso.
I really shouldn't be telling you this,\nbut...
Sorry, but I have nothing to tell.
No one's paying me to lose,\nso I might as well win.
Bring me leaves next time,\nand I'll lose for you.
I fought my best, and I lost,\nso fair's fair.
Bring me leaves next time,\nand I'll lose for you.
I fought my best, and I lost,\nso fair's fair.

Moniker: Ironarm Lexi
Height / Weight: 3 cubits, 1 span / 10 stone
Weaknesses: Sword / Staff / Tome / Dark

A giant of a woman who wields a massive axe. Her younger brother is also a talented gladiator.
Do you want to know how to\nget stronger?
Take it. To the victor goes the spoils.
If I follow you, I'm sure to grow\nstronger...

A retired swordsman once hailed as a legend, he lost his reason to fight when his friend and greatest rival passed away. With nothing left to keep him here, he ponders what it is he will live for now.
You want to hear my story...?
I apologize, but I do not feel like\ntalking right now.
I haven't fallen far enough to turn\nmy back on a challenge.
I hope you learn to value\nyour health more.
I entrust my hopes to you.
I hope you learn to value\nyour health more.
I stride once again into the mouth\nof hell.

A spearman of the Frostlands, in his youth he distinguished himself in battle after defeating a succession of opponents, but found himself egged on by those around him to seek even greater heights. To this day he continues to search for new challengers, but for no greater purpose other than to test his spear and his mettle.
Apologies, but I'm in the midst\nof training.
Very well! Let us fight with honor!
Hmph! I didn't even break a sweat.
Hmph! I didn't even break a sweat.
Very well. I am at your command.

A swordsman who strives to live up to the expectations of his hometown. Weak as a child, those around him claimed he had no potential as a gladiator. After tireless training, he managed to grow strong enough to enter the arena, and though some of his past frailty remains to his chagrin, he pushes ever forward.
I don't have time for this now.\nI've got a match comin' up.
What are you doin'!?\nP-please stop that this instant!
Wh-why is this happenin' to me...
I hope I can meet your expectations\nof me!

Moniker: The Enigma
Height / Weight: 4 cubits, 1 span / 8 stone
Weaknesses: Sword / Dagger / Bow / Lightning

Rumor has it Oswald here hails from a certain kingdom.
You think I'll lose to a young'un\nlike you?
That won't get my blood boiling.
This isn't much, but if you want it,\nit's yours.
Ho ho ho...\nYou want to hear my old yarns?
Ho ho ho...\nNothing to tell, I'm afraid.
Ho ho! Well, there's an idea!\nI'd be happy to join you.
You young'uns think you can\nget away with anything!

Moniker: Lightfoot Riley
Height / Weight: 3 cubits / 6 stone
Weaknesses: Bow / Tome / Fan / Light

She became a gladiator like her father, and now searches for clues as to where he could have gone.
There is something I must do...
I'm not in the mood to talk.
I can't lose to the likes of you.
A journey in good company...\nDoesn't sound half bad.

"Riley's father, why, he was a tough sort. Lots of folk bet on him, but then...poof! He just vanished. Some say angels carried him off to heaven; others that this horrible monster lurking under the arena gobbled him up! In my opinion..."
It's not a story worth getting into...

This info broker used to ply his trade in the dirty underbelly of society, until he discovered a deadly secret while investigating Leopaldi the Wealthy. Realizing his life was forfeit if he didn't call it quits, he quickly retired and washed his hands of it all. Now, he does his best not to draw attention to himself, and gets by selling info on the arena gladiators.
Curious to learn more, are you?
Sorry, you'll have to try harder\nto get <i>me</> talking.
I don't have time for fools\nwho lack heavy purses.
I don't have time for fools\nwho lack heavy purses.
If you insist, fine. I can help.

A woman who greatly admires Edoras, land of hope. Tales of the kingdom's large castle and urban town reached her ears through traveling merchants, and now she dreams of one day settling in that glorious realm herself.
Do you want to know what it's like\nliving an impoverished life here?
Why should I have to talk to you?
That price is a little too low...
Are you sure you want this?\nWell, okay.
I'm not interested in such\nbarbaric pursuits...
Will you take me out of this\nboring place?

A wealthy heiress from the Flatlands who fled her restrictive, albeit glamorous, home to elope with her beloved. Though she threw away a bright future to get here, she is really enjoying her newfound freedom. Honestly, she's somewhat forgotten the harsh circumstances she's in—and her beloved's feelings, to boot.
I'll gladly tell you about my life—\nand all its twists and turns.
Have you no shame? Now leave me be.
I cannot part with this for such\na paltry sum.
Yes, yes, see that you take good care\nof it.
