Is that all? I cannot help you for\na pittance.
Oh, I bet something truly exciting\nis waiting for us!

Once a gardener for a noble family in the Flatlands, this ambitious fellow was hoping to win the hand of the young lady of the house. That way, he reasoned, he'd inherit both title and fortune. All was going according to plan until he angered his lord, forcing him to elope with his love and flee one step ahead of the headsman's axe.
That small of a sum won't\nhelp me now...
Maybe this will help me treat\nmy wife to something nice...
That small of a sum won't\nhelp me now...
I guess anything's better than\ntwiddling my thumbs around here.

A new recruit into the rising power that is the mafia. He loved to pick fights, until he quarreled with an old man he didn't know was a retired gladiator. After getting the snot whooped out of him, he's lost his confidence and no longer judges books by their covers. He's also doing his best to be polite to everyone he meets, to uncertain effect.
Allow me to introduce myself to ya.
Beggin' your pardon, but I don't\ngot anythin' worth talkin' about.
Wouldja pretty please not try'n\ntake advantage of me?
Go on, good person folk,\ndo whatcha will with it.
Sorry to displease ya, but my boss\nDelvecchio wouldn't like that, no siree.
A teller of tales in the Woodlands, versed in the history of the beast tamers. To hear the stories, one need only ask.
I haven't time for trivial tales.
You interest me, so I'll accept\njust this once.
Impressive. Mayhap impressive enough\nto become a legend in your own right.
Time for me to spin a yarn anew.

Moniker: Champ
Height / Weight: 4 cubits / 13 stone
Weaknesses: Axe / Ice / Dark

Despite winning the arena championship, he still resides in the old quarter.
It's not that interesting a tale.
I don't like talking about myself.
As long as the fighting's good,\nI'll go anywhere.
You want me to help you? Sorry,\nyour fight's small potatoes for me.

A wrinkled elder who remembers the good ol' days. The old town's fortunes changed ever since the rise of the mafia, and now this aging gent mourns the ghost of a town that once was filled with laughter and song.
Will you listen to this old man's tale?
Don't you have any respect for\nyour elders?
Time to bring some life back into\nthis old town.
Perhaps I can leave the future to you.
I've spent my life here.\nI can't just leave.
I may be old, but I'm not daft.

The longest-serving member of the crime syndicate, he was close with the rough-and-tumble Delvecchio. "With you to lead us, Boss, I'm sure we'll rule Victors Hollow one day." Words the young orphan once uttered, and a dream now grown cold. Where did they go wrong? Where is the beloved boss who he was convinced would lead them to greatness?
I ain't got anything to say to you.
...Might be fun to follow you for a while.
...You make a good offer, but I can't\nleave the boss's side.
We've fallen on hard times, but\nwe're still the Delvecchio family.\nDon't insult us.

A former gladiator living quietly in a corner of town. Once a star who drew massive crowds, he suffered a terrible injury in the underground arena and retired to the old quarter. He often frets about the daughter he left behind in the town proper, and occasionally walks by the arena to check in on her.
Would you like to hear about\nthe triumphs of my past?
Y'know, putting my faith in another\nain't so bad.
I got no interest in a fight without\nspectators.
Heh, I've got nothing for the likes\nof you.

A dancer employed at the arena entertaining spectators between fights. Able to beguile any crowd, the energy of her dances seem to resonate with the excitement of her audience, growing ever more bold, ever more passionate.
You want to hear more about\nmy dances, yes!?
Sorry. I need to practice my steps.
No, no. You need to ask with\nmore...charm.
My passion will follow you into battle.
You don't have what it takes\nto get me riled up.

A veteran announcer who commentates on tourneys. Whether the day's fighters are skilled or scrawny, he can whip the audience into an excited frenzy with his deep baritone voice and stirring speeches. He hopes that one day, he'll witness the birth of a champion who will carve their name into the annals of history.
You want a briefing?\nIt'd be my pleasure!
Announcers aren't meant to talk\nabout themselves.
I can't be seen playing favorites.\nSorry.
You have a job for me?\nConsider my interest piqued.
I'm an announcer, not a fighter!

A man who used to sell powder in Valore. It became far more difficult to peddle his illicit goods after the death of the Covetous Witch, so he began moving from town to town searching for a new line of work. Eventually, he set his sights on the gambling circuits of Victors Hollow, and is scheming to curry favor in order to get in with the bookies.
Whoops. Can't go spilling any secrets\nnow, can I?
Huh... I never thought I'd be\nhelping you.
C'mon now, stop with the bad jokes.
I wouldn't make an offer that insulting\nif I were you.

A guard stationed at the arena gates. Big shots of the underworld pass by with frightful frequency, so he is careful to dust every nook and crevice of his domain. When he spots a speck of dirt, he rushes off to grab a broom posthaste. Those who aren't in the know think he's some sort of janitor.
Want to know about this place?
I've got nothing to say.\nGet out of my sight.
Customers who raise a fuss\nget the boot.
Leave, before I get your blood\non the floor.

A cook who has been serving the gladiators for over a decade, giving them the high calorie meals they need, though steadily increasing the flavor content to absurd levels. His voice and personality have also grown more bombastic as the tavern has become almost as chaotic as the battleground itself.
I'm busy right now! Save it for later!
You want to hire me!? Now that's\nwhat I like to hear!
Sorry, but there are folks here\nwho need me!

Moniker: Mister Tomahawk
Height / Weight: 4 cubits / 13 stone
Weaknesses: Polearm / Lightning / Ice

Though he acts like a gentleman, he becomes a savage in battle.
Taste the power of my axe!
It is very kind of you to want to\nknow more about me.
That isn't very gentlemanly of you.
You're even tougher than a tree.
Nothing can stand before my axe!
I'll gladly oblige as long as there\nare opponents I can duel or trees\nI can fell.
Apologies, but the arena is where\na gentleman truly belongs.

Moniker: None
Height / Weight: 3 cubits, 1.5 span / 12.5 stone
Weaknesses: Sword / Polearm / Axe / Dagger

He has never participated in a match, but operates behind the scenes.
I already know everything about you!
You won't get it out of me that easily.
I guess you're one cut above.
The outcome was set in stone\nbefore the match even started.
A gladiator's place is here,\nnowhere else.

A skilled goldsmith who creates exquisitely detailed accessories. She always dresses in lovely, graceful outfits, and is well-known for being pleasant to converse with. This has caused many of her colleagues to be jealous of her, but she shrugs it off and continues down her own path nonetheless.
Are you here to let the sparks fly!?
Oh! You'd like to hear more?
Don't stick your nose where\nit doesn't belong.
No, I think I'm fine where I am.
A bartender who knows Valore front to back. Information naturally finds its way to him. Though he has a gentle demeanor, laughter never lights his eyes.
You might not believe your ears,\nbut...
Did you honestly think I'd fall for that?
A fearless woman who single-handedly manages the long-standing Fenice Inn. Her establishment provides succor not only to customers, but also young folk who have failed in their "work" with the mafia.
I don't know if this is what\nyou want to hear, but...
Don't you dare try to peek behind\nthe desk!

A fierce-looking man with a rugged physique. People tend to mistake him for a syndicate member due to his looks, but in actuality he is an earnest, forest-loving lumberjack. After his tools were purchased by those from abroad, he quit his job and spends his days lazing about.
I'm not much for talking about myself,\nbut I suppose I can for you.
Don't you know better than to pry?
Mayor of the mafia-controlled town of Valore. While his position is little more than a title now, there remain many who love this kindhearted man.
Promise you won't tell other travelers?
If you think I always do as I'm told,\nyou're gravely mistaken!
An elderly woman who has lived in this area for a long time. She has known Taviani since he was a child and thinks fondly upon the days when he managed the town. Her favorite food is raisins.
Would you like to hear a story?
You're just trying to rob me,\naren't you?

A young man who looks like a hunter, but is actually a farmer. His father was strict in teaching him the ways of hunting, but this led to him taking up agriculture in opposition to inheriting his parents' profession.

A young man who issues warnings to travelers. He keeps this up day and night in the hopes of making Valore a better place. Alas, his efforts have amounted to little, as the travelers he speaks to continue to disappear one after the other.
I'd say "be careful," but...there's nothing\nto be careful of.
There's nothing to talk about, really.

The woman with Taviani. She lost her parents at a young age and was taken in by Taviani after he found her sitting on the outskirts of Valore. She's been with him for so long that she's started to see him as her real father.
I've had enough trouble for one life.

A trader from Cragspear, royal capital of the Cliftlands. He heard that many moneyed aristocrats gathered in Valore and so came hoping to make a fortune. Though he once struggled to pay his dues to the mafia, a large sum of leaves began circulating about the town, and his dreams unexpectedly came true.
I've got nothing to show you, but...

A mother worried about the environment in which she's raising her children. She tries to keep them indoors to safeguard them from the mafia's bad influence. Lately, she's taken to using the wealth she's earned from their foreign visitors to play "the Covetous Witch" with her son.
"Nothing in life is free—including\nmy knowledge!" Hehe, do I sound\nlike her?
"I'll not give you a single leaf of\nmy wealth." Hehe, how was that?
A young boy living a quiet life with his family. He secretly admires the mafia and slips out from under his mother's watchful eye every so often to hang out at their hideout.
Want to play a game with me?
Go away. I'm playing with my mom\nright now.

A black market dealer who got his hands on some powder and dissolved a bit of it in honey. He sells the new product, known simply as "honeypowder," unaware of the fact that the powder he obtained was fake.
Hehe. Things are really looking up!

A man who started a powder business by imitating what he saw. He lacked understanding of the trade and crushed up nuts, then sold them in powder form to the delight of palates all over. His product has been well received by those from abroad and he rejoices in selling it for quite the sum.
Right now, you can ask me\njust about anything!
Sorry, but there's nothing I can do.

A dancer living strong in the mafia-controlled town. She once worked at the tavern in Sunshade, but ran away because of the manager's foul treatment of the dancers. Though she cherished the training clothes given to her by a kindhearted colleague, she easily parted ways with them when presented with heaps of coin by those from abroad.
Do you need something from me?
I have nothing to give you.

A sad-eyed man standing on the outskirts of town. He warns travelers who approach Herminia's estate. In truth, he is the survivor of a tribe annihilated by the Covetous Witch, and now an unquenchable fire of hatred burns in his heart.

A man who once worked as an actor onstage in Theatropolis. His natural talents helped him become something of a lead, but he was reviled by the playwright. In his frustration, he turned to powder, which cost him his reputation.
Listen to the words in my heart!

A young woman with hollow eyes. Though dressed in rags, she is quite beautiful. After having her heart broken, she turned to powder, but no longer indulges in it. She merely pretends to so she can be with the man next to her.
I don't know anything worth\ntalking about.

A veteran hunter well aware of his excellent bow skills. He once lived in the village of S'warkii, but has since moved on in search of new monsters to hunt. Now that he's sold his hunting tools to those from abroad, he finds himself excited at the prospects of his new life.
I have my eyes set on the future.
Forget about who I was in the past.

An extremely anxious priest. After his home was broken into, he feels compelled to check that the doors are locked four times before going out. But he no longer fears thieves for even the lowliest of their ranks are now wealthy beyond compare.
It feels like a weight's been\nlifted off my shoulders.
Why are you so curious about\na total stranger?

An elderly woman devout in her faith. She was born in the Coastlands, but married into this land. While on a journey, she was struck by the majesty of the Flamesgrace Cathedral and has not missed a day of prayer since.
I've been blessed by the Sacred Flame!
I must finish my prayers first.
I've made sure everyone in town\nknows you're to be trusted.

[Crime: Theft]\nA Scarlet Wing who is completely devoted to Tytos. Growing up in an impoverished area, he had no choice but to steal to survive. Ever since Tytos captured him and brought him into the Scarlet Wings, he's kept watch at the town gate.
I'm not obligated to answer that.

A little girl standing out in the cold. Her father went into the mountains six months ago, but was never heard from again. She continues to await his return and holds fast to her last memory of him: a beautiful stone they found together.

[Crime: None]\nA Scarlet Wing who speaks of the terror of giant monsters. He used to make a living hunting monsters with his friend until his friend was killed by a bear. Having lost his purpose, he then joined the Scarlet Wings of his own accord. To this day, he refuses to part with his friend's weapon.
Shut up and leave me alone.
I'll be there when you need me.
I need to focus on my mission.
A mercenary who is distrustful of Tytos, the hero. He has made it through a myriad of battles relying only on his own skill.
You want to have a go at me?
Take good care of it, okay?
I may not look it, but I know my way\naround a fight.
...No. I don't think I can trust you\nto keep it a secret.
I was just thinkin' of leavin' town.
I can't imagine anywhere's\nmuch better than here.
A man who came from Theatropolis with his wife. Even while traveling, he refuses to part with his prized adventurer's journal. In truth, his wife dislikes journeys, but is content to see him happy.
I'm worth much more than that.
This is worth much more than that.
This is a lovely town, isn't it?
Yes, the journey continues!\nCall me anytime.
I'll add that to our travel funds.
A minstrel who sings Tytos's praises. Once a wandering bard of no significance, his <i>Song of the Hero</> lit Emberglow aflame, causing him to linger there.
Sorry, but my throat is a little sore.
My song will aid you in battle.
Please take good care of it.

A man who adores the Scarlet Wings. Drawn by Tytos's strength, he has even considered taking up the sword himself. However, being chased around by white bats quickly put an end to that. He hardly has any equipment to his name, anyway.
What, you want to take me on?\nDon't think the Scarlet Wings\nwill sit back and watch!
Come quick, Scarlet Wings!\nThis person is highly suspicious!

[Crime: Assault]\nA Scarlet Wing who is completely devoted to Tytos. He used to get into fights at the drop of a hat. Ever since he joined the Scarlet Wings, he has sworn his undying loyalty to Tytos.
Sure, I've been itching for a fight.
What would you like to know?
I don't take orders from anyone\nbut Lord Tytos!

A merchant who is a little too devout. He starts praying a week before conducting business, and continues praying from morning until night for safety, bringing to mind one fear after another: that he might get mugged by bandits, that he may be killed by monsters, and so on. Perhaps he is nothing more than a worrywart.
I may be pious, but I still need to\nmake a living.
Would you like to pray with me?
You have a deal!\nPraise be to the gods!
O gods, please protect me on this new\npath. May it bring me good fortune...
Your town?\nI don't know if that's a good idea.

An old man who watches over the girl who waits for her mother. The girl's father died when she was young, and her mother was forced to serve Tytos after he took a liking to her. Whenever the girl's mother speaks to her reassuringly, he always feels horrible for his inability to better the situation.
You want to brawl?\nAll right. I could use the exercise.
You shouldn't judge a book\nby its cover.
You shouldn't judge a book\nby its cover.
This is difficult for me to talk about,\nbut...
Sorry, but I can't bring myself to\ntalk about it.
I can heal your wounds, at least.
I don't need this anymore.\nTake it.
A girl innocently awaiting her mother's return. She adores her mother's stories of Tytos, the hero, as if they were fairy tales. Her old fur robe smells like her mother.
Sorry, but you can't have this.

A lady who was once a renowned noble in the Frostlands. After hearing of the House Bertolini tragedy, she renounced her name in fear and moved to Emberglow. She doesn't know how to use the weapon that is her family's heirloom, but she treasures it all the same.
In years past, nobles were taught\nswordplay as a matter of course.\nHere, I'll show you.
I worry for your future if that is\nall the strength you can muster.
I worry for your future if that is\nall the strength you can muster.
I'm afraid I'll not be very useful\nin battle.
Yes, you may be worthy of having this.
A woman who knows much about the town of Valore in the Woodlands. Her cousin from that area sends her lumber, which she uses to support herself.
That's a little too low for me.
That's a little too low for me.
I'm not one for idle chatter.
Woman Who Is Stern to Strangers
An old woman who is very harsh toward outsiders. She is greatly concerned over the disappearance of many residents, and is intentionally hard on travelers as a means to warn them.
I see words mean nothing to you.
Let me give you a word of warning.
A child who's having fun making snowmen. No matter how many paths he blocks or people he inconveniences, he will keep on making more and more snowmen. For that is his duty.
You think you can make fun of me\n'cause I'm a kid!?
A man suspicious of the underground research facility built by the Scarlet Wings. He spends his days wondering whether the cries he hears come from humans or monsters.
Have to keep moving to stay warm...
Ugh, that was hardly worth it.
Ugh, that was hardly worth it.

A man well-versed in both history and the Order of the Sacred Flame. He came from Flamesgrace, the home of the Order, eighteen years ago and is deeply disturbed at how the scarlet cathedral seems to oppose the one in Flamesgrace.
I have no such obligation to you.
I have no such obligation to you.
No, I shouldn't be spreading stories\nI'm uncertain about.
I have little strength to offer.
A mother who loves to hunt for things to gossip about. In truth, she does it to tell her ailing son of the outside world.
Oh, you want to try me?
I'll spin a good tale out of this one!
I'll spin a good tale out of this one!
Some things should be left unsaid.
You take care of that now.

A boy who rarely leaves his house due to illness. He took up needlework to help with the expenses. His mother is a bit of a gossip, but he chooses to think her loose tongue is for his sake, that he might know more of the outside world.
I-I can't give that to you...
Would you talk with me\nfor a little while?
Mom says I shouldn't talk\nto strangers.
Man Who Knows the Dangers of Snow

An old man who warns those who would venture into the mountains. He knows of many who have lost their lives to blizzards, avalanches, or even a single misstep, and is desperate to prevent further tragedies.
I'll not pay heed to that request.
I'll not pay heed to that request.
Turn back now, while you still can.
All I can do is pummel them\nwith my staff.

An aloof old man who lives at the edge of town. In truth, he was once a mighty hunter, known as "The Longbow Master," who took down monsters and fierce foes with his giant bow. He has long since given up battle, but still longs for the day when someone might challenge him again.
Is that a challenge? I gladly accept!
Ho ho... You have a long way to go.
Ho ho... You have a long way to go.
You wish to know more about me?
Splendid! That was a marvelous fight.\nAllow me to join you.
Splendid! Take this as thanks for\ngiving me some long-awaited\nexcitement.

An apothecary who travels the realm. He came to Emberglow after hearing about a rare medicinal herb from a botanist friend. Unfortunately, he forgot to ask about the plant's whereabouts and now does not know where to look.
I doubt it'd be of much use to you.
It's so cold...\nI'm not in the mood to talk.
Thanks, but...I think I'll stay here\na little longer.
A beautiful hunter with a cool smile. The cold aura she emanates makes her seem unapproachable, when in actuality she is very friendly and finds taking care of others to be worthwhile.
You will learn to fear my bow.
Keep training that arm of yours.
If you want it that badly,\nyou can have it.
If there are people who I can help,\nI'd be glad to join you.
I can't go with you. It doesn't look\nlike you need my help.

A pious woman who spreads the teachings of the Sacred Flame across the realm. There are many who admire her devotion to the faith, though there are many who also try to take advantage of her kind nature much to her annoyance.
There may be someone else who\nneeds this more than you.
Is there something you wish\nto speak to me about?
Please forgive me.\nI am very busy...
Go with the grace of the Flame...
I see... Then I will join you in\nyour town.
There is still work for me to do here.
A black cat that stands amidst the snowy landscape. Though it hates the chill of the snow, it does enjoy leaving footprints.
Don't think mew can just walk up\nand talk to me like we're fur-ends!
Mrooow? Mew pickin' a fight\nwith me?
What do mew mean there are\ntowns without snow!?
I don't take orders fur-om anyone!
Ha! All hiss and no bite.

A doting father whose daughter ran away to elope with her love. He was ready to give the marriage his blessing, but a few careless words escalated into a quarrel. His daughter left in a huff and he hasn't seen her since. He spends his days gazing out at the snowy plains, worrying about his runaway daughter.
Sorry, but I have my reasons\nfor not wanting to leave.
I've been saving this to give\nto my daughter.
There are some things a man\nwould rather not talk about.
I've got a reason to go on a journey\nof my own. Let me accompany you.
In that case...\nHere, you can hold onto it.

A woman whose daughter is the apple of her eye. She pampers the girl, striving to give her everything she wants before she even asks for it. Neighbors call her "overprotective," but in her mind, she simply wants to spare her daughter from all the suffering that she herself went through as a little girl.
I can't leave my daughter\nto fend for herself!
I won't give this to you for that.
Let me tell you all about\nmy beloved daughter...
I'm too busy caring for my daughter.
More work means more leaves,\nwhich means more love I can\nshower on my daughter!
Hehe. I can buy my daughter\na lovely new outfit with this!

A traveling merchant who peddles furs from the Woodlands. The high-quality pelts he procures from S'warkii are renowned for their softness and luster, and they sell exceedingly well. It's profitable work, but his true motivation for dealing in furs is the beautiful huntress who acts as his supplier.
I can't sell her pelts for such\na pitiable price!
Sure, I'll lend you my skills.

A recent arrival who moved here with her husband to start a new life. When he told her he wanted to quit his lucrative job in Emberglow and leave town, she told him he was a fool. Later, she learned about the scarlet snow tragedy and realized she owed him her life. After they moved here, she got a job in the tavern and now makes more than her husband.
That's not enough to be worth\nmy time.
I can't part with this for that price.
You never know what life has in store...
How dare you try to cozy up to me!

This greedy merchant rushed here when he heard rumors of fine artisanal goods on sale for cutthroat rates. In a heady daze, he spent his life savings buying up stock, only to realize he had no means nor money left to transport it. Now he shivers in the cold, hoping customers will come to him.
Sorry, but I'd rather work for myself\nthan for that paltry pay.
I'd love to move this merchandise,\nbut I am running a business here...
Browse as much as you like.
I don't have time for window shoppers.
You've got a good eye, my fine friend.
This melancholy old lady was the only daughter of a noble family, but has been reduced to spending her twilight years alone in a quiet corner of town.
I'm sorry, but I can't leave.
Did you think I would say yes?
You want to hear my tale?
You just want to have a laugh at me.
I hope my time can be of use to you.
I think you would get more use\nout of this than I would.
Once a scholar who traveled the continent researching monsters. The anecdotes of his astonishing encounters, though true, sound like mere fairy tales, so there are few who believe him.
Kids these days have no respect...
There's still something I can do.
No, not if you're asking in jest.
Ask me if you ever need help.
You've got potential, whippersnapper.\nHere, have it.
A proud soldier and keeper of the peace in Flamesgrace. Unfortunately, his job often makes him the target of resentment, so he drowns his sorrows in liquor under the guise of keeping warm.
Oh? Ya wanna fight me? *hic*\nDon't underestimate a drunk!
Ah!? What're you tryin' to pull!?
Not yours, no! Get outta here,\nyou rotten little monster!
Wanna have a drink with me?
I'd love to work for you! *hic*
Whee! *hic*\nHave as much as you like!

From a young age, she dreamed of joining the Knights Ardante. Achieving knighthood, however, left her without any other passions in life. Her expression is now as cold as ice, though a fire still burns in her eyes during the heat of battle.
Very well. I shall test your resolve.
You are not worthy enough to wield it.
As a Knight Ardante, I listen to\nthe voices of the people.
There is nothing for us to discuss.
If there is something I can do as\na Knight Ardante, so be it.
There are things I must do here.

A hunter operating out of Flamesgrace, he is painfully aware his proficiency with the bow is below average, and so spends his days stalking small fry. His secret dream is to one day hunt a quarry as big as a house.
Help you? Are you pulling my leg?
No way. Hunt your own prize.
I have a dream, you know.
All right! I'll help you take down\na big one!

Mom always makes my favorite foods and buys me lots of nice outfits, but she doesn't understand what I really want! She doesn't understand me! Not at all, nuh-uh! Can't she see that I don't need anything as long as I have her?
You know what?\nMy mom doesn't get me at all.
This is our problem, not yours.
Sure, just take good care of it.

A former minion in Tytos's employ, he fled here from Emberglow with his wife after learning a colleague got disposed of. When he heard about the scarlet snow tragedy, he discovered newfound faith in the Sacred Flame.
I'm not in the mood right now.
I can't give this to you. Sorry.
It really was for the best.
I'd rather not talk about it.
Let this aid you on your travels.\nGood luck out there.

This old matron absolutely despises the cold. Even the tiniest draft can make her teeth clack. She has considered moving to warmer climes, but her granddaughter—even more sensitive to the cold than her—refuses. Left with no other choice, she layers up to bear the frigid Frostlands air.
I can't leave my granddaughter here\nto fend for herself.
You're willing to keep me company?
It's too cold to stand around and talk.
Only for a short trip, mind. And keep\nit a secret from my granddaughter!
Countless years she's lived by the foot of the hill. Sometime in recent memory, she started spotting two young girls taking regular trips up to the top.
I wouldn't go around counting on\nold ladies to help you out of jams.
I'm sorry. This is important to me.
If you're happy to hear an old lady's\nramblings, I'd love to borrow your ear.
I don't have any particularly riveting\nstories to tell.
Well, let me pack my gloves\nand we can go.
If you can use it, I'd be glad to\npart with it.

The mayor of Flamesgrace, cradle of the Order of the Sacred Flame. Notably ardent in his faith, he believes it is only thanks to the guidance of the Sacred Flame that his beloved but sickly granddaughter Kate continues to thrive.
I hope it can be of some use.
I doubt I would be of much help...

The granddaughter of Flamesgrace's mayor, she was born with a weak constitution and suffered frequently from sickness. Since she began taking medicine prepared by a traveling apothecary, her health has slowly started to improve. She dreams of one day helping others—just like the apothecary that saved her.
No! I won't give this away!
I gotta ask my grandpa first.
You want this? All right, here.
We're in the very shadow of the\nchurch here. No roughhousing.

Once a dancer in the town of Sunshade, she ran away from her cruel master, and now lives a new life under a different name. She often has nightmares about her dreadful past, but accepts them as a punishment for leaving her friends behind.
Hmm... Sorry, I don't think I can\nright now.
Let's just say I've had an...eventful life.
I'd rather not talk about my past.
I'd be happy to perform one\nlast dance for you.
Sure thing! Hold it close and\nthink of me.

A vain knight so obsessed with his armor that some think he joined the Knights Ardante just for the attire. He spends hours every day tinkering, maintaining, and polishing his armor to a mirror's sheen, insisting that all his efforts are to further his ability to protect the faithful.
You think you can pierce my armor!?\nAbsurd!
You know what they say, right?\nThe armor is mightier than the sword!
A knight's duty is to protect\nboth people and things.
Would you like to hear more\nabout my armor?
I'm busy tending to my armor.
My armor and I will protect you.
I could not protect it... Here, have it.

Woodcutting is this man's trade, and he spends much of his time among the pines with axe in hand. Not one to scorn the gods, he makes sure to pay homage at the cathedral several times a week. In truth, he's really just there to see the archbishop's two beautiful daughters.
I got no reason to give you anything.
This, too, may be the Flame's guidance.
I'll help, if you'll have me.
I'd be much obliged if you got\ngood use out of that thing.

This young lad wishes to follow in the footsteps of his brave and noble older sister, who he greatly admires, and join the Knights Ardante. His sister, on the other hand, is worried a soldier's life is no place for her little brother's gentle soul.
You want this? I'm sorry, but I can't\npart with it...
I have no reason to speak to you.
It is my honor to give this to you.
I don't think I'm ready yet...

This giant of a man was once a mercenary who struck fear into the hearts of his foes, until he came across a group of war orphans. Confronted by the horrors of war, he realized that violence solves nothing, threw down his weapons, and entered the priesthood. Now he strives to be a surrogate father for children with no one to turn to.
Forgive me, but I cannot give this\neven to someone like you.
If you really want to talk, then...
There's no use talking about it.
Joining you will benefit children\nall across the continent. You have\nmy aid.

A former swindler who deceived innocent folk all across the continent. She once solicited alms for the Order of the Sacred Flame and planned on making off with the money, but repented her wicked ways after witnessing the altruism of the church's clerics up close. Gathering donations has become a genuine passion, and her daily prayers are honest and true.
My heart, my soul, my body,\nall must reside in the shadow\nof this great cathedral.
That sum isn't even worthy of\naccepting as alms.
Please don't interrupt my sacred work.
If you agree to donate great sums\nto the needy, I will gladly help.
Your noble heart moves me to comply!

Born into desperate poverty, this girl often visited the cathedral for free meals. She received endless helpings of sermons on the side, and before she knew it, she had memorized them all and somehow ended up a cleric. She's not really clear how that happened, but she knows one thing: she loves the rice the church serves its flock.
Trying to take advantage of me?\nThat's infuriating.
You give me money, I give you\nthe goods.
It might be nice to grow rice\nfor a change.
I still have duties as a cleric\nto attend to.

A merchant who deals in jobs; his original clientele were commoners looking for work. After the scarlet snow tragedy, he began helping reformed members of the Scarlet Wings find gainful employment. He was nervous about working with them at first, but after discovering that many are kind and earnest folk, that fear has faded. Now, he's struggling to imagine what drove them to crime in the first place.
Hmph. Offering employment is no\ncasual matter. Keep that in mind.
Would you lend me an ear?
Sorry, I'm preoccupied at the moment.
Experiencing what it is to be employed\nmay be a useful endeavor.

A true believer who prays with all her heart to Aelfric for salvation. The victim of countless misfortunes, she concluded a lack of faith was the root cause of her suffering, and so strives to ever greater heights of devotion. The bishop often praises her zeal, but she refuses to let it go to her head.
Why must I give this to you?
I don't have anything particularly\nnotable to say, but...
Be silent and pray with me.
This, too, is the will of the Sacred Flame.
May the Sacred Flame guide your path.

A cleric who raised his children—now in the Knights Ardante—on the teachings of the Sacred Flame. From it, his eldest daughter learned its intensity; his younger son, its kindness; and his eldest son, its dangers.
Why would I do that?\nI cannot think of a single reason.
This must be the will of\nthe Sacred Flame.
My apologies, but I don't have\nany time to spare.
If you wish it, I will let the Sacred\nFlame guide me.
This must be the will of the gods.\nPlease, take it.

A veteran of countless battles, this fearless warrior visits the cathedral on quiet days to pray for the souls of departed comrades. Her exploits have earned her the title "the Red Valkyrie" from allies and "the Bloody Goddess of Death" from foes. Many think her heartless, for she never bats an eyelash when a friend falls to the blade.
Why is everyone so quick to\nseek death?
My war is not by your side.
The weak deserve nothing.\nThat is the law of the battlefield.
Working for you should be interesting.
To the victor goes the spoils. Here.

Decades of faithful service to the church under his belt, this ancient cleric plans on hanging up his habit in a few years. Despite aching joints he struggles on with his work, and alongside Archbishop Josef, prays he will live to see another Kindling come to completion.
I cannot waste the little time remaining\nto me on such a trifling task.
May the Sacred Flame light your path.
I will do what I can, though I doubt\nthese old bones will be much use\nto you.
This must be the will of the gods.\nPlease, take it.

After losing both his wife and daughter to the Great Pestilence in the Riverlands, he dedicated himself to spreading the teachings of the Sacred Flame, which had been his only light in those dark times.
I cannot shirk my churchly duties.
Just call and I will be with you!
This must be the will of the gods.\nPlease, take it.

A scholar of the faith, she is currently researching why it is that Aelfric brought the Sacred Flame here of all places. Day after day, she dusts off ancient tomes and unrolls brittle scrolls in her search for answers. To her dismay, she has not yet found one. Still, the journey is as joyous as the destination, and she continues her work with a smile on her face.
You want me to go with you?\nHmm... Illogical. Request denied.
I can't hand valuable relics over\nto those who don't know how\nto properly handle them.
May I hear your thoughts on\nthe matter?
Not now. I'm performing my research.
Perhaps this will lead to some\nnew discovery!
I'm sure you'll find some use for this.

Bright, cheery, gracious, and devout, she works at the tavern but has fans all across town. The archbishop often orders meals from her establishment, and she makes it a habit to stop by the cathedral to pray whenever she makes a delivery.
I'm sorry, I can't give this to you.
Have you come to pray too?
If you'll please wait until I'm done\nwith my prayers...
You need this...? Well...okay, here\nyou are.

A precocious young lad with clever hands who spends his time crafting beautiful flowers out of ice. He hands them out to pretty girls on the road with the words "A gift. Pray, may I accompany you until this ephemeral flower melts?" and promptly steals their hearts. Rumor has it even adults have sought out this young man for romantic advice.
Will you be the one to melt\nmy heart of ice?
I'd like to be alone right now.
You want this?\nOkay, here you go!
A nobleman known for his generous donations. He believes it is the duty of the aristocracy to redistribute wealth fairly, and he aptly provides support within his financial means.
Give me a moment to think.
Is there any way I can help you?
My apologies, but the needy come first.
I am certain I can be of help.
There might be other towns that\nneed me more.

A cleric who lives a modest life in gratitude to the Sacred Flame. He was born into a wealthy family and grew up wanting for nothing, but when he learned that there are many in this world who are not nearly so blessed, he decided it was his duty to help them. He's so pure of heart that even the most hardened criminals can't help but repent after a single conversation with him.
I'm terribly sorry, but I can't let you\nhave that.
What is it you want to talk about?
Would you mind waiting a moment?
Please make good use of this.
If I can be of help...
I cannot leave this town just yet.

A seamstress who pays close attention to every detail of her work, from the cloth used to each individual thread stitched. She is responsible for making all vestments for the church, which are lauded for both their comfort and durability.
That's too important for me to\npart with.
Phew... I suppose we could talk\nwhile I'm taking my break.
I'm working right now.\nTalk to me later.
This is a very important item.\nPlease take good care of it.
I'd like to continue making the best\nclothes I can in a comfortable\nenvironment.
I'm busy now. I don't have time\nto contemplate moving.

A cat that takes pride in its luxurious coat. The only reason its snow white fur is so nice is because its owner brushes it daily, but it assumes this is the natural order of things, because what could make a human happier than caring for a cat?
Hehehe... I see you, too, have\nbecome my captive.
Stay away from me!\nYou'll soil my pristine fur!
This furrier is an Emberglow institution. Of late he roams the desolate streets distributing furs in an attempt to encourage the people. Why? Because a warm body means a warm heart.
You really wanna hear what I have\nto say?
All right, all right.\nLemme see what I can do.
All right, you win. Go on, take it.
I'm just a furrier.\nWhat good would I do you?
I'm running a business here. I have to\nbe strict about money, all right?

A malcontent man who never stops grumbling. He never liked the Scarlet Wings' high-handed manner, and was even imprisoned once for grumbling about it in front of them. Now that they're gone, he grumbles about not having them around to grumble about anymore.
Hear me out, all right?\nI promise it's not a grumble.
Grumbling's not my style...but did I just\nget hoodwinked into something?
I'm not the grumbling type...but I feel\nlike I'm getting a raw deal here.
Far be it from me to grumble...but\nI think you underestimated me.
I hate to grumble...but this town's\njust not safe anymore.

A woman who was once a slave of Tytos. She was freed by Tytos's death, but lost her family in the scarlet snow and has no home to return to. Learning that someone is hiring dancers in a village called Stillsnow, she considers turning her life around there.
Please, I just want someone to listen...
Telling you wouldn't solve a thing...
I'm with you. Lead the way.
This is why I can never save any money...
If anything, shouldn't you be\nhelping me!?
If anything, shouldn't you be\ndonating to me!?
This deacon was sent from Flamesgrace to help at the orphanage. He's not as popular with the children as Phina and struggles with the assignment daily.
Sorry, but I'm quite busy right now.
I just hope you can find a use for me.
I apologize, but the children do\nkeep me busy.
This is for the children, I fear.

This newly joined novice of the Knights Ardante didn't expect to see actual combat for some time, but with the Scarlet Wings devastated by the scarlet snow tragedy, he has been sent out as a replacement guard. Many of his comrades have been wounded, and every day he fears he may not see tomorrow.
You can count on me!\nProbably... I hope...
I don't have anything to give.
Head of the Artisan's Guild in Emberglow. A doting father to his only daughter, Roxanne, he is determined to rebuild the town as rapidly as possible to protect her.
You want a piece of me?\nYou got it!
You know what? I like you!\nHere, take this!
Sorry, but my job is rebuilding\nthis here town!
I ain't got nothin' for the likes of you!
Active in the areas around Emberglow, this hunter used to track down monsters solely to make a living. Since the death of Tytos, she has begun to watch over the town in order to make herself useful.
I'll show you what I can do.
You beat me... Go on, then.\nTake what you will.

Owner of a bar by the Cathedral of Tytos. Tucked away in an alley, the bar was once a well-kept secret for connoisseurs. But word got out when construction of the cathedral began, and now it seems to get quite a lot of business.
Just try not to summon me\nwhen the bar's open.
I can't leave the bar unattended, can I?
Come for a drink next time.
We finally have peace here.\nDon't make too much trouble.
A deacon helping to rebuild Emberglow. He goes to great lengths to be of service to the emotionally scarred survivors of the scarlet snow tragedy.
If I can be of use...
I have business to attend to...
Anything to help the wounded\nand suffering here.
If this will help light your path...
For now, my place is with the people\nof this town.
I doubt I have anything that could\nhelp you.

A man who grieves for the victims of the scarlet snow. He visits the memorial almost every day to pray for the souls of those who lost their lives to that terrible tragedy, along with the soul of Lord Tytos.
If this will bring some comfort to\nthose poor departed souls...
If these leavings are of use to you,\nyou're welcome to them.
I'm in no position to help anybody.
I'm afraid I can't help you.
A man who cannot sleep. When the scarlet snow fell on the town, he was safe at home and avoided harm. Now, however, he lies awake night after night, wracked by survivor's guilt.
Ugh... My head hurts.\nLeave me alone.
Maybe this will be the change I need.
Do with it as you will.
Leave me alone.\nI just want to sleep.
This isn't the time for that...

An old woman who lost her son in the scarlet snow tragedy and now lives with her granddaughter. Is the smile she always wears to protect her granddaughter from sadness, or to keep her own grief at bay?
Oh? What do you want, dearie?
I'm sorry, but I'm busy looking after\nmy granddaughter.
If you can find a use for an old lady\nlike me...
I can't leave my little one on her own.
Nothing I've got would be any use\nto you.

A devout adherent of the Order of the Sacred Flame who is riven with sorrow over the recent rash of hardships that have befallen not only this town but Orsterra as a whole. She also laments her own inability to do anything but pray.
Surely this was ordained by\nthe Sacred Flame.
May the Sacred Flame warm you.
I must keep praying until the right\npath opens before me again.

After losing his family to the scarlet snow, this boy withdrew into silence. Rinyuu was the first to reach out to him, and with love and care from her and the other churchgoers, he is starting to recover. He idolizes Rinyuu so strongly it borders on fixation.
What do you want? Go away.
Rinyuu says we should be kind\nto others.

A nobleman who lost everything when his mansion was destroyed by vile monsters. Utterly bereft of religious feeling, he had never even donated to the Order prior. Now, the church has taken him in. Genuine faith still eludes him, but his gratitude to the people of the Order is sincere.
Will you have compassion on me?
If there's money involved, I might be\ninterested.
If this is what you need, take it!\nGo on!
If it doesn't pay, I'm not interested.
Look at me! Do you mean to squeeze\nblood from a stone!?
Merchant on Good Terms with the Church

Deeply connected to the church in Flamesgrace, this merchant is currently in Emberglow to get in on the reconstruction project. Even as the incidents pile up and the scent of conspiracy grows more pungent, the merchant simply cannot stop getting more involved. Money talks.
I don't detect even a whiff of leaves\nfrom you.
Why not? My business here is\njust about wrapped up.
This is your lucky day!\nVirtuous deeds are rewarded, eh?
Sorry, but I still have some money\nto earn in this town.
Sorry. This is bound for the church.

The head of the Cathedral Guard, he dreamed of working under Tytos, the hero, since hearing the tale of how he drove the barbarians out. That dream will never come true now, but he remains determined to protect this cathedral as his hero's final legacy.
Is there a crisis that needs handling!?
I cannot answer that while I'm on duty!
Not this cathedral, not on my watch!
I'm sure Lord Tytos would permit it.
I suppose I can trust you with this.
Lord Tytos! Were you watching me!?
I can't just hand this over!
A parishioner who is easily spooked and terrified that the rumored cathedral ghost is actually Lord Tytos's unquiet spirit seeking vengeance for his violent death.
I'm scared to speak of it.
I'm terrified, but I'll do what I can.
I hope it is of some use to you.
I fear I cannot give this to you.

A visitor from Goldshore in the distant Coastlands who came to see the Cathedral of Tytos. She can't stop marveling at how much bigger it is than the cathedral back home. She's actually lost—she just hasn't realized it yet.
I haven't seen all of the cathedral yet.
A new journey!\nThat does sound exciting!
It's just a trifle, but perhaps\nyou'll find a use for it.
Sorry, but I haven't finished seeing\nthe sights yet.
You'd beg a traveler for handouts?\nRather gauche, don't you think?
A priest dispatched from Flamesgrace. His administrative competence, steadfast faith, and sagacious policy of not asking too many questions are most pleasing to Bishop Tarset.
If it's something I can help with...
I must see to my duties right now.
If the Sacred Flame wills it thus,\nso let it be.
If such as this can aid you in\nyour travels...
Would that I could help, but alas...
But what do I have that could help you?

A teacher in charge of the Knights Ardante's training. There were frequent monster attacks after the red snow fell in Emberglow, so he was dispatched to take command of the town's defense. He is an experienced knight and his subordinates have the utmost faith in him.
I'm sorry, but I'm quite busy\nat the moment.
You wish to protect your town...\nVery well. I will lend you my strength.
I cannot yet leave this place.

A calico cat kept by a believer of the Order of the Sacred Flame. Staying in the Great Cathedral allows it to curl up by the fireplace to escape the cold and get leftover stew if lucky. It is determined not to give up this privileged position to any other cat.
If you're just going to complain,\nthen no thank you.
It's good to share your wealth\nwith others.
Did you honestly think you could\nbuy my time for such a paltry sum?

A veteran apothecary who came to Emberglow after learning of the scarlet snow incident. He still resides there and continues to tend to those who require his aid. His son, Esperre, is also an apothecary.
What would you like to know\nabout me?
I would like you to have this.
I cannot give this to you.
Save the thrilling negotiations\nfor the stage, would you?
A soldier guarding the entrance to Theatropolis. His gruff countenance tends to strike fear into travelers, so he makes a point of offering a warm, cheerful welcome and any directions they may need.
Very well!\nYou may have what you ask!

A merchant who makes his money from tourists visiting the theatre. By making friends with actors and obtaining their personal effects to sell, he makes vast profits. At the moment, he appears to be selling props used in a play about a clan of beast-taming hunters.
I'm just not feeling your sincerity,\nyou know?
This was a premiere item!\nI can't let it go that cheap!
A pleasure doing business with you!

A merchant from the town of Valore in the Woodlands. He was hoping to test his business acumen in a town where money talks, but he drifted over to the Flatlands after his dream fell flat. A shady apothecary in Valore taught him to make a concoction that still comes in handy when fleeing his debts.
I'm in the middle of something.
This stuff works like a charm...

A Knight Ardante visiting from the garrison to stock up on supplies. Drills and discipline have given him a body of steel and indomitable willpower...but when he took in a play at the theatre, he wept openly for the first time in his life. Now he is desperate to share these emotions with somebody else.
I've no time for idle chatter.
The spirit of the Knights Ardante\nbe with you!
Obtaining this was no easy task.\nCare for it well.

This boy became unable to sleep after his parents took him to the theatre. His father and mother both come from long lines of artists, and were hoping that their son would hear the call, too...but he wants to be an archaeologist instead. When he can't sleep, he plays with the treasures he has furtively accumulated.
I can't give it to you just like that.
Can I tell you a secret?
I'm too scared to say it out loud.
With this money, maybe I could\nbuy a fossil...
Girl Waiting for Her Thespian Parents
Mommy and Daddy didn't come back after the play. They used to be so nice to me... Maybe that was acting, too.
"Very well." I sounded just like\nMommy, right?

A calculating blacksmith's apprentice. He respects his master's insistence on authenticity, but finds his earning potential curtailed by his master's selectivity about work. He is trying to convince his master to handle theatre props, too, but with little success so far.
...At that price, I'd be selling at a loss.
Talking to customers is part of\nthe job.
I don't want the master to think\nI'm neglecting my work...
Real weapons, or just ones for the stage—\nI'll make a place that sells them all!
It's not a very tempting offer,\nI'm afraid.

A merchant who spends every trade journey in a state of permanent terror. A froggen licked his face as a child, and he still has nightmares about it today. Now all monsters look like froggens to him, sending him into constant panic, much to the consternation of his bodyguards.
It's only frogs that scare me, okay?
I don't think you could get\nthe better of a tadpole.
I don't think you could get\nthe better of a tadpole.
Sorry, I don't feel like talking.
I'm sure I'll be useful to you.

An old lady who gazes at flowers with tender affection. Long ago, she bloomed on stage herself as an actress, but now her bad back has her all but confined to her house. Watering her flowers in the morning and evening, and reading the occasional poem help her stay serene.
I can't sell at that price.
Would you like to hear a poem?
I'm busy tending to my flowers.
A woman from the Coastlands. Cheery and capable, she attracts the advances of many would-be suitors, but is quick to brush them off. Of course, this only makes her more appealing to them.
That's kind of pathetic, to be honest.
Sure. I was starting to get bored\nof this place anyway.
Sorry, but I can't right now.
Old Man Who Wants to See a Cheerful Play

A ruddy-faced old man who loves his booze. He understands the power of Auguste's dramaturgy, but would still rather see something with more general appeal, like a farce or a swashbuckling adventure. Originally a hunter by trade, he is now retired and spends his days in glorious idleness.
Oh, I can't give you that.
Sorry, but I wanna revel in this post-play\nbliss a little while longer...
It may be old, but it's still useful.\nJust like me!
This librarian came to her career out of sheer love of reading. She prefers books with a narrative element, like novels and chronicles, so she decided to find work in Theatropolis, city of the arts.
I think this could get interesting.

A grim-faced scholar who gets lost in his reading. His patronizing manner has left him slightly isolated in the scholarly community. Since losing his wife, he regrets not being kinder to others in the past.
Tshaw! Is brute force the only\nlanguage you speak?
This was a gift from my late wife...\nTreat it with care.

A burly fellow diligently pursuing his studies. He lived a rougher lifestyle once, but turned over a new leaf and became a regular at the library. He secretly pines for the novel-reading librarian, and has begun writing in the hopes of winning her over.
Well, if I can use it in my story, then...
Time to put my book-learnin' to use.

A cleric who is also a dedicated reader. Her thoroughgoing command of the sermon literature seems to have helped her grow the audience for her own preaching, starting with the librarian and the burly man.
Have you come to listen to my story?
It's time to teach others what\nI learned here.
There's still so much I have to learn...

A man who has mastered the Way of the Book. Thrilled by his teacher's rapid riffling, he spent day and night turning the pages of weighty tomes, practicing the fundamentals until even the heaviest history of Orsterra was his to command. To ensure that his technique will not be lost, he is currently writing an even thicker book.
Feel the weight of the written word!
It appears your studies are\nnot yet complete!
It appears your studies are\nnot yet complete!
Your strength intrigues me. I shall\ninvestigate further while I assist you!
This is a very special book.\nGive it a thorough read!

A woman who loves the theatre so much she got a job there as a receptionist. Unfortunately, she didn't consider that being stuck at reception means she can't see any plays at all. She has recently started pestering the manager to let her switch places with the janitor.
That wouldn't even buy you\nstanding room!
That wouldn't even buy you\nstanding room!
Just call if you need me.
An aristocrat who speaks of the importance of reality. A devoted Auguste fan, he always places the winning bid when one of the great dramatist's scripts comes up for auction at the Grandport Bazaar.
To fight! To win!\nNo truer reality exists!
Try a more realistic approach next time.
Try a more realistic approach next time.
It seems my assistance is needed.
People are but beasts, after all.

A man of great wealth who came all the way from the Everhold Amphitheatre in the Highlands to learn more about the man hailed as the greatest playwright on the continent. He once harbored ambitions of writing his own plays, but the atrocious quality of his work proved an insuperable barrier. Now he focuses on supporting other talented playwrights instead.
I sense no inner light in you.
I sense no inner light in you.
I could discuss the finer points of\ndramaturgy forever.
I've no interest in speaking to you.
Let me show you some of my work.

This man is glum after missing out on tickets yet again. He used to be part of a small theatre troupe himself, but tickets here are so expensive and in demand that he never manages to buy any, though not for lack of trying.
I don't want to talk to anyone\nright now.
Money talks, friend!\nCall for me any time!

An aspiring actor who recently joined a theatre company despite his parents' protest. He used to help his family with their sewing business until he was unexpectedly captivated by the glamor of the stage.
I don't care what you think!
If this is really what you want,\nyou can have it.
This is the path for me!
I get the feeling...you would take\nme down the wrong path...

A farmer who came to sell his produce in Theatropolis. He tried his aggressive sales pitches, but quickly learned the town already had a distribution network in place. He now searches for somewhere his methods might be appreciated.
I don't feel like sellin' right now.
I know you want to hear what\nI have to say! And even if you don't,\nyou're gonna hear it anyway!
Thank the heavens. Glad <i>someone</>\nfinally bought something.
I don't need an invitation!\nI'm going whether you like it or not!
I'll go if and when I feel like it!

A famous actress often hired for supporting roles. Although she was satisfied with her repute as an unsung hero of the stage, her heart was shattered when she overheard a child innocently ask, "She's never the lead, is she?" As much as she wishes to be the star of the show, she will never have the chance.
I can't imagine you want something\nfrom a simple supporting actress...
You want to hear <i>my</> story?\nYou're an odd one...
You don't know the first thing\nabout me...
Go on, take it!\nIt even has my autograph!
Could I play a leading part in\nyour town?
I think I'll stay. No doubt I'd always\nbe in your shadow...

An actress who came to the amphitheatre after Auguste's passing. Desperate to win a leading role, she puts on melodramatic performances in an effort to stand out. However, this sadly backfires on her more often than not, because such melodrama ill suits the roles she is assigned. There are rumors that she is particularly close with Milos, who came to the theatre around the same time as her.
Please, ask me anything you like!
You'll let me star in a play!?

An up-and-coming actor cum playwright who was once a scholar. Unfortunately for him, people cannot help but compare his works to the late Auguste's, making it difficult for him to draw an audience. He racks his brain daily to find a remedy for this.
No, I'm determined to make a name\nfor myself in Theatropolis.
A chat might make for a nice\nchange of pace.
It's not a good time. I have a lot\non my mind...
Perhaps it's time I try something\nmore unconventional...
This noble is particularly fussy about his liquor and will pay any amount of money for a quality drink. He is especially fond of brandy made in Valore.
How could I possibly trust someone\nso stingy?
Are you perhaps a kindred spirit?
You wouldn't understand even if\nI explained it to you.
You know where I can purchase\nbrandy from Valore!?

A man who loves plays so much that he'll watch any highly acclaimed production twice. His curiosity compels him to get involved in things he shouldn't, which tends to get him into dangerous situations.
I'm not really in the mood to talk\nright now.

Born in a small mountain village, this youth is on a journey to discover his purpose in life, funding his travels through physical labor. He enjoys a life of solitude and is skilled in both cooking and sewing. When it comes to preferences, he likes cats over dogs and soft-boiled eggs over hard-boiled ones.
I'll decide for myself where I go next.
You're looking for someone who\ncan take over a fisherman's job?\nSounds interesting.
I'm in the middle of some self-reflection,\nif you don't mind.
You'll never know what you're capable\nof unless you try.

A dog who likes to sneak into the amphitheatre to watch plays. He has memorized lines from numerous different productions and does a fair Auguste impression, except no one can tell because they do not understand him.
Oh, is this a traveling performance?\nI'm in!
This is special to me, but you can\nhave it.
Only after I become a star here first!
I can't give that to anyone.

A low-class lender of money who spends his days finding just the right marks to prey upon for their money. Since he was never a good student, he seems to harbor his own personal grudge toward those who seem intelligent.
You say you're interested in\nthe work I do?
What? I got nothin' to say to you.
Me, save the world? Ha! Good one!
Hey! What do you think you're doing?\nYou got a problem!?

The finest chef in Atlasdam. When he worked at the palace, his cooking could please even the pickiest eater of the royal family. Now in pursuit of making the finest food, he is very particular about his ingredients, but has difficulty finding them.
Want to know the secret ingredient?
Sorry, but my recipes are a family\nsecret.
Chef's training? Don't mind if I do!\nCount me in.
Thank you! Now I can cook delicious\nmeals again.
I'm a little busy at the moment.\nCould you come back later?
Wh-what are you doing!?\nCan't you see that's dangerous?

A merchant who secretly trails Know-It-All Milo in an effort to press him for information. He follows Milo wherever he may go, creating "coincidental" opportunities to strike up conversation. All in the name of information...
I'm not the one you want to talk to!
Could someone take my place here?\n...No, wait. I'll go.
There. That oughta do the trick.
Hey, are you pulling my leg or what?

In charge of defending the castle town, this mercenary-turned-guard keeps a vigilant eye on those coming in and out of the gates. Due to his past as a mercenary, he's quick to spot suspicious figures, and uses whatever means necessary to apprehend them.
You want to talk to me?
I'm on duty right now. Sorry.
These occasional challenges of my skills\nare a bother more than anything else.
Hey, where do you think you are?
What do I look like, a merchant?
A thoughtful rancher who delivers fresh milk from his cows to his parents every morning. His parents worry for his future, though, as he dedicates his entire life to work alone.
I'd be happy to talk about my cows.
I'm only interested in talking\nabout my cows.
If I can bring my cows, sure!
I see you raise cows, too.
Caring for my cows is more important\nthan any journey you might be on.
You can't have it. I need this to\nraise my cows.

The childhood friend of the reckless boy by her side. Being the older by two years, she was entrusted with watching over him. Despite his endless curiosity oftentimes putting them both in danger, she takes her role seriously and is prepared to face whatever challenges come her way.
You wanna know more about me?

Brimming with curiosity, this young boy will go to whatever lengths necessary to sate an interest, even if it puts him in danger. Though his reckless tendencies worry those around him, the oftentimes rare and valuable items he brings back from his adventures have earned him the esteem of his friends.
You wanna know who I am?\nHaha, lemme tell you...
I wanna go for a walk instead!

After the Great Pestilence in the Riverlands, this apothecary decided to dedicate himself to disease prevention and swift treatment. He now works together with the Royal Academy in Atlasdam to create both medicine and new forms of treatment.
I'm working on something right now,\nactually.
Why do you care about what I have\nto say?
I'll join you. It'll be a good change\nof pace.
These are important research materials!\nI can't give them to you.

Every morning, this fisherman hauls his catch to the castle town of Atlasdam to earn some leaves. The less common fish tend to be left over, so he takes them to the tavern to have them cooked, and enjoys the fruits of his labor alongside a few drinks.
If you wanna know about liquor,\nI'll talk your ear off if you let me!
Not without a drink in front of me.
Good, good. Get the body moving\nan' get the mind nice an' clear!
Heh... You've got me all sobered up.
No, no, I ain't got enough to share.
A female cat belonging to a member of the nobility. She has left her owner's manse and is strolling through town in search of a suitable male companion.
I suppose it might alleviate\nmy boredom.
I'll graciously bestow this on you.
As if I'd acquiesce to such a request!

A woman who married into one of the many high houses of Atlasdam. She loves her thirty-something son so deeply that she just can't bear to cut the apron strings. She's so worried about him on his throne-room guard duty that she watches over him in secret.
Mind if I pour my heart out to you?
I'm more worried about my son\nright now.
I suppose it's time to let him go.
Is that all? Sure, I don't mind.
My apologies. I have my hands full\nwith that boy.
I'm sorry, I didn't catch that.

An erstwhile star actress who'd dazzled audiences in theatres throughout Orsterra. After her older friend and fellow actress Francesca died, she took in her son, Mikhail. She considers putting a smile on the face of this utterly bereft boy to be the greatest role she has ever been given.
Would you mind if I reminisce a little?
I shall win at any cost.\nI stake my pride as an actress on it!
Yes...I admit it. You're indeed powerful.
Well, when you put it that way...\nYou may have it.
Whether on the battlefield or stage,\nthe last one standing is the victor.
Did you really think you could\nfilch this from me?

A man with insider knowledge who's very much in demand of late. It's no exaggeration to say that his scoops on upcoming products have never been wrong. Word is, even Lord Eltrix of the merchant town of Grandport has taken notice.
You'll have to try harder if you want\nto learn anything from me.
Your reputation's well deserved!\nI say you've got what it takes.
Hey, I don't want any trouble.
Afraid nothing's quite that easy\nin this old world of ours.
At a glance, one might presume him a diligent and dedicated guard, but he has been scolded several times for sleeping at his post, as well as gambling with some of the scholars.
It's not easy being a guard, you know.
I mean, if you insist... I guess I have\nno choice.
You take good care of that, now.
I already have enough work to do.

Once in charge of protecting the king, this guard suffered a serious leg injury and was since transferred to a less intensive post inside the grounds. Though the injury healed long ago, he hasn't mentioned it to anyone, and enjoys the serenity of his new post.
Why would you want to talk to me?
I wish you wouldn't cause a ruckus\nright here in town.
Take it. It's better than nothing, right?
It's a dangerous world out there\nthese days.

No item or individual that passes through the palace gates escapes this guard's notice. Rumor has it, however, that his dedication is not rooted in any sort of loyalty, but is rather born of the desire to perpetuate and protect his own unscrupulous business of selling trinkets to black market merchants.
I'm just a sentry. Nothing more.
I suppose I can afford to be away\nfor a short while.
Please make some use of it.
I'd better get back to my post.
Come on. What are you doing?

Born and raised in Atlasdam, this old widow now visits this place every day in memory of her late husband. Even after she passes, she wishes to be by her husband's side, just like the relief of the king and queen in the academy's library.
I was young once, too, you know.
I'd feel bad making you listen to\nan old woman's ramblings.
I can join you for a walk, anyway.
I can see you're enduring much hardship.\nHere, take it.
Goodness me. I may be old, but I'm not\nabout to be bossed around.
But this is precious to me.

An avid reader who spends most of her waking hours in the Royal Library. She would rather read than eat, and once neglected to sleep for three days, too absorbed in a good book. She claims to have inherited her love of literature from her late mother.
I'm reading a fascinating book\nright now!
I'm in the middle of a book, so...
You want me to travel with you?\nOh my, a real-life adventurers' epic!
Thank you! I'll put this toward some\nnew books.
You'll have to tell me your tale\nsome other time.

Though one might expect the library's staff to be more inclined to read tomes, this man has a talent for reading individuals and intuiting their myriad concerns. As such, the many who seek his counsel regarding personal matters generally leave feeling satisfied—at least, so far as Mercedes can tell.
And what might you be looking for\ntoday?
I won't fall for that trick.
All right. I'll protect books and\nthe people who love them.
That's the thing about libraries—\nthe books we have here aren't for sale.
A modest man and purveyor to the royal family who takes great pride in his goods. Whether his customer be commoner or king, he refuses to sell something unless he deems the buyer worthy of it.
Small talk is all part of the sale.
I'm preoccupied at the moment.\nCould you save it for later?
Oh, is this a business opportunity?
Thank you kindly!\nI've been looking for the right buyer.
I can't sell this to you.

With a passion for paintings, this aristocrat spends his time and money collecting art pieces from painters in every region. Eventually, he plans on opening a museum to show the world the magnificence of all the artworks he has amassed.
Let us speak of the beauty of art!
Quite the collection, is it not?
You there!\nWhat in blazes are you doing!?
It would seem you can't comprehend\nthe value of art.

Born in the slums in the Coastlands, this guard was recognized for his skills and now protects the throne room. Despite holding the same position, he is ridiculed by the other guard simply for his origins, causing tension between the two.
I've got a complaint I need to\nget off my chest.
We're not allowed to talk on the job.
I won't let you run roughshod\nthrough this palace!
Certainly. I can lend you a hand.
I should be getting back to my patrol.

The son of a prominent family in Atlasdam and one of the guards responsible for protecting the throne room. Rather than earning his post, however, it was granted to him as he has no merits of his own.
Surely a quick break wouldn't hurt.
We get reprimanded if we prattle\non too much.
You won't defeat me that easily.
I'd be happy to lead the way.
I hope you can put this to good use.
You! Rogue! What are you doing!?
I refuse to be beaten by\nsome lousy travelers!

A young boy who sneaks into the palace to steal valuables. He sells his spoils to provide a life for himself and has yet to be caught. In actuality, the queen has already noticed, but has turned a blind eye to his actions.
I told you, I got nothing to say.
You want your share, eh?\nHehe...if you insist!
Are you trying to trick me?\nI'm not falling for that!

The eldest son in a family that has served the king for generations. He takes pride in his job as a guard and would risk his life to protect the king and his palace. This loyalty has earned him the king's utmost trust.
I'm in the middle of my shift...\nBut I suppose I have no choice.
Please forgive me, but I'm in\nthe middle of my shift.
Might you be lost, traveler?\nHere, let me show you the way.
I'll let it go just this once, though\nI'd ask you to refrain in the future.
Ruffians shall not pass. Off with you!
I beg your pardon, but what exactly\nis it you're trying to do?

Some 200 years ago, our Kingdom of Wold came about with the invasion of the Flatlands of Grandport, when the eight clans were formed. Atlasdam here became Wold's royal capital. You could say it was the fruit of the Flatlanders' friendship and wisdom! It's our duty to convey this truth to generations to come!
This is not just idle chatter!\nYou must give me your full attention!
Let us inscribe our names on the first\npage of history—together!
Please, keep the history of Wold alive.
Oh, no, thank you.\nIf it's not history, I'm not interested.
I can't give this to someone who\ndoesn't even understand history.

A still inexperienced professor at the Royal Academy. Kindhearted but easily persuaded, he finds himself acquiescing to particularly forceful people. But a secret fire is always burning in his heart, and he'd like to make his dreams come true one day.
I'm ashamed to admit it, but...
I'd love to speak with you, but\nI must prepare for my lecture.
I'd go anywhere to reach someone\nseeking an education.
Thank you so much.\nI'm one step closer to my dream.
I'm afraid I'm...not cut out for\nviolence...

Lauded as a child prodigy, this young man is now a scholar at the Royal Academy, but unfortunately lost his genius as he grew older. He now spends day and night conducting research in secret that he might regain the respect of his peers.
Ah, yes! So you want to hear my tale—\nthe tale of a child prodigy!
How dare you address me\nso frivolously!
My research requires funds.\nFunds require labor...
...More money for my research fund,\nI suppose.
I don't have time for your antics.\nI've got to get back to my research.

A divine scholar who's been taking classes at the academy for a long time. Scholars who question the gods' existence challenge him with divine controversies, but none have bested him—none, that is, but one beautiful silver-haired woman. Rumor is she became a cleric in a snowy land after she left the Academy.
Please do not interrupt my train\nof thought.
What a delightful argument you make!\nDo call upon me if you have the chance.
That would go against my beliefs.

[The Pros and Cons of Knowledge]\nIt's said that the scholar Salomon's abuse of magic led to the fall of the Kingdom of Bernstein 200 years ago. Yet it's also historical fact that the ancient kingdom prospered due to the study of magical arts. Knowledge and technology aren't bad per se; their user determines whether they're good or evil.
I don't like debating with laypeople.
I hope you find it helpful in\nyour studies.
Well, that's not very scholarly, is it?
No, I need to keep this.\nI use it in my classes.

[The Orsterra Riots]\nTwo centuries ago, the large nations of Grandport and Bernstein repeatedly invaded other nations, and soon found themselves on the path to destruction. Why is unrest so concentrated within this period of history? Was it coincidence? Divine guidance? Or could it be that the people of that era subconsciously sought out ruination?
Oh, are you interested in history?
I have class now, but maybe\nsome other time.
Experience is everything...\nAll right—count me in.
Now, this will really make you\nfeel history.
Our time here is so limited.\nI'll use my share for myself, thank you.
If there's something you want,\nyou ought to get it yourself.

A student studying medicinal herbs. He was researching panaceas on the urging of a certain woman; although he saw great success, the clinical trials ended in miserable failure. What's more, his friend and research partner was imprisoned and sent to Emberglow for reasons unknown. He continues his research to clear his friend's name.
I can't speak of this too loudly, but...
Please, I can't get into the details.
You want me? Are you sure?
Please, make some use of this.
Whoa, whoa...\nI can't possibly do that!
I'm afraid I don't really have\nanything...

A military scholar who attempts to analyze and theorize the tactics of the many wars in the legends of the heroic Beowulf. According to her research, it is not heroes, but optimal strategic structuring that is needed to win. She says that generals fail to understand this, despite never seeing battle herself.
I have a strategy for how I would\nget things done.
...Sorry, but my theory isn't yet\nready to share with others.
It's time to put my tactic into practice.
You want to strategically rebuild\nyour town, yes?
This is what you want, isn't it?
You can't have this, I'm afraid.

[Flowers That Change the Soil]\nCultivating certain flowers can change the quality of soil. She theorizes that by applying this method—which desert clans learned from experience—it is possible to protect areas prone to flooding and other natural disasters.
Basically, what I'm trying to say is...
I don't know if you would understand\nmy theory.
I may be able to test my theory there.
It's not time for me to go yet.

[Theories in Agriculture]\n"Why do neighboring plots of the same land yield different amounts of crops?" We can construct solid theories by unraveling this mystery, making it possible to increase output, but farmers have difficulty accepting new ideas.
I can tell you more about it\nsome other time.
I'm going to put everything into\npractice once we get there!
Can you get any use out of this?
I'm in the middle of my studies,\nyou see...
A scholar studying Orsterra's geology. His primary area of interest is volcanoes erupting with lava, in which he sees great intensity and beauty.
Let me tell you what I love about\nvolcanoes...
Will you truly understand my passion?
An investigation of seismic activity?\nCount me in!
Do you want to take this with you?
I doubt you share my passion.

Behind its adorable little eyes, this rabbit hides a vicious streak a league wide. It rips local monsters apart with its big, pointy teeth, and all who attempt to cuddle it quickly find themselves fleeing in terror instead.
I'll let you in on some advice.
Don't you dare think I'm just\na harmless little bunny!
You got me. Fine, have it.
I'm no ordinary rabbit, you know.
Try not to make too many waves.

This former sailor retired due to severe seasickness and went into business as a merchant in Rippletide. He is currently back in his hometown gathering stock to sell. He still fondly recalls his days riding the waves, and is always eager to hear from travelers or caravans.
I love to travel, but...at that price?
I may be new to my trade,\nbut I'm no fool.
I miss being out at sea.
Hush. I want to listen to the sound\nof the waves.
It's been too long since I hit the road!\nNow, where's the action!?
These are the finest of wares,\ngathered from far and wide.\nTake good care of them!

"The name's Le Mann. Le Mann, Explorer Extraordinaire...is what they'll call me one day. You wait and see. I'm busy preparing for my grand departure at some future date, so I can't accompany you, no matter how pitifully you plead. ...What do you mean you weren't pleading?"
I can't give away something\nso precious!
Let me tell you my story!
Consider it a token of my esteem!
L-looks like I'm not ready for\nadventure just yet.

Olneo runs the Colzione Family Provisioners. In his youth, he was a traveling merchant. He even met his wife Marina on the road. But his dream was to set up shop in his hometown, Rippletide. On the year his beloved daughter was born, that dream finally came true.
Sorry, but I'm running a business here.
Is there something you want to\nknow about?
I'd say we have a deal!

Marina is Olneo's wife. She met Olneo working in a general store in her hometown. The two fell in love, and she moved here to marry him. Head-turningly beautiful, she is still often mistaken for Olneo's daughter instead of his wife.
Well, aren't you a wicked one?
I'm not sure I can help you.
