This woman came to Rippletide to marry a fisherman, but was widowed in an accident at sea. She took up fishing herself and has made a comfortable living that way for decades now. She can't bring herself to throw away her husband's old harpoon despite the burden it represents.
In the old days, I even chased pirates\naway. This is going to be fun!
I don't sign up to serve weaklings!
I can't give it away that easily!
If you want to talk, save it for later!
I haven't been so impressed by\nsomeone since my husband died!
It's finally time to part with it...\nYou take good care of it for me.
This rather wan young man lives in Rippletide. He might look like he'd blow away in a strong breeze, but he has inner strength and an iron will.
G-go outside!? Do you realize\nhow scary it is out there?
I can't just give it away, though...
I'd rather not talk about it.
Y-you think going with you\nmight toughen me up?
W-well, if you put it that way...\nHere.

This woman hasn't missed one of her twice-daily training sessions in fifty years. Though she is past sixty, she remains powerfully muscled and is more than a match for the average man. Legend has it that, in her days as a wandering merchant, she once drove off a dragon with a spear.
There's still folks out there who dare\nto challenge me, eh? Well, not for long!
Come back when you've put\nsome muscle on.
You need to bulk up if you want\nto handle this spear.
Seems like you need my skills.
Take good care of my buddy.\nWe've been through a lot together.
This Rippletide mom is tough enough to face down the burliest men. She's also worried sick about her son, Tony, who turned out as feeble and weedy as his father.
You want to test your strength?\nLet's go, then!
I just can't follow a weakling.
Toughen up a little and try again.
That's not something I should be\ntalking about with strangers.
If you don't mind a hand-me-down,\nmaybe you can get some use out of this.
Elderly Woman from a Distant Land
This elderly woman has visited many countries and become thoroughly versed in their languages and cultures. Her long journey has finally led her to Rippletide.
You're asking an old lady like me\nto go back on the dusty road?
It might not seem much,\nbut it's a memento of my travels...
Will you listen to an old lady's\nstories?
It's too painful for me to talk about.
A new journey for my golden years.\nI like the sound of that!
It's a memento of my travels.\nTake care of it.

This childhood friend of Le Mann has alternately emboldened, egged on, and watched over the would-be adventurer for years. He collects equipment and experience nuts in the hopes that they will be useful to Le Mann one day.
Sorry, but I'm not the one\nwho wants to be an explorer.
Telling you about it won't help.
An adventure of my own, huh...?\nIt never even struck me as a possibility.
You've got yourself a deal.\nTime to buy my friend some new gear.

An elderly man who makes a living as an intermediary for bartering goods. His thoughtfulness has earned him high repute among his fellow merchants, but he is starting to grow tired of constantly gauging the feelings of others.
That's a bad deal no matter\nhow you look at it.
You willing to lend an ear?
Don't want to burden you with\nall my complaining.
I'd gladly go anywhere I can relax.
Apologies, but I've still got business\nto do here.
A writer whose free spirit is reflected in her travelogues. The wealth of knowledge she acquires as she traverses the land enriches her entries.
Will the pen emerge mightier\nthan the sword?
I can hear the bells of victory\nringing—in my head, that is!
Sorry, not now! I've got a lot to\nwrite down...
Here, take this. I even signed it\nfor you.
A spirited cook with great enthusiasm. While his food may not look great, it tastes amazing. He is particularly good at making egg dishes.
Can't you see that I'm busy?
If you aren't hungry, I've got\nno use for you.
My food will blow you away!
A lazy gull who likes to nap all day and feed on fishermen's leftovers instead of catching its own food. Its motto is, "Always take the easy route."
*yawn* I'm too sleepy to talk\nright now.
No trace, no trouble. Good luck\nout there.

Legends of swashbucklers and trailblazers inspired this merchant to become a seller of adventure books. Of late, the many tales of <chara_name>, the one who defeated the Covetous Witch, excite him to no end. He has even written a book about the valiant deeds of this hero whom he has yet to meet.
Do you like tales about adventures?
It's quite a long story... Maybe\nanother time.
This might just be the start of\nmy own little adventure...
This won't even buy me a book.
A merchant who deals with ore the world over, carrying everything from beautiful and rare crystals to building materials in his bag. His head is filled with knowledge of each and every type of stone.
I'd be happy to talk about stones.
Sorry, but my wares are heavy and\nI'm awfully tired...
Sure. Who knows what kind of\nrare ores I might find?
I worked real hard to get this.\nTreat it well.
You realize your request holds\nno value to me?
It seems you don't understand the\ntrue value of this merchandise.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 052943\nWares: Weapons and armor
Browse as much as you like!
I'm not one for idle chatter.
Very well... I'll give you this.
I compliment you on your fine taste.\nNow, hands off.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 167739\nWares: Dried foods
I'm not one for small talk.\nI'd rather you have a look around.
*sigh* I buckle too easily to pressure.
That wouldn't be in my best interests,\nnow would it?
I will not part with this lightly.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 193537\nWares: Swords and shields
Life always does this to me.
Sure, whatever. You'd only take it\nanyway if I refused.
Get back! Back!\nYou're bad for business!
Ugh. People always look down\non us merchants.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 200984\nWares: Accessories and miscellaneous goods
You have a good eye, traveler.
You're not gonna buy anything,\nare you?
I'll show you how tough a merchant\ncan be!
Haha, here you are. I'm happy to\ngive this to such a prestigious traveler.
If you're only here to look, then bug off.
No way, nuh-uh. I won't give this up.
An ambitious young man who works at the wharf in Grandport. He helps merchants unload their items, and is learning various things from his employer that he might one day become a trader.
So you're the ship thief.\nPrepare yourself!
I suppose I have no choice\nbut to help...
I won't report you to the guards\nthis time, but don't get in my way\nagain.

A powerful Grandport merchant who loves throwing his weight around. His tone is perfectly polite, but his words are insult and poison. Though he insists he's honest in his dealings, his prices are anything but fair. A merchant to the core, he'll gladly wring every last leaf from a customer.
I suppose I can spare a moment.
Don't you know that time is money?
I suppose I'll give a pauper a chance.
Oho? This may be a profitable\nventure...
It's perfect for a low-income laborer\nlike yourself!
Would you mind leaving me in peace?\nThere's no profit in dealing with you.
I'm terribly sorry, but this item is\ntoo good for a beggar like you.

Born into privilege, this girl spends her days voraciously reading books she ordered from Atlasdam. As her overprotective parents do not allow her to embark on adventures of her own, she instead lets her imagination travel to every corner of the realm.
Are your stories more interesting\nthan the one I'm reading?
Don't interrupt me when I'm reading!
I wonder if I'll be any use to you.
Well, I suppose I wouldn't mind\ngiving this to you.
I don't like overly persistent people.
Perhaps you'd better leave before a\nhardcover bludgeons you senseless.

A good-humored man who frequents the tavern in Grandport. He easily befriends all he meets, claiming good food and spirits will get anyone to talk. This tactic may have contributed to his past success as an informant behind enemy lines.
Let's have a nice, friendly chat,\nshall we?
You don't seem like someone who\nknows how to have fun.
Life's great, so sure! I'll help you!
I'm in a good mood today, so here!\nTake it.
Sorry, I'm not in the mood today.
Hey, you! What're you trying to pull!
An extremely devout cleric who spends more time drinking at the tavern than conducting services at his chapel.
I have a feeling I'll be drinking some\nfine spirits today.
Huuuh? Oh, ohhh. Yeaaah, all yours.
I'll think about it over a few more\ndrinks... *hic*!
I may be drunk, but I'm a cleric of\nthe Order of the Sacred Flame!\nDon't insult me!

A beautiful woman who works at the tavern in town. Her family owes the landlord a large sum, and works hard to pay it off. Though he has offered to forgive the debt, she declined out of a sense of shame over her parents' squandering ways.
Sure, I can talk. Oh, make sure to\nbuy something afterward, okay?
I don't have time to chew the fat.
You've got a persuasive tongue.\nVery well, I'll help.
Hmm... All right. As long as you\npromise to value it.
Others may be fooled, but I'm not\ngullible enough to fall for that!
I hope we can have a meaningful\nexchange.
Always good to test the blade first.
It's not much, so take it.
What, you know a way to get rich?
If it doesn't make me money,\nI don't care.
Ah well. Go on, take it.
Hmm... Sorry, you're just not the\nright person for it.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 170926\nWares: Medicinal ingredients
Want to know what I have in stock?
Why not? I doubt I'll get another\nchance to do this again.
I don't do things that aren't in\nmy best interests.
Haha, back off. Us harborfolk will\nsnap at the slightest provocation.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 115423\nWares: Fresh fruit
Talking makes you thirsty, right?\nWell, these fruits make fine juices!
I want to sell these while they're fresh,\nso can we do this later?
Fine, if you want it that bad,\nhere you go.
I have nothing to sell to you.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 192031\nWares: Dancing garments and accessories
Take it from me, these would look\namazing on you!
You're not really into fashion, are you?
People say I'm a pushover.\nGuess they're right, huh?
You want this? All right, here you go.
If you think I'll say yes to anything,\nthen you're wrong.
You strike a hard bargain. Too hard,\nin fact, so I'd suggest you go.
Official Grandport Merchant\nRegistration No.: 200515\nWares: Hunting equipment
Sorry, but I have some business\nI need to deal with.
Come back when you've got\nmore leaves!
I don't have time to talk.
I just hope I can help...
You've got a good eye. Here, enjoy.
Hmm... I don't think I can.
You should leave before I chop off\nthose hands of yours.
Let me explain the finer points of\nmy wares first.
No, not if you're not planning on\nbuying anything.
Well...all right. I'll accept your offer.
The head of the Grandport Merchants' Association. He decides who can sell their wares at the market. A born and bred merchant, he can often be spotted scouring the stalls for hidden treasures.
How do you like our market?
I'm doing my rounds, so I don't have\ntime to talk.
It is a merchant's sacred duty to aid\ntravelers in their work.
Hmm... Yes, I believe you're worthy\nof this. Here.
Sorry, but that's not nearly enough.
Yes? If you have business with me,\nout with it already.

Ing's Log: Entry #29173\nIn my life, I've witnessed countless wonders and rarities. Of late, I find myself jaded. Nothing seems to inspire me to put quill to paper...or so I thought, until inspiration struck! That's the trick with muses: they come when least expected. My scribblings shall continue!
Is it worth writing in my diary?
I've kept a diary for seventy years,\nand it's got a good heft to it now.\nGood for bludgeoning.
Very well! Let us pen a new page\nin my annals together!
Oho! You have a fine eye. Here, take it.
I think I'll decline. I doubt the journey\nwill be worth a paragraph in my diary.

"The Grandport Merchants' Association shall bear no liability to victims of swindlers or thieves, nor will it intervene in business disputes. It is incumbent on all concerned parties to resolve such matters themselves."
Make sure to read the fine print.
Don't talk to me. I'm working.
A contest of strength? I'm a busy man,\nbut why not?
I'm a busy man, but...a journey with you\nsounds too interesting to pass up.
Suspicious. You looking to get arrested?
Try that again, and you'll pay!
A noblewoman with poor taste in jewelry. She loves collecting items from all over the world, and goes to the market every day hoping to find new pieces to add to her collection.
Do you have exciting news for me?
No, I don't think I can expect much\nfrom you at all.
I won't let some young whippersnapper\nbeat me.
I'll offer what little strength these arms\nstill have to give.
Hehe. A youth like you could use this.
You must be in dire straits if you're\nasking me for help.
Did you think you could trick\nan old lady? Shame on you.

An ancient mariner who sailed the seas with trusty harpoon in hand until a leviathan sunk his ship. Since that day, he's dedicated himself to vengeance. Unfortunately, his harrowing experience gave him a phobia of sailing, and so he's spent decades standing on the shore, glaring at the pitiless waves. An old man now, he hopes a hero will arise to avenge him.
It is a dreadful tale, but if you're\ncurious...
Ahh, this takes me back...\nWell c'mon, let's scrap!
This would be better used by younger\nhands.
You go to where my enemy is not,\nand so I must refuse.

A woman from a southern nation who traveled the realm working odd jobs until she found her way to Grandport several years ago. In her youth, she helped her mother with the daily cooking, and learned a great deal as a result. Her specialty is stewed fish with quatrait.
Let me share with you my stories.
I'd rather not speak about things\nlong past.
I just hope I can be of use...
This should go to those in need.
I don't think I'll be much help.
My folks sent me that. Please give it\nback.
Heh... How'd you sniff me out?
I don't think there's anything\nfor you here.
Eh. I don't see why not.
Sorry, but I'm conducting serious\nbusiness here.
You want something from me,\nyou gotta pay up.
A happy mutt with not a care in the world. He is a good dog—but to be fair, all dogs are good dogs, and a pox upon he who dares to say otherwise.
Don't get in the way of my playtime!
Are we gonna go for a walk!?

Today's Menu:
- Mossy cave tortoise garden salad
- Giant sheep à la tomato stew
- River bean vichyssoise
- White grape muffins
Will you lend me your ear?
I'm too busy trying to think about\nwhat to make today.
If you're in a bind, I'd be glad to help.
Help and help alike, I say.
Sorry, I've got hungry little mouths\nwaiting for me.

In the harbor she lives, working until the dawn and searching for ever elusive love. A thousand romances she's had, a thousand teary goodbyes as paramours set out to sea. Once gone, none return, and today too she waits upon the sandy beach with tears in her eyes.
Have you taken an interest in me?\nHehe... Very well.
In the end, you'll leave too, won't you?
All right. As long as you promise to\nstay by my side.
Have as much as you'd like.
Pass. You'll just leave like all the rest.

A man hired to host the Merchants' Fair. He is highly regarded by the traders for his booming yet soft, smooth voice. Word of his skills has even reached Victors Hollow, and rumor has it the arena organizers have offered him a job.
We're currently in the midst of\npreparations. Please come back\nanother time.
I can offer my help in between\nauctions.
Yes, this is a fair price.\nI shall make this trade.
I have a job. I can't just leave.
Ha ha ha!\nSorry, but I'm not a merchant.

One of the noble families sponsoring the Merchants' Fair. Unbeknownst to her unrefined husband, she is quite active behind the scenes, dictating how the fair grounds are to be decorated, as well as advising how the auctions are to be conducted.
If we'll find rarities along the way,\nyou can count me in.
It's yours on one condition! Bring me\nany rarities you discover. Deal?
We can discuss your request after\nyou've put a few fine items on auction.
If you want it that bad, be sure to\nbid high at auction.

This merchant makes frequent trips to the bazaar, where he mixes work with pleasure. A man with more hobbies than stars in the night sky, his most recent obsession is hiking. He claims he climbs to clear his mind for business, but rumor has it he's just trying to avoid his domineering wife.
Do you know of any good activities\nto fill one's time with?
Don't bother me. I'm trying to think of\nways to pass the time.
I wouldn't expect much from a simple\nmerchant like me.
Eh, if you want it, sure.
Beg all you want, I can't help you!
No way! My wife would kill me!

As a girl, this adventurous soul read a story about a mythical land far to the east where even the trees are said to be made of gold. Too pure to doubt a single word that is written, she's dedicated her life to finding this lost land, and is currently trying to fund an expedition to discover a sea route. How she'll react when she learns her fantasy is just that, none can tell.
Would you like to hear my life story?
You're out to steal my personal details,\naren't you?
Will traveling with you help\nmy fundraising efforts?
Here, take it. A gift from a fellow\ntraveler.
That isn't enough to fund\nmy adventures...
Oh ho ho! That was a close call!\nThis is mine! It'll never be yours!

An obdurate gatekeeper who always follows orders to a T. One of his core beliefs is that evil must be punished—a conviction that's been shaken by recent revelations. He respects his colleague for his hard work and dedication, but has discovered the man is an ex-pirate. Good or evil? Who can say?
If you need something, I'd be happy\nto help.
I don't talk about personal matters.
You've been a lot of help to me.\nI must repay those favors in kind.
Take it. It might come in handy.
I have no time for acts outside\nmy duties.
Sorry, but I can't part with this.
A sea captain who has returned to Grandport. The town's nobles have contracted him to ferry passengers and goods. The fishes he catch during his long voyages bring happiness to the townsfolk.
I've got plenty of stories to tell.
Sorry. Got business to attend to.
Here, think of it as a parting gift.\nAnything can happen on the seas.
The sea's my home. Traveling around\nwith landlubbers doesn't suit me.
Sorry, but I can't part with this.

A reformed corsair who changed his ways after Lord Eltrix gave him a thrashing. Now he guards the manor gates, spending his days stressing about what his hardheaded colleague will say when he discovers his criminal past.
Whatever it is, it's got nothing to do\nwith me.
Hah, this takes me back to my days\nas a buccaneer. Let's have ourselves\na brawl!
The strong'll always have my respect.\nGuess I'm still a pirate at heart.
Hope this helps you on your journey.
I have a debt to pay to Lord Eltrix.\nI can't just leave.
Take it from a reformed pirate:\nplundering never pays.

A marine biologist who came to Grandport looking for information on the leviathan that attacked the town long ago. She admires the behemoths of the world for their self-sufficient solitude, while harboring nothing but contempt for the social beast that is man.
All right, but I can only spare\na moment.
I believe I'll learn much about creatures\nof the sea if I stick with you.
Here. It's nothing much, of course.
If you were a terrifying monster\na hundred spans or more, I'd follow\nyou anywhere.
I'm only willing to share what I have\nwith those vast and terrifying monsters\nof the deep!
A sneaky thief who slipped into Grandport hoping to burgle the lord's fortune. Never one for planning ahead, he's made it out of dozens of tight spots through swift feet and sheer luck.
And why should I blab to you?
With my smarts, you'll be unstoppable,\n<chara_name>!
You're nothin' next to me,\nbut I respect your nerve.
Hands off. 'S mine, and I wouldn't\nrecommend stealin' from a pro.

A broker known for his talents in trade. His motto—"buy high, sell low"—goes against common business practices, yet he continues to thrive. His honest policies and generosity have earned him good repute.
You'll have to earn my trust first.
Oho. You want to introduce me\nto a new market?
I'll decide when it's time to explore\nother markets.
Do you even understand the value\nof this item!?

A man on the hunt for a promising investment opportunity. He seems to enjoy watching towns grow and thrive. While Grandport is a tempting choice, he feels it may not need his help. He hopes to find a town that can be transformed by his investments.
Apologies, but I have another\nappointment to get to.
Wishvale? Yes, that's a name\nbrimming with hope!
I hope it will be of use to you.
I need a little more time to consider.
It's bad form to try and buy something\nfor less than it's worth.

A farmer who has won the crop competition multiple times at the bazaar. Because of this, she is the wealthiest farmer in the area and now helps judge the competition. She takes pride in her job and being able to provide food for the people.
Is there something you want to know?
I can't talk to you before the competition.\nPeople might find it suspicious.
Are there any good farms there?
Take this. I'm happy to share!
I don't have it in me to move to\na new town right now...
A cat at the port staring out at the sea. She was saved from monsters by a human, who has since set sail and has yet to return. She mewls in yearning for a chance to see him again.
Will you listen to my story?
Please...leave me to drown in\nmy sorrows...
Sailing across the waters is quite\na frightening thought...
A Knight Ardante charged with protecting the divine rings. Few know of his existence—as well as the existence of the rings—as both are a closely guarded secret.
Now, let's see what you are\ncapable of. Show me the power\nof the Chosen One.

A woman who came to Sunshade for her own reasons. She once enjoyed success as a dancer, but she has since fallen terribly ill and can barely walk. She continues to fight her illness, dreaming of giving the attire she wore in her youth to her only daughter that she left behind in her hometown.
Forgive me, but I cannot give this\nto you.
It feels like a lifetime ago now...
That's not something I like to\ntalk about.
I hope it can be of use to you.

A kindly old man living in Sunshade. It's hard to believe based on appearances, but in his youth he was a feared assassin called the Desert Reaper. However, he was betrayed by his client who was an aristocrat, and lost his daughter, which drove him to withdraw from the trade.
There's some of my old skill yet left\nin these bones, I'll have you know!
That's it? Kids these days don't\nknow what real work is.
That's it? Kids these days don't\nknow what real work is.
Would you like to hear my life story?
Now that's something!\nIf these old bones have aught\nto offer, then offer it I will!
Fantastic! Here, by all means,\nhave what you like.

A soldier who works for the tavern owner. He was born and raised in a frigid village in the northern reaches of the continent. Having lived around folk always bundled in coats, he was shocked to see the way the Sunshade dancers were dressed and decided then that he would spend the rest of his life in this town.
Hey, if it's brute force you want,\nit's brute force you'll get!
I'll let it slide this time, but you\nmight wanna be smarter about\nwho you face off with next time.
I'll let it slide this time, but you\nmight wanna be smarter about\nwho you face off with next time.
I've nothing to say to you.
Guess this is just how it goes.\nI'll join you for a while.
Took me down, eh? Well, a win's\na win. Here, take it and go!

A soldier who works for the tavern owner. He led an impoverished life in his youth with no leaves to his name until he joined a band of thieves, which put him down a path of crime just to survive. Although he's since washed his hands of such unsavory business, he'd still do just about anything for money.
I suppose, but just for a little while.

A man leaning against the wall with a straight face. He's dreadfully uncomfortable due to the heat, but is trying his best not to show it. He thinks it would make him unpopular if he did. The dancers find him creepy because they can't get a read on what he's thinking.
Haha, admit it, you were instinctively\ndrawn to me.

The scion of a powerful trading company in the Sunlands. Though he's been a serial womanizer for most of his life, he's recently fallen for a dancer from this establishment and now sends her gifts daily. He doesn't know it, but as a result of his extravagant spending, his company is on the brink of collapse.
Bahaha! Wait till you hear this!
I don't have time to talk to you.
You've got good judgment\nto seek my help!
It's a deal! Hope to do business\nwith you again.

Formerly one of Helgenish's favorite dancers. She mainly served guests due to her lackluster performances on stage. However, after her boss discovered she had fallen in love with a generous customer, she has only been tasked with odd jobs.
Mm, no. I want a bit more than that.
I suppose a little chat couldn't hurt...
Now, now. Don't get carried away!
Take good care of it. I don't want\nto hear that it got damaged.
I can't stand working here anymore!\nTake me away. I don't care where...
I can't go. I'm just about to get\nmy old job back.

A woman who works at the tavern. Her natural cheeriness secured her a spot as the face of the establishment. Her specialty is in dealing with rowdy customers by using the soulstones she inherited from her grandfather. Quite a few customers have been charmed by her gallant appearance.
Try again some other time!\nMy door's always open.
I'm working right now, but...\nI can make a little time.
Okay then, here!\nGo on, no need to be shy!
The tavern should be fine without me.\nI'd like to see what your town is like.
I can't just leave the tavern.

A man who was once an actor in Theatropolis. After Auguste's death, he lost everything he believed in. To fill the hole in his heart, he came to this tavern rumored to be the greatest on the continent. Now his heart is made full again by one beautiful tavern maid.
I can see right through this little\nshow of yours.
I'm but a puppet dancing to\ndestiny's ditty...
I'm afraid I have nothing to\ntalk about.
Yeah, fine, you make the grade.\nI'll give it to you.

A man traveling with his young wife. He is a leading researcher of culture and etiquette across the lands with a great deal of influence over the scholars within his field. However, despite his prodigious professional status, the one person who commands even more power than him is his wife. If he were to ever disobey her, he knows just what fate awaits him.
If you won't pay a fair price,\nthere's nothing to discuss!
If you won't pay a fair price,\nthere's nothing to discuss!
If it'll aid me in my research...
Don't disrupt me when I'm enjoying\nmyself.
I'll endeavor to be of use to you.
Thank you very much. This will\nsupplement my research funds.

A woman traveling with her older husband. While she may be perceived as a paragon of grace, she was made a warrior through her rough upbringing. If she is ever dissatisfied with her beloved husband, she does not hesitate to show it—with force.
If you're looking for a fight, I can\nassure you that you've found one!
Feeble! And stunningly weak...\nWe'll see about a rematch once\nyou've had more training.
Feeble! And stunningly weak...\nWe'll see about a rematch once\nyou've had more training.
What? You wish to speak with me?
It's a shame, but as the loser I must\nconcede to you. You may call me\nwhen you need me.
It's a shame, but as the loser I must\nconcede to you. You may call me\nwhen you need me.
A man who serves an art dealer. He has sworn absolute obedience to them and doesn't shirk his duties to care for the house even when his employer isn't present.
I should hope for somewhat\nbetter terms than that.
I should hope for somewhat\nbetter terms than that.
Are you looking to negotiate\na business deal?
I can't enter any formal business\ndiscussions without my employer\npresent.
I shall aid you—when I have\na break from my duties.
Very well! The deal is struck.

A wealthy merchant who accrued his fortune via gold dust trading. Due to the incredible number of people that would surround him with the sole objective of snatching a piece of his fortune for themselves, he became paranoid and refused to meet anyone. He has recently hired a mercenary whose dispassionate views of money have brought him peace of mind.
I won't be a part of such\nludicrous negotiations!
I won't be a part of such\nludicrous negotiations!
And just what do you plan to do\nwith that information!?
Well, I suppose you do seem\na trustworthy business partner.
Well, I suppose you do seem\na trustworthy business partner.
A woman gazing at the desert. She enjoys watching the sparkling flow of sand just beyond the town, and, though the exact reason isn't known, is oft brought to tears by the sight.
Please leave me be.\nI wish to be alone...
Please leave me be.\nI wish to be alone...
Do you understand how I feel?
Speaking of it would be futile.
It is my fate to move about\naccording to the will of another.
I'll give you this, so just leave me be.

A mercenary who gives every job his all, no matter how trivial it may be. Those around him view him as just another hot-blooded man, but in truth he immerses himself in the task at hand to prevent the memories of his painful past from resurfacing.
You wanna see what I got!?\nFine then, bring it on!
I win. Guess you were out of\nyour league.
I win. Guess you were out of\nyour league.
...I know when I'm bested.\nCall me when you need me.
...I know when I'm bested.\nAs promised, take it and go.
A woman who always comes at the same time to draw water. She likes older, bearded men who know how to treat a woman. If he's got a brain between his ears, even better.

A beautiful dancer who belongs to the secret organization known as the Sandcats. The Sandcats keep a close eye on the twelve tribes of the Sunlands and report their findings directly to King Khalim. They generally operate with covert precision, but as the strongest organization member, she has no concern for stealth. She acts as her curiosity wills her and is not opposed to standing out from the crowd.
Want to dance? As you wish.\nI'll gladly dally with you!
Disappointing. Come dance\nwith me again sometime.
Disappointing. Come dance\nwith me again sometime.
You're a strong one. I'm looking\nforward to fighting at your side!
Not too shabby!\nMy fan is yours, if you want it.

A soldier patrolling the market in Sunshade. He was stationed here after making a passionate speech to the mayor about protecting honest shoppers from pickpockets and unscrupulous merchants. In actuality, he just wants to see all the rare items on display.
What sort of wares does your town\nhave?
I need to safeguard this town.

A broad-minded miner with a ruddy complexion. With a bottle of liquor in one hand, she travels the land searching for quality bedrock. Once she finds some, she swings her pickaxe with daring yet delicate strokes.
You drive a hard bargain—\ntoo hard for me!
Keep going and you'll find out\nhow sharp my pickaxe is!
Wishvale, huh? I heard there's some\nrare stone there.
Maybe another time. I've still got\nsome rocks to mine.
A beloved chef who whips up meals across the Sunlands. His fees are high, but he honed his cooking skills in the capital of Marsalim, making them well worth the price.
I'm afraid I don't give discounts.
I have a full schedule right now,\nI'm afraid...
I feel this trip is going to be\nlonger than the rest.
I have a big order to fill,\nso I can't leave right now.
A recruiter who introduces people to jobs across Sunshade. Although he can be curt, he is well-liked for helping personnel find employment under favorable conditions.
If I can be of help...
A caravan camel who has crossed the desert countless times and desperately wishes to retire, but continues to haul his body across the sands because he has no successor.
Will you listen to my tale?
I don't have the energy to talk.
If it'll lighten my load, then\nby all means.
That belongs to the merchant, not me.

A motivated and hardworking slave—at least on the surface. In truth, he is biding his time and waiting for the chance to launch a revolution. He believes he has kept this a secret, but those close to him gossip about his rebellious intentions.
I'll make an exception for you.
Leave me alone!\nThere's nothing to see here!
Heh. Just make sure the pay's good,\nokay?
Hey now, that's not nearly enough.

A woman with a penchant for gazing out at the desert sands. No longer needing to work hard to make ends meet, she dedicates her free time to impossible flights of fancy. The vast expanse of the desert inspires her, providing inexhaustible fuel for her imagination.
I have some time on my hands,\nso I'll be glad to help.
This is a fair price indeed.
I'd prefer to stay and enjoy\nthe view for a while longer.
Oh. You're trying to rip me off.

A miner with a perpetual gloomy look on his face. Crushed under the weight of debts inherited from his parents, he's spent over half his life as a slave. He tried to escape once, but was quickly captured and beaten until he became unrecognizable. He's since given up on life.
Haha. A brief taste of freedom will only\nmake what follows all the more bitter.
With this, I won't need to worry\nabout tomorrow's meal.
Haha. No, I'll never be able\nto escape this horrible life.
If you want something,\nmake a less offensive offer.

A young orphan quite mature for his age. Coming from an impoverished mining family, his parents were murdered after they displeased the lord. If not for a kind apothecary, he would have never smiled again. Since that fateful day, he's done his best to support his fellow orphans.
Well, if you're willing to help then...
If you won't lend me a hand,\nI can't lend you one either.
Here. Helping each other is important,\nright?
I can't trade for that little.

A young girl who digs wells in the desert in the hopes of pioneering new routes across Sufrataljah. Though many praise her for her laudable efforts, few are willing to help her with this seemingly impossible task. Nevertheless, she continues her solitary, quiet work to create a better future.
Do you want to hear about my dream?
Sure, if you'll help me make\nmy dream come true one day.
Sorry. I'm too busy working hard\nto fulfill my dream.
I have nothing I can give you.

A former miner promoted to the post of taskmaster. Though he believes his promotion makes him one of the wealthy elite, the people he believes are his newfound peers are simply using him for their own ends. Unaware, he flicks his whip for his cruel masters day after day.
Heh, this is more than enough.
I'm a taskmaster, got it?\nI can't leave my post.

A man who flings himself into his work with bottomless enthusiasm. The truth is his wife tried to control every aspect of his life, and after falling out of love with her, he fled. He's enjoying his newfound freedom, but little does he know his wife is in hot pursuit and coming ever closer...
Hey there. Are you a traveler?
L-leave me alone! I'm not going back!
If you want me, you got me!
Sorry, but I've got to focus on this now.
Hey, c'mon now. That's far too little.

An unemployed maid looking for work. Peel back the facade, however, and her true identity is that of a spy for the United Kingdom of Triblia. She moves far too quickly and quietly for a simple servant; she can appear beside you without making a single sound...
I have nothing to say to you.
I...suppose I can help you.\nAs a maid, of course.
Looking for a job has lightened\nmy wallet, so...sure.
I am a maid! I have duties!
I'm looking for a job, so I don't have\nanything to spare.

A priest of the Order of the Sacred Flame. He is a true believer in his faith's tenets of equality, and eagerly proselytizes his cause. This has generated him much enmity from the nobles of the highly stratified society of Sufrataljah.
If this is for the betterment of all\npeople, then I would be glad to help.
Please, share this with those\nwho need it.
I apologize, but I cannot give you\nspecial treatment.
There are many who have greater need\nthan I. I suggest you speak to them.

Though he was the first man to make his fortune in the gold rush, he never lost his appreciation for the miners' struggles. Even now, he spends his days working beside them in the dust and the dirt. He understands their hopes and troubles, and wishes for nothing more than to see their prospecting dreams come true.
A change of pace is welcome every\nnow and then. Very well...
Unfortunately, we've just found\na new gold vein, so I'm too busy.\nFarewell!
Do you know how many grams\nof gold dust this is worth?\nReturn when you do.

This hard-bitten housewife is renowned as a legendary warrior, even amongst the martial Detof. Though she's settled down to domestic life, her skill has not abandoned her, and she performs every household chore with deadly precision. Her unparalleled skill with a dagger is now being put to use mincing vegetables rather than foes.
You want to hear my story?\n...You sure are an odd bird.
Heh. I'm just a simple housewife,\nso go easy on me!
Guess it's worth stretching\nthe old muscles now and then.
If you want it that much, fine! Here!
Sorry! As you can see, I have\na family now.
Family and home... It's my duty\nto protect them both.

A watchman who has patrolled his town night and day since he joined the guard. He takes great pride in keeping the peace, and has done so since the reign of Lord Julian. Though he serves the despotic current ruler, in secret he does all he can to protect the powerless from the cruel excesses of the nobles and elite.
I have work to do right now...
If you can make use of this,\nI give it freely.
I cannot abandon the town that\nLord Julian loved so...
I will not give this up.

A roustabout often found sitting by the roadside, glaring at passersby and spitting a lot. Too poor to afford enough rations, his growling belly only fuels his rage. Really, he's perpetually angry, but doesn't know who to be angry at. He's decided to split the difference and be angry at everyone.
If you're gonna be pushy, fine...\nBut you better pay up!
Tch! Stubborn fool! Fine, fine!\nIf it'll make you go away!
I don't have to help you, got it!?
Huh!? Yeah right! You trying to\ninsult me with that offer!?

Hailing from the Kingdom of Riven, this young man has great faith in his skills. Having washed his hands of his homeland after they became vassal to Edoras, he moved to affluent Sufrataljah to find his way. Unfortunately, so did hordes of others just like him, and while he wallowed away lost in the crowd, his homeland regained its independence.
Are you one of us too?
I don't feel like talking right now.
All right! Let's have ourselves\na match!
Sure you want this? All right, here!
I'd love to help, but I've got\na fresh new job coming in...
Huh? This? Sorry, no can do.

A bodyguard in the employ of Lord Scarabe. Long ago, the Detof tribe hunted the desert, slaying countless monsters. For this, they were known as the Sand Sentinels. Time has not been kind to these folk, however, and now they are forced to sell their skills to wealthy nobles to make ends meet.
What do you want to know?
I have nothing to tell you.
For the honor of the Sand Sentinels!
For the honor of the Sand Sentinels,\nI will aid you!
I cannot accept another job right now.
My sum wealth is but a handful\nof desert sand...

A bowyer of the Detof clan. Particular about her work and stubborn in general, she only bestows her bows on hunters who earn her respect. That being said, her bows are custom-made to mold to the wielder's hands, and arrows shot from them always fly true. Light, springy, and beautifully crafted, archers hold her work in great esteem.
What can I help you with?
Sorry, but I'm busy right now.
Come back after you've practiced\nsome more.
I've got nothing for you. Out.

An alcoholic journeyman who traveled from Valore in search of tasty brews. He spends his days drinking, drunk, or somewhere in between. Interestingly, rumor has it that any tavern that serves drinks to his fancy ends up making it big, so connoisseurs view him as some sort of inebriated god.
Nothin' better than a tasty drink!
I see, I see! I'll save the world...\nwith this drink!
Sure, sure! Go on, take whatever\nyou want! *hic*
*hic* Eh? Wha?\nNo, I'm not givin' you my drink!

A woman in search of her runaway husband. Though she seems quiet and soft-spoken at first, she is actually quite forceful and tried to control her husband's every move. Unfortunately, she hasn't realized this is exactly why he left, and believes instead it was because she didn't love him enough.
I don't have time for you.
I hope going with you will lead me\nto him.
Hehe. You're a sweet one, aren't you?
This is important to me.\nI can't give it away.

This beggar girl is always hungry. Luckily, her puppy dog eyes and kind folk see to it that she gets a steady stream of victuals. She actually gives most of the food she receives to the local cats, but that's a secret she keeps from her patrons.
Hey, I have a little request...
Hmm... No, I think I'll pass.

An explorer who travels the world in search of mysteries. Willing to brave fearsome forests and scale the steepest summits to unearth lost loot, this fearless soul is currently on the trail of a hidden hoard rumored to be in Sufrataljah.
I already have a mystery on my hands.
I'll solve your most perplexing puzzles!
I'm done with it, now. You may have it!
Sorry! I need to focus on\nthis riddle right now!
This may be an important clue.\nI'm sorry, but I cannot part with it.

A gardener who has served the ruling house of Sufrataljah for many a long year, maintaining the exquisite grounds of their estate. He believes he's accepted Lord Julian's passing as an unfortunate stroke of fate, and yet... Perhaps the reason he still putters in the garden is to await the coming of the town's next true master.
Did the garden catch your eye?
If you really need a gardener,\nI'd be pleased to help.
If you want this, it's yours.
I cannot trade for that sum.
A merchant from a distant snowy kingdom. He spent a fortune purchasing expensive curios, knowing that he was sure to find buyers in a city filled with so many wealthy folk.
Why not browse my wares while\nwe talk?
I doubt that'd be good for business.
Come now, that's not nearly enough.
I can't sell for that low.\nThis is business.

A wealthy noblewoman bedecked in glittering jewels. She's covered from head to toe with gems, and something about her bearing reminds one of a gilded shell—bright on the outside, hollow within. This gaudy exterior raises the question, too: is she trying to compensate for something, or does she really think this is a good look?
I see you've fallen for my beauty!
How dare you speak to me!
Oh! You are charming.\nFine, but only for a little while.
Ohohoho! If you desire this\npaltry thing, take it.
You must be joking! I'm too busy\nwith parties to follow you!
Too bad! I have no baubles\nto fling at you!

A young sentry tasked with guarding the palace. Dutiful to a fault, he has never once been late to work or dozed off on the job. The recent unrest in town has led him to begin questioning whether the ruling class is worth serving.
I don't like the look of you!\nBegone!
Guh... Very well. I'll obey your orders.
Guh... Very well. Take whatever\nyou like.
Hmph. You talk a good game,\nbut it's only talk.
Come back after you've trained\nsome more.
A gate sentry who has served the local rulers for many a season. His long experience with the job has taught him that sometimes you need to make compromises.
I'm afraid I can't help you.
I suppose I've got no choice.\nI'll help you.
I can't just abandon my duties.

A painter famous throughout the continent for her skill. Talented enough to accommodate any request in her field, she's earned great respect from the upper crust of society. She also sneaks in satirical elements that ridicule the hierarchy.
Interested in my paintings, are you?
I might not look it, but I'm pretty busy.
Interesting! I'm sure this will come\nin handy for my next piece!
If you'll model for me, it's a deal.
Sorry! I'm searching for inspiration\nfor my next piece.
I refuse to part with any material\nthat I can use to mix new paints.

A young man from the Ahdju clan in the Sunlands. His people are experts at finding underground water veins, digging waterways, growing grass, and raising livestock. He dreams of raising all sorts of different animals one day.
Tell me more about the land\nout there.
I'm in the middle of something\nright now.
I can't just leave my sheep here.

A man who specializes in mixing Sufrataljah's famous spices. The hottest mix he created has become a favorite of a certain syndicate member from Valore, who comes to buy the concoction in bulk. Ironically, he does not enjoy spicy foods and has never tasted his mixtures himself.
I might just find a new flavor!
If you like it, come back for more.
I can't imagine there's a better place\nout there.
I can tell you don't appreciate\nmy products.

A woman working at a tailor shop that sells clothes and cosmetics among other things. She was once a dancer, but dancing has become forbidden in Sufrataljah since the reign of Lord Scarabe. Though she quietly obeys the ruling, her body has yet to forget those cherished steps.
I don't have anything of use.
Did you think I couldn't dance?
I see that things are about to change.
Well, well. I guess you've earned this.
I'm not ready to leave this place\nbehind just yet.
You still have a ways to go.

A man in charge of gold-mining slaves. At first glance, he appears as cruel as other supervisors, but is actually a decent man, striking his slaves in a painless way and ensuring their meals are nutritious. Although employed by a noble, his conscience still remains.
All right, time for a break.
Sounds like working conditions'll\nbe better out there.
You're pretty tough.\nAll right, you can have it.
I'm not lookin' for a new job.
Got nothin' for you, I'm afraid.

An exhausted mother hen and her little chicks who refuse to listen to a word she says. They have no idea how dangerous it is to wander away from their mother, and by the time they realize their mistake it will be too late.
Ugh, you're fussier than my chicks!
This is a small village.\nOnce I spread some good\nrumors, it'll be right as rain.

The strongest woman in the village—a fact in which she takes tremendous pride. Word of her physical prowess has spread far and wide, inviting invitations of marriage from equally formidable men. However, the only invitations she cares to entertain are from challengers seeking to test their might against hers.
You want to try your hand against me,\ntoo? Then ready yourself!
That was a good match. Let me know\nwhen you're up for another.
That was a good match. Let me know\nwhen you're up for another.
I haven't seen you around before.\nI've got nothing to say to you.
I think I could learn a lot\nfighting alongside you!
We should spar again sometime!\nUntil then, take this.
A girl who lives with her grandfather. She has adored stories of his worldly travels ever since she was little and hopes to visit those places with him while he is yet able.
Do you have a story for me?
An old man who lives with his granddaughter. He enjoys telling her his vivid recollections of a "goddess of the spring" he met in his youth. He hopes to take her there someday.
You need to rethink your manners!
You need to rethink your manners!
Do you want to hear my story, too?
My stories are only for my granddaughter.
I'm just an old fool.\nI do hope I can be of help.

A proud son of Clearbrook, this fisherman reads the winds and the waters like an educated man his letters, and could bring home a king's bounty every day if he saw fit. Out of love for the river, however, he takes only what he needs and leaves that which he does not.
Hey! Didja think I wouldn't notice\nwhat yer tryin' to do!?
Hey! Didja think I wouldn't notice\nwhat yer tryin' to do!?
It looks like I'm the one\nwho's bein' reeled in...
Okay, I'll catch ya next time.

This culinarian uses only the finest local fish to make simple fare that is beloved by all. People are beginning to flock to his establishment every day to enjoy what some have called the true taste of Clearbrook.
I refuse to serve such churlish\ncustomers!
Welcome! What can I get you?
Well, are you satisfied with\nthis transaction?
Sorry, but I I can't leave.\nI love this village.

A devoted connoisseur of Magg's cuisine who has eaten every dish on the menu. No other fare can seem to satisfy his cravings, and in recent days he hardly ever eats at home, earning him many complaints from his family.
I guess I can tell you.
I'd rather keep it to myself.
Perfect. I'd rather not go home\nright now.

A spirited and sociable young resident of Clearbrook. Despite his perennial frown and endless string of complaints about his fellow villagers, it's no secret that in truth, he cares quite deeply about one and all.
Why would you ask for such a thing?
Why would you ask for such a thing?

A young new apothecary of Clearbrook, Zeph lost his parents to the Great Pestilence at an early age, and thereafter had to care for his sister, Nina, by himself. As she is his sole surviving family, he feels this responsibility keenly, and has resolved to place her welfare above his own.
I can't leave Nina on her own.
Would you lend me an ear?
I can be of some help.
I wish I could offer it to you for free,\nbut alas...

This old woman was carried to Clearbrook by the whim of fate, much like the waters of the river she watches over. Perhaps she hopes that, in time, it will cleanse her of a lifetime of sin—of banditry, of murder, of worse. Or perhaps she seeks only to live out her days in quiet damnation.
Do I know how to fight?\nWhy don't you come over\nhere and find out?
I think it's time somebody\ntaught you a lesson!
I think it's time somebody\ntaught you a lesson!
It isn't of much consequence, but...
Goodness, I'm afraid my memory\nescapes me.
I'm starting to like you.\nHere, you can have this.

A guard who keeps an eye out for bandit attacks, which have been on the rise with all the recent wars. He'd never admit as much, but he's concerned about the village's ability to defend itself, given that there are few able-bodied warriors here.
If you lost to me, then you clearly\nrequire more training.
If you lost to me, then you clearly\nrequire more training.
You need to be on guard.
It's more of a local problem, so...
You're incredibly strong.\nPlease, allow me to accompany you.
Thank you for that valuable experience.\nPlease, take this as thanks!
A woman who stares at the river with hazy eyes. She became a fisherwoman because she was born in a riverside village, but curses her lot in life and prays for a chance to leave her home behind.
Is there something worth doing...\nin your town?
No, I think I'll stay here for now.

A descendant of cattlemen who worked in Clearbrook for generations under the motto of "to raise cattle, understand their hearts." Though he looks after his herd with great care, he acts like a raging bull when it comes to people, which has caused him to miss out on countless business opportunities.
You got work for me, right?
Tryin' to shove me in a pen, are ya!?
A man who fishes for eelfish at the river, but has difficulty capturing the thin, slippery little buggers. He's constantly trying to think of an easier method to catch them.
Don't feel like giving that away.
Talking about it won't solve anything.
Eh, I'm not really in the mood.
After the Great Pestilence claimed her parents at an early age, this young girl was raised by her elder brother, Zeph, whom she loves and adores. She hopes to one day repay him for all he has done.
I can't tell you. It's a secret.

A cow who was lovingly raised on a ranch in Clearbrook. She believes humans to be her servants and constantly bombards them with self-indulgent requests, but they don't mind because they can't understand her.
Don't you know it's rude to stare!?
Shall I teach you some manners?
Moo... It wasn't supposed to be\nlike this.
You know what will happen if you\ndefy me, yes?
Some deeds can't be washed away.\nTake care out there.

An elderly apothecary who spent his years traveling the land, healing the sick and wounded. Several years ago he suffered a grave injury and lost the use of one of his arms, and so he set out for Donescu to settle into a quiet retirement. He'd only just put down roots when G'roha showed up, and now he is tormented by his inability to help those suffering around him.
This is a boring story, you know.
I'm not in the mood right now.
You think I can still help people?
What good can I do as a decrepit,\none-armed apothecary?

A merchant who traveled here anticipating a massive demand for goods now that the war has wound down. However, when confronted by the horrors left in the wake of the fighting, he resolved to share his merchandise with the unfortunate for free. Though he's got a sharp eye for commerce, he's well aware that his good nature is preventing him from making a fortune.
I see you're in a bind,\nso I'll help you out.
I'm pouring all my profits into\nthe reconstruction effort.
I can't leave this place. The people\nhere need the supplies I can provide.
Those with ample leaves shouldn't\nplay tight with their purse strings.

A boy who lost his family when the Westerlings attacked. With the town overrun and many of the adults wandering around in a daze, he realized the only one he could truly count on was himself. Spurred by tragedy, he resolved to become someone who could get by on wits alone.
You think you can dupe me!?\nWell, think again!

A third-generation resident of this town who worked an average job doing nothing out of the ordinary. He married a local girl and was busy raising kids when the war turned his everyday life upside-down. Grievously wounded and his family gone, he no longer has any idea of how to face his cruel new reality. How can one live after everything has been taken from them?
There's only misery left for me here,\nanyway...
I hope it comes in handy.
What? What will you take from me\nnext?
I have memories attached to this.\nI can't give it away.

An ex-soldier turned ferryman, he retired from the army when he realized he had no talent for fighting. He'd been ashamed of this decision until he began helping crowds of refugees escape the fighting by ferrying them to safety. The sight of him maneuvering his craft through the chaos of war, unbent and undaunted, gave courage to the terrified townsfolk.
You want to know what I'm doing?
Now's not the time for that.
And so I set sail on a new adventure...
I was just looking for someone\nto take this off my hands.
I will not give this up.

A fugitive who used the confusion of war to slip out of his jail cell. Though he is a hardened criminal who has committed every evil deed in the book, when he saw the carnage left upon the town, regret for his past misdeeds welled up from within him. He immediately set to work helping those in need, yet remains keenly aware that it will never absolve him of his sins.
I can't speak about this too loudly,\nbut...
I'll help. If you'll have me, that is.
I'd been hiding this on me for\na while, but...here, have it.
I've just made it outside.\nI don't have anything to give.

This old warhorse has been a mercenary for many a season and has the skills to show for it. Unfortunately, he feels restless without a bottle in hand, and so he spends his days and nights in whatever tavern will take him. He planned to wield his beloved axe against the Westerlings, but he always drinks himself under a table before he can get out the door.
What happened?\nThat's what I want to know!
Call me when you need my axe.\nI'll be drinkin' till then.
An axe's like a bottle of ale, see?\nDon't swing it 'round willy-nilly.
I won't lose to you. Not in a duel\nof strength, or a drinkin' contest.
Ahh, my head's clearin'. Cheap booze\njust won't do it anymore... *hic*

A fisherman who made a peaceful living catching fish in the river and selling them at the market. He lost his beloved wife in a battle with G'roha, and now spends his days standing amidst the ruins of his town. The only thing keeping him going is his hatred for the nation that destroyed his life.
I'll never forget what they did to us.
You're here to take what little's\nleft from me, aren't you!?
Oh. I see now. If I follow you,\nI can make those bastards pay!
I've lost it all now...\nForgive me, my love...
I won't give it to you!\nIf I do, I'll have nothing left...

A scholar recording the history of the war against G'roha and the fate of those left in its wake. The locals view him as compassionless for these efforts, but he is in fact driven by great empathy. He hopes that recording the horrors that unfolded here will prevent future generations from repeating the same mistakes. He grits his teeth when insults are flung his way and continues his thankless task.
You want to know how I can keep\nmy composure?
I have nothing to talk about.
I've learned more from history\nthan mere academic facts.
I have finished my research here.\nIt is time to move on to the next town.
If you truly want it, you may have it.
I learn from history so as not to\nrepeat the mistakes of the past.
Plunder follows war. That is merely\nthe way of things.

A compassionate woman born to a wealthy family. Though her home and fortune were untouched in the war, she was stricken by a sense of powerlessness when she saw the damage her town had endured. Discovering reserves in herself she didn't know she had, she wiped away her tears and stood up with fierce determination in her eyes. Her newfound goal is to grow strong so she can protect those she loves.
Might you lend me an ear?
I do not have time to spend on you\nat present.
I will fight, and I will fight...and I will\ngrow strong!
I will go with you to learn the ways\nof war.
I do not have the strength yet\nto fight...
I will not give this to you.

A young soldier of Donescu, he joined the army because he heard the pay was good. The problem is, he hates violence and thinks it'd be simpler for everyone to just surrender without a fight. He spent the entirety of the war against G'roha in hiding.
Go away. This isn't the time.
I said I don't wanna do this!
Finally, I can leave this town!\nGoodbye, Donescu!
I'm a soldier of Donescu.\nWe're a tough lot.
If you want it, you've got to\nshow me you're serious.

A soldier hailing from a nearby village, he's had the eyes of an eagle since he was a boy. Employed by the army as a lookout, he was the first to notice the G'roha battleship on the horizon, but even sharp eyes couldn't reveal just how fearsome a foe they would prove themselves to be.
I don't feel like speaking right now.
Kill on sight! That's army policy!
I'll accompany you to scout out\nthis new environment!
Lookouts are often first to fight!\nWe're no pushovers!

A weather-beaten soldier who has served Donescu since the reign of the previous king. Hardworking and loyal to a fault, King Dudek placed great trust in him, offering him a promotion, which he refused. This career soldier wished to remain a simple guard, protecting his town shoulder to shoulder with its people. Even now that Donescu has been ravaged by war, this desire remains unshaken.
I don't have time to talk to you.
Let me show you how a true son\nof Donescu fights!
I can't protect my city as I am...\nso I must see this as a chance to\nhone my skills.
I believe you will use this honorably.
You won't survive on the battlefield\nlike that!
Come back when you have\nmore steel in your spine.

A soldier possessed by rage. Overly confident in his skills, defeat at the hands of the Westerlings left him fuming and indignant. He believes that if he was only a little stronger, they could have achieved victory, and this frustration mercilessly drives him down the path of vengeance.
I guess I can tell you.
Don't act like you know me!
You lookin' to bleed too!? Huh!?
I'll follow you to the ends of\nthe earth!
This means everything to me...\nbut take it.
Damn... If only I had fought like this\nagainst the barbarians...
I can do it... I can do it...!

Young though she may be, Roberta is widely looked up to as the leader of the harlots of Donescu. She'll never abandon a friend and never back down from a fight—even from the most vicious thugs and ne'er-do-wells. Respected, admired, loved, and feared, she's matron to all under her care. From the shadows, she does her best to support the struggling women of the war-torn kingdom.
It's nothing to write home about.
Do you honestly think this is the time\nfor that?
I have a soft spot for tough people.\nSo, where to?
I'm impressed you've survived this long.
Fall in love with me yet?

A member of an organization that operates in Donescu's shadowy underbelly. Inability to conform to society's standards was what set him down the criminal path, but he loves his hometown as much as any upstanding citizen. When the Westerlings came, he gladly volunteered his specialized skills to aid those fleeing the violence. Now, he works hard alongside his fellows to restore the city.
Willing to listen to my gripes, eh?
I don't have anything to say to you.
I ain't gonna let an amateur like you\nmess with me.
Okay, okay, I get it, you're tougher\nthan me. I'll help you out.
I've gotta set a good example\nfor my underlings, y'know.
Our town's got one rule: might makes\nright. Been that way forever.

As a minstrel searching for a champion to extol, he wandered the continent until he finally stumbled into Donescu. There, he witnessed the gallant form of King Dudek courageously striding into almost certain death, and was struck with inspiration—here indeed was a hero worthy of song! Though the king perished, this bard remains, composing ballads to the king's nobility and strength.
Do you know why I sing?
Let us tell the world of King Dudek's\ngreat deeds! Hail King Dudek!
Here! Go, and remember the king's\nmighty deeds!
I'm trying to sing.\nPlease stop interfering.

A priest dispatched here from the cathedral in Saintsbridge; he bemoaned his assignment to Donescu as a demotion in all but name. The invasion of the Westerlings changed all that, however, and now he believes it was the Sacred Flame itself that guided him here. Since this holy revelation, he's thrown himself into his duties with relentless fervor.
May I confess something to you?
I understand. Let the Sacred Flame\nlight our path.
Please, take it with the blessing of\nthe Sacred Flame.
My purpose here is not yet complete.
No. This is precious to me.

A young girl living in a corner of town. Always off in her own little dreamland, she's had trouble making friends her own age and her parents are worried sick about her. The town may be falling apart around her, but even now she waits for the friend who once played with her to return.
Give this to my friend, okay?

An old woman dressed in rags who stumbles around town, shouting about calamities yet to come. This earned her sour looks and a poor reputation—until the armies of the G'roha appeared at their doorstep. Now, she's treated with awe, respect, and not an inconsiderable amount of fear.
Do you want to know what\nthe stars say?
It is not I, but the stars that speak.
The stars have shown me that\nI must go where you lead.
I foretold that this day would come!
The stars tell me not to follow!\nThey do!
The stars tell me that you cannot\nhave it! You cannot!

A lumberjack who resides in Donescu. Nothing makes him happier than seeing the lumber he felled being put to good use. He worked hard for the sake of his town, so seeing it instantly reduced to ashes in the war left a deep scar in his heart.
It seems I've still got some strength\nleft.
Go ahead. You can take it.
Would you mind leaving me alone?
I really don't feel like it.

A woman who adores animals, and none was more precious to her than her beloved dog growing up. Unfortunately, that same dog was killed by a panther while trying to protect her during G'roha's invasion. She is lost without her dearest companion at her side.
These are mementos of my dog...\nThey're too precious to give up...
I guess I don't need them...since\nmy dog isn't around anymore...
I can tell you about my dog...
Sorry, just thinking about it\nmakes me teary...
I'll keep living...for the sake of\nall animals.
I can't... My dog's spirit is here...
A rabbit that digs holes here, there, and everywhere. It quickly hides in said holes when it senses danger, which is how it has been able to survive despite being quite low in the food chain.
I hope there's some good dirt\nto dig up out there.
I can't leave this place. I already dug\nso many holes here...
Hehe. Do try a little harder next time.
A soldier who stands watch over Cragspear, the royal capital of Edoras. He is fully determined to carry out his duty—that is, the protection of the capital of the greatest kingdom in Orsterra.
You wish for a training bout?\nI'll not hold back!
That was good practice.\nLet's do it again sometime!
That was good practice.\nLet's do it again sometime!
Talking on the job is strictly prohibited!
I need to get stronger.\nLet me come along with you!\nIt will double as training!
I couldn't rival your skill. A promise\nis a promise—take what you wish.
A soldier who guards the road to Edoras. Entrusted with the safekeeping of the gates to the greatest kingdom in Orsterra, he is filled with determination.
I'm on duty, so I must refuse!
By all means—so long as this\ncooperation of ours can take place\noutside my normal working hours!
By all means!\nTake it with you!

A soldier who stands watch over Cragspear, the royal capital of the Kingdom of Edoras. He has been entrusted with watching over the slums of the greatest power in Orsterra—and evidently his enthusiasm can be bought.
Doesn't seem like you're making\nthis offer in good faith, y'know.
Doesn't seem like you're making\nthis offer in good faith, y'know.
If you only wanna chat, I guess I can\ndo that free of charge.
Heh, thanks for the business!\nI'll take a little vacation, I will!
Heh, thanks for the business!\nI've got plenty of expenses, y'know?

A priest who always preaches love. In this war-torn place, there live a great many people who have experienced terrible sorrow. He, in fact, was once one of them, and became a priest only after losing his wife and children.
Just because one desires love\ndoes not mean they will receive it...
Just because one desires love\ndoes not mean they will receive it...
Will you lend me an ear?
I cannot bring myself to speak\nof it yet. Please, forgive me.
I shall love you, your companions,\nand even your foes!
We have love to thank for this.

A noble who was friends with the child of a relative of the royal family of Edoras. He spent his own funds to convince a distant relation to include him in their aristocratic circle so that he could finally attain noble status.
I beg your pardon!\nFor little more than pocket change!?
I beg your pardon!\nFor little more than pocket change!?
Do you want to hear how I went\nfrom rags to riches?
You're awfully brazen for a mere\ncommoner.
Such is the duty of the genteel\naristocrat. I shall lend you my aid.
An agreeable price!\nYes, such is the privilege of\na noble with assets to spare.
A man who lives next to a bar in Cragspear because it's cheap. He can't drink alcohol himself, and the smell alone makes him dizzy. He wants to move, but doesn't have the money.
Don't joke around like that!\nNow I feel even worse.
You know what I can't stand?
Thank you! I needed some money\nfor my medicine.
I'll go anywhere, so long as it's far\nfrom the scent of booze.
I'll bet there's a tavern in your town\ntoo...

A woman on the hill studying birdians. Her interest stems from an incident in which a birdian saved her from a monster targeting her. She hopes to one day be able to speak to and make friends with a birdian, and perhaps be granted the honor of flying upon its back.
I've adopted birdian movements\ninto my fighting style. Now you get\nto help me test it out!
That wasn't enough!\nMy research demands more testing!\nCome back soon!
That wasn't enough!\nMy research demands more testing!\nCome back soon!
Are you interested in birdians, too?
I'm sorry, but I'm busy observing\nbirdians right now.
You really can't discount the value\nof fieldwork! I'll fight with you and\nkeep my research moving forward!
My technique's not yet ready\nfor practical applications, I see.\nI appreciate your cooperation in testing it.

The head of a band of thieves who looks to be an ordinary citizen of the kingdom. The former leader of his band taught him that the essence of thievery is to always go unnoticed, and so he stole a position as a citizen of Edoras.
Something you want to know about\na regular old citizen like me?
There's nothing I can tell you as\nan ordinary citizen of this country.
You're saying it's a citizen's duty?\nWhy you damned dirty...er, I mean,\nyeah, here.
I hope it's an ordinary town for\nan ordinary citizen like me!
No thanks. I'm a citizen of Cragspear.

A merchant from Victors Hollow, a town known for its arena. He came here seeking a gladiator, and can be rather pushy in his negotiating. Or at least, he could, until he was almost hauled off for trying to recruit soldiers of Edoras, after which he has exercised a bit more caution.
Such cajoling isn't gonna work on me!
Such cajoling isn't gonna work on me!
Have I got a deal for you!
Let's see your strength up close\nand personal!
No skin off my back, really. I'll recoup\nmy losses in the arena before long.
One time I saw a fish jump out of this really shiny river! It was so pretty! My dad actually caught the fish...and it was really tasty.
No, that's mine! Don't take it!
You wanna know more about fish?
I'm not s'posed to talk to strangers.
You need this? Okay then, you can\nhave it! Take good care of it, okay?

A woman who serves an Edoras noble of moderate prestige. Their manor is a modest size, and her master does not demand luxurious foods. They hold no parties so she need not worry about the busy evenings that result from such events, and her wages are acceptable, thus she has no real complaints about the situation—except, perhaps, an excess of free time.
I could lend an ear. I have some\ntime on my hands.
I may not look it, but I am <i>very</> busy.
I'd happily part with this if you\ndesire it so.
You know a place better than this?\nI must admit, I am...interested.

A man who once led soldiers in the Edoras army. He is not an especially gifted fighter, but his mild demeanor and broad knowledge of tactics made him a trusted leader. However, he was badly injured in the rear guard during a retreat, which paralyzed him and forced him into retirement. He is greatly missed by his subordinates.
No, I can't part with this.
You really think I'd be of help?
I don't think I'd be of much use to you...
Here, make good use of it.\nI hope it comes in handy.
Could I really get treated in\nyour town?
My old wounds still hurt.\nI don't think I could go very far...

A merchant who practically stares a hole through his customers as he speaks, ever eyeing his patrons carefully. He wonders what they think, and whether they can be trusted. He only does business with them once he can figure those things out. Despite being a respectable merchant, the staring seems to drive many customers away.
...Your face tells me that\nyou're raring to go!
...Thought you could kick a merchant\naround no problem, didn't you?
...Thought you could kick a merchant\naround no problem, didn't you?
Yes... You have a trustworthy look\nabout you.
You don't seem like an honest sort.
...Out of respect for that face of yours,\nI shall aid you!
...Those are hungry little eyes you've got.\nAh, just pulling your leg! I'll give it to you.

A young man with a nonchalant air about him who lives a quiet and comfortable life. He runs a weapons shop, which has thrived in the Cliftlands for generations, and thus he lives without inconvenience. However, his business is based on reselling goods diverted from the army on the black market. Households other than his own are all very worried.
Bless your heart if you think you can\nbest me in a business negotiation.
Bless your heart if you think you can\nbest me in a business negotiation.
Please don't bother me while\nI'm drinking my coffee.
You wish to hire me!? You have pluck!\nVery well, I'll come along with you.
That's a good buy.\nYou've got promise, you do.

A woman who believes that those born into poverty in Edoras have few human rights. She also thinks that paupers like herself will never leave the slums, much less live in the capital, though that is not actually the case. However, sulking keeps her from having to think too much about the truth.
I can't believe you'd challenge\nsomeone like me...
Did you think I'd be a pushover?\nYou're just as pathetic as me...
Did you think I'd be a pushover?\nYou're just as pathetic as me...
I don't wanna hear it, and I'm not\nin the mood to talk.
You want me to help you fight?\nThat's fine with me.

A man from the slums who is deep in debt. What little money he earns is claimed by the debt collector who has come to live at his home. He spends his days thinking he'd be better off dead, but lives for the delicious food his debt collector makes from time to time.
Come on, please give me a little more!\nI can't survive on this!
Come on, please give me a little more!\nI can't survive on this!
There's nothing you can do,\nso leave it alone.
I'll sneak out a bit later. Keep me hired\nas long as you can, please!
Thank you! Thank you!\nI can live another day now!

A woman who lent a man in the slums money at a predatory rate. She claims what little income he earns, keeping him trapped in perpetual poverty until the day he finally dies. She sometimes cooks for him purely because she needs him to stay alive.
I'll show you what a terror I can be!
You see what you get when you\ndisobey me!?
You see what you get when you\ndisobey me!?
You got somethin' you wanna say?
Quit pokin' your nose into other\npeople's business!
I...I just went easy on ya! I'll talk to\nyou outside, so wait fer me there!
...Yer not half bad. Not that I was\ntakin' it totally seriously, of course,\nbut still.

A woman who leads a modest life. She lost her husband in a coal mining accident, but she is able to maintain her humble yet healthy lifestyle thanks to the money he left her. But...when did he even make all that money, she wonders.
I'm sorry, but I must refuse.
I'm sorry, but I must refuse.
I don't have anything particularly\ninteresting to talk about, but...
Sorry, but I'm thrifty with my words,\nas well.
If you insist, then I will help as much\nas I am able.
If you insist, then I shall give you\nwhat you ask.

A supportive young boy who earns money to help his single mother. He poured all the money his father left behind into buying up stocks of precious minerals in Edoras. Once market prices get driven up, he sells it all off, then uses the profits to buy up even more.
Thought you could write me off\nbecause I'm a kid? Think again!
Okay, but don't tell my mom.
I'll do this for you 'cause you're special!\nCall it an investment in the future!
A man who makes use of his stout body for his job—which is to take literal beatings. He earns money by letting stressed miners beat on him.
Oh, we doin' this? Hit me all you want!
I did it again... I'm real sorry, just\nforget this happened!
Doesn't look like you need my services.
Oh, that felt great!\nHere, take it, on the house.
Woo! All that pain for all this gain.
Sorry. Can't leave town while\nmy services are still needed.
Man Who Insists He Lives in the Capital

This man left home swearing he'd make it big, but work didn't go well for him and now he resides in the slums. On the occasion he runs into a friend from home, he makes little boasts like, "Oh, I live in the capital, just not in a particularly big place!" and so forth. Living in the capital is perhaps the only thing propping up his self-esteem.
What!? Don't treat me like a fool!
What!? Don't treat me like a fool!
I can tell you everything there is to know\nabout the capital.
A country bumpkin like you wouldn't\nunderstand.
I suppose it wouldn't be a bad idea to\nget out of the city every now and then.
All right then! On behalf of\nall the citizens of the capital,\nI'll give it to you.

The Kingdom of Edoras has done well for itself in the gold mining industry for a long time due to the fact that much of the raw material for the leaf currency used in Orsterra is mined in the Cliftlands. Upon learning that, this coal miner came here hoping to hit it big. Pure as he is though, he'll likely end up hoodwinked by some unscrupulous local lord at some point.
That wouldn't even cover a single dig!
That wouldn't even cover a single dig!
The secret to success is predicting\nthe course of the future.
I can't go handing out my nuggets\nof wisdom to just anybody.
I need the money, so fine.\nI'll stick with you till I strike it rich!
It's fine, I guess. I'm gonna be rich\nbefore long anyway.

The strongest of the poor in the slums. He is a veteran and a hero who once served as one of Edoras's generals. He couldn't keep up with King Pardis III's warring way of rule and conceded his position to the young Mahrez.
I'll put your merit to the test\nand see if you have what it takes\nto be the bearer of a new era!
Work on your skills and your mind.\nI'll take you on again so long as life\nflows through me.
Work on your skills and your mind.\nI'll take you on again so long as life\nflows through me.
Will you listen to an old man's tale?
There's no point in talking about it now.
Superbly fought! What life of mine\nremains, I entrust now to you.
Superb! I think I can safely entrust this\nto you.
A man trying to improve living conditions for the poor by inciting a coup in Edoras. He admires the way his brother lives in Bolderfall, and became a leader in the slum district of the royal capital.
Each one of us has something\nwe need to do...
Each one of us has something\nwe need to do...
Let us band together for the greater\ngood!
Very well. I'll work with you.
All right then!\nI hope I can be of service!

A writer who adores the slums. It is a place of hunger, illness, envy, jealousy, despair, and sorrow—but also the occasional smile. This, he believes, is the essence of humanity at the heart of oppression.
Hmm... I like the cut of your jib.
I'm sure traveling with you will have me\npositively bursting with ideas!
I would like you to experience the tales\nmy possessions have to tell for yourself!

A merchant who maintains his self-esteem by helping the poor. He unfortunately has a habit of browbeating and insulting them while he does so, but he's still quite generous with handing out money and essentials to the needy.
I'll have to pass.\nIt's not a great deal for me.
I'll have to pass.\nIt's not a great deal for me.
I assume you're paying me for this?
I'm not saying a word unless I'm paid.
Seems like a good chance to make some\nnew discoveries. I'll come with you.
That's a reasonable price.\nHappy to make a good deal.

Once upon a time, a loving family who lived in the slums was brutally murdered and their house set ablaze. This woman bore witness to these events, and the sight haunts her to this day. In her nightmares, she desperately calls for help, but no one hears.
Please, I have to tell someone.
