It's too difficult for me to speak of it.

A soldier of Edoras who volunteered for the Western expedition, hoping to improve his lot in life. However, he was relegated to providing support from the rear when his kindness left him unable to strike down enemies. Realizing he cannot get ahead in a country that values military merit, he is now looking for a chance to leave the army.
I take it you're stronger than me,\nof course.
I can't give something so precious\nto a soldier weaker than myself.
Are you sure you want to ask <i>me</>?
I wouldn't be much help, I'm afraid.
Be sure you have enough supplies.
I would be happy to provide support\nto your town.
I still have duties to attend to here.
A tavern maid in Cragspear whose constant smile soothes the hearts of patron soldiers. Rumor has it that she's recently learned the recipe for a certain pie popular in the slums.
That's not for sale, I'm afraid.
I've got some time to spare.
Sorry, but now's not the time.
It's not on the menu, but...\nIf it's what the customer wants.
That's not a service we offer here.
A woman who fell into poverty along with her young child after losing her husband in the war. She's starting to rebuild her life at long last, now that Alaune has ascended to the throne.
I finally have some peace of mind.
I don't have a moment to rest.

An apothecary who attends to the needs of the destitute at no cost, causing the people to revere him like a god incarnate. In truth, he is a member of the Donescu royal family, but he left the castle behind to study medicine.
A chef who makes aesthetically pleasing food. To him, appearance is primary, and flavor secondary. His omelettes are truly a beauty to behold.
I have work to do. I can't just drop\neverything and follow you.
Do you want to know the secret\nto my art?
Sorry, but I've got no room in my head\nfor anything but classy looking cuisine.
A soldier guarding the gates of Castle Edoras. There aren't many foes who would attack the gates from the rear, but he has nonetheless shut the "gates" of his mind up tight.
Apologies, but I am duty bound\nto protect this gate. I cannot grant\nmy trust so lightly.
Apologies, but I am duty bound\nto protect this gate. I cannot grant\nmy trust so lightly.
May I have a word with you?
You're clearly not to be trusted!\nLeave here at once!
...If I'm to be honest, work is pretty\nuneventful. I'd be glad to cooperate\nwith you.

A brawny but arrogant man with his sights set on the castle. He is constantly trying to evaluate his prospects: "The army of Edoras is said to be the strongest in all Orsterra. I wonder if they can provide the pay and standing that I deserve. Maybe they can? Maybe not? Hmm... I wonder."
I'll be expecting a fight worth my time.
I can't follow someone so weak.
It'll take more than that to beat me.
I'd be happy to hear you out.
We are evenly matched.\nFine then, you may call upon\nme when the need arises.
We are evenly matched.\nFine then, this is yours.

A scholar of geology who came to research the various landforms of the Cliftlands that have developed over ages of natural processes. He has sought permission to investigate them from the Kingdom of Edoras for years, but it seems that every place he wants to look at is the site of some new fortress—or so he is told.
Sediment isn't the only thing that\nbuilds up over time, you know!\nI'll show you the power of knowledge!
Sorry, I'm busy with my research.
I can't give this to you.
Will you listen to my tale of woe?
A journey to discover new miracles\nof geology... Hmm, that might not\nbe so bad.
An aggressive alpaca feared as the tyrant of Cragspear Pass. Though it may look peaceful while chewing on grass, approaching carelessly will earn any passersby a swift kick off the cliff.
I'm gonna pretend I didn't see ya.\nNow scram.

This veteran formerly supported King Pardis III, though that was more out of a desire to stick to what he thought would be the winning side rather than out of any true loyalty to the king. Alaune graciously forgave him all the same, gaining his full admiration in the process.
I'm not at liberty to leave the castle.
I cannot allow you to use that.
You wish to speak with me?
Perhaps you shouldn't stick your nose\nwhere it doesn't belong.
I trust you'll make good use of this.

A maid who served during the end of King Pardis III's reign, first under Elrica and now as chief maid. She used to live in fear of being decapitated without warning, but things have improved tremendously since Alaune took the throne. She takes great pride in her work, and greets guests warmly.
Maid work requires its fair share\nof muscle, I'll have you know!
But who would do the cleaning\nif I were to leave?
If it's something I can speak about,\nthen certainly.
I should have known you'd best me.\nI'll help in whatever way I can.
I should have known you'd best me.\nPlease, take this.

A young soldier who has served Alaune since she was a princess. He once charged Krauser head-on in battle and was easily batted aside, but miraculously lived to tell the tale. Since then, he has convinced himself that Krauser bears a grudge against him, but in truth Krauser is impressed by the man's bravery.
I vow to be of service!

The minister in charge of Edoras's finances. Since Alaune has taken the throne, more of the nation's expenses have gone to tackling issues such as poverty. He understands that supporting those in need is invaluable to creating a better nation and supports the queen wholeheartedly.
Those are my personal funds, so...
Let us speak about things of great\nconsequence.
I will entrust it to you.

Though he has been prime minister since the days of King Pardis III, he lacks decisiveness and does not have what it takes to be a leader. On the other hand, he is very good at enacting policies, as long as they are given to him.
I am preoccupied at the moment.

A soldier devoted to maintaining equipment. Though he has no confidence in his abilities, he greatly desires to be helpful. He spends his days fervently tending to everyone's equipment. He's constantly eating nuts in an attempt to grow stronger, but he's yet to see any results.
I'm sorry, but I cannot leave my post.
Sorry, but I'm on the job right now.
By your command!\nI will do my utmost.
By your command!\nI hope it's useful.

An assertive young man who enjoys chasing after the latest fad. He's developed an interest in politics after observing the recent turmoil of the Altinian soldiers. However, it is doubtful that this fascination will last, and so he only pretends to be wise in such matters.
Have you come to invade us!?
Whoa... What?\nWas that the best you got?
Whoa... What?\nWas that the best you got?
This isn't the time for that!
I...I don't want to be a prisoner!\nI'll do whatever you say, promise!
Th-that was a strategic loss!\nHere, take this as compensation.

A kindhearted old man. He has a younger sister in Cobbleston who was widowed. He greatly enjoys hearing about his nephew through his correspondence with her, and hopes the boy might find a good father figure.
I couldn't possibly be of benefit to you.
I cannot offer that to you.
Will you hear an old man out?
Speaking of it won't change anything.
I hope I can benefit you in some way...
A carpenter who mostly conducts repairs. He takes pride in his work, believing it to be a way of maintaining Shepherds Rock's traditional architecture.
Are you trying to rip me off!?
Not in the mood to talk.
If it's repairs you need, you've come\nto the right guy!
I've got my hands full here.\nNo time to be traveling!

An old man who boasts that he would be nothing without vegetables. He believes that those grown in Cobbleston are particularly nutritious and insists that his son's wife include them in every meal. Though he understands how much of a burden this puts on her, he continues to badger her anyway, thus continuing to subsist on vegetables.
You care to test yourself against\nthis vegetable-enhanced physique?
I have no room in my mind for\nanything but vegetables.
Very well. I will show you the\nhidden power of vegetables.
A cleric who speaks in a rather circuitous manner. Though in truth he loves peace and constantly prays for others' well-being, he's often misunderstood because of his digressions.
The fact that I do not need to work\nproves that the world is at peace.
Avariciousness will only come\nback to harm you in the end.
Sure, I can give you a moment of my time.
I don't have time to spare.
I receive donations, and peace\nis brought about. Splendid.
May the leaves I have gained\nbecome someone else's wealth.

An old woman who is always standing around talking. In fact, she has developed such a wide circle of acquaintances through all her gabbing that the moment something reaches her ears it immediately becomes known throughout the town.
Lay a hand on me, and I'll have\na crowd gathered in an instant.
How can you get any juicy tidbits\nwith that sorry display of strength?
How can you get any juicy tidbits\nwith that sorry display of strength?
Ugh, go away! Can't you see\nI'm talking to someone!?
I might hear some interesting\ntales if I go along with you.
A housewife who loves to stand around talking. She was once a fierce warrior feared even by the soldiers of Altinia, but she has since traded in her spear for a broom and lives a life of peace.
Keep that up and I'll acquaint you\nwith the business end of my broom.
Keep that up and I'll acquaint you\nwith the business end of my broom.
It's rude to butt in on people's\nconversations!
I suppose I could take up my spear,\nfor old time's sake.

A gardener who was hired just recently. Her work ethic has impressed not only her employer, but the townspeople as well. In truth, she had intended to steal her employer's fortune once she had earned enough trust, but now is unsure of what course to take after discovering she has an aptitude for gardening.
This wouldn't be my first rumble.\nBring it!
Oh. I'd expected more of a fight.
Oh. I'd expected more of a fight.
Ha, that was a good change of pace!\nLookin' forward to working with you.
Go on and take it. Not like a gardener\nhas any use for it anyway.
A vigilant Altinian soldier who takes his job seriously. His stern personality is well evidenced by the constant gleam of his equipment.
You wish to spar?\nThen take up arms!
I have emerged victorious.\nI look forward to our next duel.
I have emerged victorious.\nI look forward to our next duel.
I will answer any questions\nyou might have!
Sorry, but I'm working right now.\nPerhaps later!
That was a valuable experience.\nI will aid you in your efforts!
That was a valuable experience.\nPlease take this in return!

A boy who says no to everything. He grew into his stubbornness after his mother once told him he had a hard head, though he mistook her meaning. His rock-like skull serves him well as a finishing attack in any scuffle he gets into.
Don't treat me like a kid!

A young man who was once set on becoming a knight. His efforts earned him the favor of a noble, but his patron died in battle before he could be instated. Having lost both his dreams and any status he had claim to, he now wanders the streets carrying the coin his patron bestowed upon him.
You wish to spar? En garde!
Perhaps you should work harder\nat honing your skills.
We can talk, if you wish...
I prefer to keep my memories close.
I admit defeat. Please, take this\nwith you.
I will teach you everything I learned\nto be a knight.
I cannot say that I am ready just yet.
It's all right.\nI've cleared up any\nmisunderstandings.

An Altinian soldier with good humor. His pleasant demeanor has earned him many gifts from the townspeople, and he's constantly munching on something. At times he wonders if he's too relaxed, but then he remembers that there are plenty of others who are too tightly wound.
Nope. I'm not in the mood.
Want to take a break with me?
I'm expecting to get a nice meal\nout of this!
If you insist.\nI can't very well say no.

A gregarious antique dealer who travels from land to land. He's taken up the profession in the place of his sickly parents. Unfortunately, he doesn't have much sense for the value of things and will buy anything if someone presses him hard enough. And yet strangely untold treasures seem to find their way to him.
I'm sorry, I can't let you have that.
You seem to have a keen eye.
I'm a little busy right now...
I was having trouble selling that,\nso you're welcome to it.
I have a hard time saying "no"\nif someone needs me...
I'm very sorry, but I have work to do...

A merchant who makes a living selling iron. He has a keen eye for good quality iron mined from the nearby town of Stonegard. Because he must navigate perilous mountain roads to buy his wares, he is quite confident in his martial prowess.
Don't make light of me!\nI've weathered countless\nroads fraught with danger!
You're like a hunk of scrap iron.\nTry again when you've refined your skills!
You're like a hunk of scrap iron.\nTry again when you've refined your skills!
Do you have a good deal for me?
You're as strong as wrought iron, I'd wager.\nI'll gladly follow you!
You're as strong as wrought iron, I'd wager.\nYou're more than welcome to this!

A calm and collected soldier who inherited his love of learning from his scholar father. He later decided to enlist in the army to protect the Kingdom of Altinia, but he continues his studies to this day, believing that his wit may one day earn him a place in government.
Listen to my words of wisdom.
Yes, I would not mind aiding you.
Yes, I would not mind aiding you.

A young man who lives for going to see plays. He adores Auguste's works in particular, and even considered moving to Theatropolis at one point. Auguste's death affected him greatly, but his spirits recovered upon meeting a marvelous young woman.
You're a friend of the Chosen One?\nThat's quite the claim to fame.
I have to show my girlfriend\nhow generous I am!

A dancer with a dangerous allure about her. Her sweetheart was a syndicate member, and so she has survived more than one brush with death. The experience has lent a heartrending air to her performances.
If it's a dance of death you want,\nI'll certainly oblige!
Over already?\nPerhaps some other night, then.
Over already?\nPerhaps some other night, then.
I'm a little curious about something...
Remember me whenever\nyou look upon this.

A former vassal who fled from Hornburg after its collapse. While he was of no high rank, his love for his country was deep, and even now he is ashamed of his actions. He is now conducting inquiries into the uprising that ruined the kingdom, compiling his findings in a book.
How dare you try to take\nadvantage of me!
How dare you try to take\nadvantage of me!
Will you listen to my tale?
I have nothing I can talk about.
I will do my utmost to earn\nmy keep!
I have no other choice if\nI am to earn my daily bread.

Her petite frame belies her fierce swordplay. That, combined with the magnificent achievements she won despite her lowly status, has earned her the title of the Raging Bird. Rumors abound as to whether she stands on par with the great heroes of Hornburg, or if she is in fact the youngest daughter of a noble family, but all inquiries into her circumstances dissolve in the face of her smile.
Isn't Raging Bird just\nthe loveliest title?
If you can overcome this bitter loss,\nI would gladly cross blades with you\nagain.
If you can overcome this bitter loss,\nI would gladly cross blades with you\nagain.
Is there something you'd like\nto know?
Don't you know it's rude to pry?
Perhaps I could hone my skills\nfurther if I travel with you.
Yes, you are worthy of receiving\nthis sword. Please, take it with you.

A man who appears to be a merchant, but isn't doing any trading. Little wonder, considering he's a spy from the Kingdom of Edoras. However, he's taken a liking to the climate of the Highlands, and is slowly being won over by King Solon's gallantry.
I'll come with you, but don't drag me\ninto any wars.
Sure, I don't mind. It's not like\nI was trying to sell it anyway.

A young man who had been training to be a hunter, but washed his hands of that after his hearing deteriorated, settling on his current profession. His experience as a hunter has helped him greatly as a carpenter.
Hey, just because I'm hard of hearing\ndoesn't mean you can cheat me!
Hey, just because I'm hard of hearing\ndoesn't mean you can cheat me!
Hm? What is it you want to know?
I can't hear you! Speak up!
Time to put my hunting skills to use!
You drive a hard bargain!\nTake it then, swindler!

A former merchant who traveled across the Coastlands's sea to hawk his wares. His honeyed words and ship-handling were on par with that of a pirate's until he was finally convinced to become one. He rented his house to Karan and Carabell while at sea, but now he's retired and enjoys a quiet life in town.
You can't hope to persuade\nanyone like that!
You can't hope to persuade\nanyone like that!
I can tell you a tale or two.
Leave me be. I want to relax in\npeace and quiet.
It's been quite some time since\nI last served under someone!
Hmm. I suppose I'll give you\na passing grade.
A young acrobat who hails from the Cliftlands. He was greatly heartened when King Solon once happened to wander by and praise his act, and so he now performs largely in the Highlands.
I've seen my fair share of fights!
Hahaha, were you struck by\nthe beauty of my techniques?
Hahaha, were you struck by\nthe beauty of my techniques?
Come see what I can do!
I am a traveling entertainer, after all.\nI will gladly follow you to new lands!
I'll relinquish the tools of my trade.\nTake good care of them!

A young girl who has great confidence in her artistic skills, claiming to be much better than the other children. She does indeed hold remarkable talent despite her years, and is particularly good at landscapes.
I won't give anything to someone\nwho won't appreciate it!
You adore my paintings, don't you?\nI can tell.
You'll buy it from me!? Then you'd\nbetter hang it up somewhere nice!

A merchant who greatly admires Wise King Solon. He wears the same turban the king uses to disguise himself and spends his time coming up with proverbs about sheep. Though he may seem odd, his skills as a merchant are second to none.
None can escape my keen gaze.
This is a quality item.\nYou have my word.
A brave new land, is it?
Your words could not lead a sheep\nout of its pen.

One of the most experienced stonemasons in Shepherds Rock. He specializes in paving mountain roads and his work is trusted by all who traverse those paths. Even in his old age, he is still eager to take on new architectural projects.
I'll use these rocks against ya,\nso help me!
Better take good care of that!
Buildin' a new town, are ya?\nLeave the masonry to me!

A researcher of outdoor cultivation whose delicious vegetables are the talk of the town. She lives in the Highlands with her father-in-law who is so fond of her crops that he pesters her for them at every meal.
I don't think you could truly\nappreciate these.
I'll help your town thrive,\nfrom the soil up!
There are still things I'd like to grow\nin the soil here.
A sheep who once led the herd, but lost their trust after failing to beat the sheepdog in battle. Now it is nothing more than a fellow member of the herd, meekly falling in line.
It was a long time ago...
I have no excuses to make.
Sure, might be good to get\na fresh start.
Take it. I don't need it anymore.
I don't think I have it in me to\ntravel to new places.
Are you trying to steal everything\nfrom me?
An orphan living in Berecain. Ever since his parents left town, he has eked a living unearthing relics from old Bernstein—though their true value is unknown to him.
I'm not budging, not one single leaf.

A descendant of one who served at the Forbidden Temple, a monument to the sealed god that played host to foul magical experiments. She believes the old kingdom fell in retribution for its blasphemy against the gods and accepts her lot.
If you are wise, you will keep\nyour distance...
Fine. If you are to insist, then I will\naccompany you.
A cursed thing, from a cursed land.\nBe ready for what it may invite.
I cannot give this to you.\nI must not.

A treasure hunter making his way around Orsterra. He's managed such feats as raiding the Finesand and Fort Wold Ruins alone. Though he traveled from afar to explore the ruins of Bernstein, he has already gotten sick of all the ash and fire.
I've got no business with you.
I bet I'd see some pretty good stuff\nif I tagged along with you.
You've got quite an eye! It's all yours.
I don't think so. I'm just not feeling\nthe pull of adventure with you.
Gonna have to open your wallet\na little wider.

The village chief of Berecain and descendant of Jennish, the last of Bernstein's kings. She served as keeper of Salomon's journal, which held the true history of the kingdom. While relieved to be released from that duty, she is also tormented by a sense of loss.
So the time has finally come...
I'll show them what a Bernstein royal\ncan do with a staff.
Perhaps the world is worth wielding\nthis accursed skill of mine...
Put it to good use. Do so for the world.
Return to me when you're ready.
A descendant of a Bernstein scholar of magic who devotes his time exploring the arcane for the sake of peace. He wishes to atone for the sins of his forefathers who drove the kingdom to its ruin.
I am not here to give presentations.
Allow me to demonstrate what\nmy research has wrought.
I will journey with you, to atone\nfor the sins of my forefather.
I can tell you would put my research\nto good and proper use.
And now we both know that\nmy research is top tier.
I could not part with something so vital.

Guards the Berecain village chief's residence. Descended from a knight to Bernstein royalty, his family has protected the chief since the kingdom's fall. He thought the children's song a fairy tale until he laid eyes upon Ceraphina.
Is there something you wish to know?
I have nothing to tell you...
Very well, then. My strength is yours.
I suppose I can trust you with it.
My place is here, protecting the chief.\nI cannot leave.

A child of Berecain who loves to sing the local children's song, though he doesn't quite understand what it's about. He's sick of his town that has nothing but rocks and lava and yearns to leave like the others.

A descendant of the Bernstein survivors. The land and its people were cursed when the kingdom fell, burdened with their past sins. However, this man has toiled hard to restore the church to its former glory.
I don't have many years left.\nI'll be glad to see it be of use.
I would like it if you were to make use\nof this.
I'll die with the church.\nLeave me in peace.
You can't take anything from\nthis place.

An old woman who knows much about the Kingdom of Bernstein's history. People come from all over to hear her tales. There was one young man who was oddly keen to hear about King Jennish. He claimed that he was an aspiring playwright...
If you don't mind listening to\nthe ramblings of an old woman...
I have nothing to share with you.
You think my knowledge could be\nof use?
If you insist, please take it.
I can't give that to you.

A Knight Ardante of the Flamesgrace Cathedral, and a pious soul who views difficult missions as trials of the Flamebringer. He devotes himself to his duties of guarding the road from monsters and promoting communication between the town and the outside world.
Of course! What would you like\nto know?
I'm afraid I can't share anything\nwith you.
Take it. And may the Sacred Flame\never shine its warmth upon you.
Impossible. I have a duty to uphold!
Impossible. That was given to me\nby my captain.

The descendant of slaves used as test subjects for magic research when the kingdom still stood. She claims to have inherited a great, forbidden magic from her ancestors, but cannot conduct the ritual because she lacks key items. She picks up random objects from who-knows-where to use for who-knows-what.
Hyeh hyeh hyeh... Curious, are you?
Will I be free of this place...?
You might get some use out of this.
I can't leave until I fulfill the wishes\nof my ancestors.

A stray cat living a carefree life. The volcano keeps the desolate land nice and warm all year round, making it a perfect habitat for a cat. He wonders why humans fail to choose a free and easy life like his.
You're not from around here.\nYou wouldn't understand.
You want this? Then it's yours.
Maybe dying will teach you a lesson,\nhuh?

[Cause of death: Slain in battle]\nAn Edoras soldier from the reign of Pardis III. He defended the town with unfailing loyalty, but was accidentally slain by a friendly blade in the chaos of the invasion by Alaune's revolutionary army.
I can't give this to you...
If it is to protect Edoras...
[Cause of death: Execution]\nA gatekeeper at Cragspear. He was beheaded for being too slow to open the gate for King Pardis III on the latter's return from a campaign late one night.
I cannot give this to you...
The changing of the guard...?\nAt last...

[Cause of death: Execution]\nA devotee of the Order of the Sacred Flame who visited the church daily. To save a dying child in the slums, she stole a portion of the funds meant to be donated to Edoras. When the theft was discovered, she was captured and executed.
I cannot let you have this...
Death was not my rightful lot...
I cannot render aid to you...
Could this be the guidance of\nthe Sacred Flame...?
[Cause of death: Unknown]\nAppears to have been a guard in Cragspear, but communicates only in unintelligible groans.
My authority is <i>not</> to be questioned!!!

[Cause of death: Assault]\nA former Cragspear guard. The finest swordsman in his hometown, he entered the service of Edoras where General Mahrez's awesome might shook him to the core. Changed by the experience, he set about oppressing the people with what little power he had. He met his end suddenly, at the hands of those he had tormented.

[Cause of death: Illness]\nDaughter of one of the greatest noble houses in Edoras. Her father's opposition to the war on the Westerlings displeased King Pardis III, and the family was stripped of its wealth, rank, and everything else. Tried her best to survive by any means necessary, but her efforts won her only illness that finally took her life.
If it is any use to you, you are welcome\nto it...

[Cause of death: Illness]\nA man who went to Quarrycrest to work in the mines. He was lucky enough to strike a seam of gold ore and send money back home. However, the dust he inhaled while mining accumulated in his lungs and gave him a terminal condition.
I dug this up myself...\nThe mines, you know...

[Cause of death: Assassination]\nAn apothecary from the reign of Pardis II. He was approached by Prince Osasuna and tasked with the creation of a highly poisonous concoction. In return, he was promised a role in the castle, but upon completing the task, he was taken to the slums and silenced.
Even medicine can be poison in\nthe wrong hands.
No. This I cannot give you.
Take this and let me be...
Dying... All of them, dead or dying...

[Cause of death: Illness]\nAn official of Ferian, a kingdom that fell four centuries ago. At the time, the Cliftlands were a mere subject province of Ferian, which greedily mined them for resources. Seeing the poverty this created, he petitioned for more merciful governance, but the king banished him, and he threw in his lot with the poor and downtrodden.
I cannot let you have this.
Share it with the poor and\ndowntrodden.
Nay. This is where I belong now.
You would lead me away from\nthis place?

[Cause of death: Incineration]\nAn entirely ordinary citizen of Valore. With the city cut off from supply lines, he stabbed a friend for the sake of his family. However, he was burned to death in the riots that broke out after.
Sorry, but I can't help you...
I feel like I can trust you...to make\nproper use of it...
No... I don't want to hurt anyone else...

[Cause of death: Poisoning]\nA merchant who made a fortune in agricultural speculation. Born a poor farmer, she admired Herminia's success and mimicked her ways. This ended up making her a rival to her hero.
Not much of a head for business,\nI see...
I'm taking a shine to you.

[Cause of death: Strangulation]\nA former arena champ. After arguing over a fight's winnings, he beat the promoter to death, fled to Valore, and became a syndicate bodyguard. His prodigious strength made him the first target for a rival organization.
I'll show you...what a real champ\ncan do!
Test me again when you're ready...
You'll be the next champ...\nI can feel it...

[Cause of death: Assassination]\nSlain by one of Herminia's assassins while attempting to kill her. Losing his beloved Valore was more than he could bear, and he still protects his turf even in death.
Come back once you're ready\nto put up a fight...
What do you...want to tell me...?
I don't have...to listen to you...

[Cause of death: Assassination]\nSlain by one of Herminia's assassins while attempting to kill her. He has been freed from his deep delusion now that he has found someone to whom he can entrust his beloved Valore.
Come back once you're ready\nto put up a fight...
I can't really be bothered...but I guess\nI should.

[Cause of death: Cut down]\nA wealthy Woodlands man. He hoped to monopolize Herminia's distribution network known as "the witch's fortune." He had invited Valore's most influential man, Bargello, to dinner, intending to assassinate him.
I bet we could do good things\ntogether...

[Cause of death: Powder addiction]\nA young man who was once an aristocrat. After trying powder on a whim, it became an addiction that led to a debauched life. He continued to smoke the substance until his death.
I'll tell you everything you wanna\nknow...
I don't know what you're talking\nabout...
I guess this is how it has to be...

[Cause of death: Falling object]\nOwner and chef of the restaurant LeRuiz. There was a time in which he was employed by Herminia as her personal chef. He reopened the restaurant after her death, but during the chaos following the attack on Delcado, a vase fell on his head and he died shortly after.
We're closed for a private party...

[Cause of death: Suicide]\nOne of a hunting tribe that was wiped out by Herminia. She stood against Herminia—who was spreading powder in her town—and lost. As she verged on becoming an addict herself, she took her own life while she still had the will to resist.
I can't give this to you...
I have a lot of regrets...
The past is in the past...
[Cause of death: Hypothermia]\nA man who frequented the tavern on Valore's main street. Never found without a bottle of liquor, he always drank until he passed out—even on frosty midwinter nights.
Think I can d-d-drink if I go with you...?
[Cause of death: Unknown]\nA woman who worked as a prostitute in a brothel. She is absolutely livid about something.
I told you to stay away from me!
This town is mine! Mine, mine, MINE!
Shut up... Get out of here...

[Cause of death: Execution]\nA woman who worked as a prostitute—the most popular in her brothel. Charmed by Lord Taviani's magnanimity, she became his lover and supported him wholly. Shortly after his death, she was captured and executed.

[Cause of death: Suicide]\nAn executioner who served King Pardis III to put food on his family's table. He had sent a great number of criminal heads rolling on his master's orders, earning him the animosity of the Edoras people. When his son was sentenced to be executed for treason, he himself carried out the punishment and took his own life soon after.
No matter who...I must carry out\nmy duty...
I won't take orders from you...

[Cause of death: Cut down]\nA former Edoras soldier. After his commander incurred the wrath of King Pardis III and was put to death, he turned to a life of thievery from his Gray Grotto base. He attacked Lebrandt and <chara_name> who'd come in search of adorabloom, but was killed in turn.
I want to forget it all, but...

[Cause of death: Slain in battle]\nA deeply patriotic Altinian soldier, this former shepherd left his flock to join the army and safeguard his country. In a battle against Edoras, he found himself face-to-face with the enemy general.
Please, use this...\nFor King Solon's sake...
[Cause of death: Cut down by robber]\nA pauper whom Elrica had been close with. She fell victim to the crime wave that swept the land in the chaos following Mahrez's death.
I am sorry...\nI have nothing to offer you.
This is all I have left...
I will only speak with Princess Elrica...

[Cause of death: Starvation]\nA child whom Elrica had often played with. The kindhearted paupers in the slums had been looking after him in lieu of his absent parents, but as the political situation worsened, soon there was no one left to pay him any mind.
[Cause of death: Poisoning]\nThe firstborn of King Pardis II. With his many fine leadership traits, he was expected to take over the throne, but was fatally poisoned by his younger brother Osasuna.
I shan't give it to you.
[Cause of death: Illness]\nAn attendant to Queen Ulrica of Edoras. After Ulrica died by her own hand, she fell ill from a surfeit of sorrow.
So you'll use force to make\nme comply...
Using force will get you nothing.
Please, if it can be of any use—\ntake this.
Ah, those were the days...\nSuch wonderful days indeed...
There are no words to express\nmy grief...
I'll make sure folks don't get\nthe wrong idea.
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA merchant whose village was sacked by enemy forces while he was selling his wares in the capital.
You want to hear of my homeland?
I'd rather not talk about it...
This ought to be good for business!
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA guard of Hornburg. He's proud to be able to do his part to guard a once thought impervious mountain castle town that has existed for over two hundred years.
You want a fight? You've got one!
You won't take it that easily!
I'll tell you what I know.
You won't convince me that easy!
Bard Who Sings of a Dragon Slayer
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nHornburg's premiere bard. She sings of the first king Beowulf's triumph over a dragon in his late years.
You wish to hear me sing, don't you?
Ah ah ah. Not for you.
I haven't finished composing\nmy song yet...
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nThe head of the church in Hornburg. He is blessed with a flock of devout clerics and has high hopes for the kingdom and the Order of the Sacred Flame.
May the Sacred Flame light your path.
May the Sacred Flame guide us.
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA woman married to a Hornburgian knight. She heard the tale of the twin blades of Hornburg, and came to witness their skill and friendship for herself.
I can't and won't give you that.
I don't care what's going on outside...
I can't leave the children behind.
I could use a break from my daily\nroutine.

[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA noble's daughter who is training to become a Hornburgian knight. She rides with the fearless knight Ogland as she learns about loyalty to country and the kinship of comrades.
I shall join you as a means\nto refine my skills!

[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA guard of Hornburg since the reign of the previous king. He has dedicated his life to his duties and has fostered a good relationship with the townspeople. As a member of the Orsterran Alliance led by the hero Tytos, he bravely fought against the clan from the south.
This is not yours to have...
There is not much to tell, but...
I will lend what help I can...

[Cause of death: Old age]\nA lone sheep nestled on the edge of Hornburg. It feeds on grass as it watches over all with a gentle gaze. When civil war broke out, it barely batted an eye. To this day, it continues to watch over all of those who have died.

[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA spendthrift Hornburgian soldier who was granted a fortune by a famed soldier from the Riverlands. Alas, that was the one night he failed to lock the side door...
No... No one's ever bribing me again...
Are you going to trick me too...?
No... No one's ever bribing me again...
I have to get out of this castle...\nThe shame is too great...
[Cause of death: Slain in civil war]\nA knight of the Kingdom of Hornburg. In the eyes of King Alfred, he is second only to the twin blades and is trusted to protect the castle.
I trust this will be of use...
You would truly like to know...?

[Cause of death: Incineration]\nA lady-in-waiting who had sworn loyalty to Herminia. Where once a syndicate boss named Gregory had worked her like a slave, she discovered her innate self-worth under Herminia.
I cannot give this to you, I'm afraid.
Please do not bother Lady Herminia...

[Cause of death: Burned by molten gold]\nThe eldest daughter of House Bertolini, a family whose fortune comes from mining. When the previous head of the household died, she disowned her youngest sister Herminia, so she and her younger sister Anita would have the family's riches all to themselves. Soon after, she began plotting to be rid of Anita as well.
Praise me...\nTell me...what I want to hear...
You must be after my riches...

[Cause of death: Burned by molten gold]\nThe middle daughter of House Bertolini, a family whose fortune comes from mining. When the previous head of the household died, she disowned her younger sister Herminia, so she and her elder sister Gabriella would have the family riches all to themselves. Soon after, she began plotting to be rid of Gabriella as well.
I would never work with you...

[Cause of death: Slain in battle]\nA celebrated Theatropolitan actress. In her early days in Everhold, a certain minstrel introduced her to Auguste the playwright. It was under his tutelage that her talent flourished.
For <i>his</> sake, I would give up\nanything and everything...
What is it you want to know now?
[Cause of death: Strangulation]\nA Theatropolitan actress. It was her life's greatest joy to have been able to receive guidance directly from her idol, Auguste the playwright.
No, but I need this, you see.
I'd rather not talk to anyone but\nMaster Auguste...
Cover-ups are no easy thing,\nyou realize.
May peace reign on the island...

This maiden is charged with keeping the ancient remnants safe. She has also been ordered by the head priestess to cooperate with any visitor embodying the teachings of Bifelgan, the Trader, god of merchants.
As Bifelgan says, "The treasure offered\nmust match the value requested."
I see you know the value of things.\nTake this.
I have a duty to keep these remnants\nsafe...

This maiden-in-training believes that only digital strength can keep the rings safe, and plunged her hands into the searing sands of Wellspring to toughen them up. A jab of her finger can pierce meteorite armor; a chop of her hand can snap the horn of a gigantes.
Holy, holy, holy! A finger is strength,\na fist is sustenance, a strike of the hand\nis life.
I'm in the middle of my training.
It is time for my fingers to fight\nfor Orsterra.
Nothing can withstand the jab\nof my finger.

This maiden-in-training is still a novice to the dance. The dance of the ring maidens uses gesture, tread, and incantation to inscribe a kind of unseen magic circle and recreate sorceries that were once lost. Fragments of these techniques have been repurposed by other parties, such as the Hornburgian Church's ceremonial dancers.
What do you think of my dance?
I ask that you don't speak to me\nwhile I'm training.
I'm still in training, so I can't\ncome and go as I please.
Seeing the world will surely be\nan education in itself.

This former ring maiden now uses her deep font of wisdom and experience, built up over years on the island, to educate the children of Agapea. Her lessons cover a vast range of topics, and the shrinekeeper Ataraxia is among her former students.
Well, if you have a use for it...
I can't talk about the island.
Aren't you expecting a little much\nfrom a woman of my years?
The years haven't been kind to my joints,\nbut if I can be of service...

This child training to be a ring maiden is a quick study who works especially hard at her training because of the praise it wins her from grown-ups when she demonstrates what she knows. However, her true passion is ancient remnants, and she sometimes sneaks off to secretly operate the Guardian.
Um...do you know anything about her?

This child lived in Hornburg when she was just a baby. When the kingdom fell into chaos and collapsed, a young priest in the Order of the Sacred Flame rescued her and brought her to this island. She knows nothing of her origin, but idolizes the ring maidens.
A self-proclaimed feline maiden. Watches over every cat on the continent from its lair on this hidden island...or so it claims.
It is my duty to watch over the cats\nfrom this island...

Guardian of the island of Agapea since ancient times. Tradition among the maidens holds that it was created by the gods along with the divine rings and shielded the island from the wars of sorcery that raged during the time of the ancient civilization.

Shrinekeeper and head priestess, Ataraxia is the highest authority on Agapea, and formerly served as Thumb. Strict but kind, she is beloved by many of the maidens, and she cherishes them as her daughters in turn. The island is known only to a select few within the Order as a means to protect it from subversive elements, and Ataraxia is highly vigilant to anything that could rupture this veil of secrecy.
Even to the Chosen One,\nI cannot relinquish this.
Use it wisely, O Ringbearer chosen.
A maiden with strong powers of healing. She is mindful of the health of the other maidens, and her handcrafted trinkets made with love and prayer are said to heal their wearers.
That is a rather poor estimate.
Are you in need of healing?
I see you need my help.

An old woman who used to run a farm in Wishvale. The vegetables she grew were famous for their rich flavor, thanks to the care she took in tending them and the soil. She is currently convinced the marshlands on the path to Herminia's manse are her old fields.

An old farmer who lives in Wishvale. She's very meticulous about her work, even paying close attention to the quality of the soil, so people clamor for everything she grows, especially her flavorful vegetables. She toils in the fields day after day to provide food for the people of her town.
I'm sorry, but I can't let you have it.
Do you want to learn more\nabout the fields?
Can't you see I'm a little busy\nright now!?
What is it you want to know?
I can't spare the time right now!
I don't want to leave this place.
You say there's someplace better?\nWell, I'd like to see this place for\nmyself.
<chara_name>'s father and Wishvale's captain of the watch. Everyone in town trusts him with their lives.
Don't let one or two losses stop you.\nCome at me again when you're ready.
I've been waiting for this moment.
You need to train some more.
<chara_name>'s mother. She lovingly watches over her darling husband and child, and makes the tastiest <favorite_dish_name>.
It's too soon for you to have that.
Sure, but don't tell your father.

Stia's father and the carpenter responsible for most of the buildings in town, all of which excel in both form and function, granting warmth and peace to everyone. His one consolation after his wife's passing has been watching his daughter grow.
Hahaha! That's not something\nI can just hand over.
You want it that badly?\nAll right, you got me. It's yours!

A priestess who has served Wishvale for many years. She liked to take walks when she was younger, but has lately become bedridden. Realizing that it might be time for her to retire, she plans on nominating her granddaughter, Laurana, as the next priestess.
I'm sorry, dear, but I can't give that\nto you.

A deacon who serves Wishvale's church. Some believe her to be more knowledgeable about the Sacred Flame's teachings than even the priests of Flamesgrace, perhaps due to the fact that she was raised by a priestess.
I'm terribly sorry, but I'm too attached\nto that.
You really want it that much?\nThen please, take it.
You wish to speak with me?
I'm sorry. Maybe some other time.

A member of Wishvale's town watch. He was born into a hunting tribe from the Woodlands, but he came to live in this town after losing his parents during a hunt. He's like an older brother to <chara_name>.
A young man who lives in Wishvale. Once known as an awful mischief-maker, he turned over a new leaf after being lambasted by his childhood friend, Nellum.
A young woman who lives in Wishvale. She used to be worried that her mischievous friend, Lutus, might fall in with the wrong people. Well-known for being scarier than a gigantes when she gets mad.
I'm saving it to share with Lutus...
If you want it that badly, please,\nbe my guest.

An old farmer who lives in Wishvale. She's very meticulous about her work, even paying close attention to the quality of the soil, so people clamor for everything she grows, especially her flavorful vegetables.
So be it. Take good care of it, all right?
I'm a little tied up right now.
A gentle old man who lives in Wishvale. He was born in Atlasdam and studied agriculture there. Now he puts that knowledge to use helping his wife with the farmwork.
Sorry, but I'm not inclined to\ngive that away.
It's got some years on it,\nbut it's still good.
A young man who lives in Wishvale. He may seem timid, but he can spring into action when it comes down to it.
This is too important to me.
Illian and Olive's only daughter. She can be a bit precocious, and likes to use big words whenever she can.
Hehe. Trying to haggle a child?\nYou've got some nerve!
If you really want it that bad,\nI guess you can have it.
A farmer who lives in Wishvale. He's a religious man who never misses a day of church, and he adores his daughter, Lila, more than anything in the world.
I'm sorry, but I can't give this\nto you.
Sure. I don't mind. Take what you like.
I've got a lot on my mind...\nLet's talk later.

A farmer who lives in Wishvale. A fun-loving woman who enjoys surprising those around her, she once learned the skills of a dancer just to see the look on people's faces. Lately, she's been teaching her daughter, Lila, extremely difficult words in order to surprise her husband, Illian.
Hehe. A graceful move, don't you think?
And that's it for my show.\nHere, have this!
No, you can't look! Go away!
Have you upset someone?\nDon't worry. I'll handle this.

A watchman who was born in the Highlands, but settled down in Wishvale after meeting Carabell, who he later married. When he's not working at the tavern with his wife, he can be found patrolling the town.
I give everything my all, even training!
Phew... That was a close one.\nI can't go losing to my junior!
I wanted to let you have your moment.\n...Just kidding. You earned this.
I'm busy right now.\nCan it wait?
A friend of Lila's. He is always at the mercy of the unyielding girl. Eating his fill of Granny Margot's vegetables is his favorite part of the Day of Reverence.
A young boy who lives in Wishvale. A longtime fan of Granny Margot's vegetables, he helps tend the fields to learn all that he can about farming and cultivation.
A resident of Wishvale who's unmatched in both his strength and love for alcohol. He's proud that the townspeople always come to him whenever they need someone to do some physical labor.
Let me have a drink first...

A man who was born and raised in Wishvale. When the town was attacked, he found shelter in an abandoned shack and has been living there ever since. Sometimes he goes to the nearby town to do some work, but most of the money he earns ends up going towards the alcohol he drinks.
Not bad... Here, you earned it!
Hahaha! Not bad for an old man, huh?
A scholar who has lived in a shack on the outskirts of Wishvale for a great many years. After the town was destroyed, he took in <chara_name> and Stia.
No, the time isn't right yet.
<chara_name>'s childhood friend. A strong-willed girl who's always helping her father, Lavore, who works as a carpenter.
Hey, I have something to tell you.

A squirrel born in the forests near Wishvale. It buries nuts to save them for later, but can't seem to remember where they are when "later" comes. Though unfortunate for the squirrel, this has greatly helped reforest the area.
Maybe you can help me find them.
I don't have time to talk.
Aw, nuts...\nFine. But just this once!

An MC called in by Adrian the Promoter to host the monster arena battles. Though he likes gladiators, he is also a fan of monsters, and as such does his research to make sure he can get the crowd excited. He ensures his vocal chords are sufficiently warmed up before matches.
I'm warmed up and ready to go!
Ahem. Ahhh... Ahhh...\nHm, my throat's a little sore.

A gladiator who arrived in town after hearing about the unusual arena. He always has to hold back against human opponents due to his incredible strength, so he is excited to go all out against the monsters here.
Not now. I'm preparing for\nmy next battle.
A gladiator who surrendered immediately after the start of his debut match in Victors Hollow. He couldn't bring himself to fight his opponent who bore a striking resemblance to his sister.
Maybe this is where my dreams will\ncome true...

A dedicated young priest who works at the Cathedral of Ash. His enthusiasm has earned him the hearts of those around him, and he is more than familiar with the cathedral's affairs. He greatly admires Sazantos, and he secretly harbors feelings of superiority toward clerics who are unaware of the Flameguard's existence.
If I may be of some help...

A deacon often seen at the Cathedral of Ash. Though his family has close ties with the cardinals and he himself has been serving the church for over twenty years. However, he has failed to move up in the ranks due to his unseemly behavior; a reality he has come to accept over time.
I have no time for this now.
A baby named <chara_name> who is fast asleep with a blissful look on their face.
A woman named Pharamea whose heartwarming presence is wondrous yet familiar.
A young boy named Sazantos who strives to grow stronger to protect the newest member of his family.
Their hometown destroyed, <chara_name> is consumed by the dark flame and instilled with a burning desire for vengeance.
Their hometown destroyed, <chara_name> harbors a burning desire for vengeance yet wallows in despair.
A man who has long traveled with his trusted companions.\n\nAll he feels now is a deep gratitude toward his fellow travelers and <chara_name>.

A scholarly individual who has pursued studies in military tactics, art, and music. He has a penchant for putting "fantabulous" items into treasure chests.\n\nBut to him, the most valuable treasure of all are the smiles of the travelers around him.
This is one of my favorite compositions.\nPlease enjoy.
I'm sorry, but I cannot part with this.

A man who landed upon the continent ages ago. Since then, he has helped so many others that he has forgotten his own purpose. A kind yet tragic figure.\n\nHe shares a familial bond with Rugis through his cat.

A man who houses different skills in each hand.\n\nBecause he excels in so many fields of work, a somewhat troubling culture of initially asking him to do everything has rooted itself into the foundation.
A cait mage who creates art that breathes life into the people of the world.\n\nCountless travelers have come under her care. Somewhere out there, she is breathing life into another.
An artist who tackles his subjects straight on in earnest and cuts out a moment in time.\n\nWhen standing before him, all are compelled to shed the armor surrounding their souls.
An artist who, along with Rugis and Delan, has shaped the world as we know it.\n\nEven when the other two could not find the words, his brush would masterfully paint their thoughts onto the canvas.
A wordsmith of great skill and love.\n\nAt times tempestuous, at times gentle, he has deftly crafted the story of this world along with Rugis and the others.
A travelling musician.\n\nBy singing of, embellishing, and at times even hindering the journeys of travelers, his songs carve out a path to adventure from start to finish.

A traveling gourmand and world-builder.

He possesses an impressive memory and encyclopedic knowledge of the world. As Delan always says, "When you have lost your way, ask Negiwata."

It is said that he is one of a chosen few that Rugis trusts to watch his back.
A man who has supported the reconstruction of the town from the shadows, away from the fanfare.\n\nHis kind eyes see far and wide, and have captured many sights over the years.

A man who walked the continent to create a map that would become an invaluable resource for all travelers.

He boldly defied those who worried that "a detailed map may be too difficult to create."

That the completed map even shows the locations of treasure chests is said to be his achievement.

A man who is always destitute for some reason.

Adding to the puzzle is that he is a master architect that can build a house several times quicker than your average carpenter.

He is shy and quiet, but is quite funny when you can get him to talk.

A storyteller who deftly spins engaging tales with wit and humor.

Though an honored veteran of his craft, he still sees a long road ahead of him.

His journey has just begun.
Honest. Detailed. Motivated. A studious man that is liked by everyone.\n\nThe grit and effort in each of his footsteps created the many paths of travel in this world.

An artist who observes moving objects such as falling snow, and all manner of things that adorn the world, which he then recreates with a meticulous hand.\n\nIt is said that the world moves as fluidly as it does because of his efforts.
A dedicated artisan who facilitated the ease of movement that all who have gathered here enjoy.\n\nIt is thanks to his efforts that all the others were able to take a step forward.
A jeweler who specializes in delicate ring crafting.\n\nIt is said that the dullest of jewels regain their luster when put into his hands.

A high seas sailor who carves out routes in the pathless oceans.

Even with the fate of his ship and his entire crew in his hands, he never once faltered.

He will continue to guide ships through the oft treacherous seas, like a shining beacon emanating from the harbor.

A gladiator who always stands for the people, at times taking center stage in the arena, and at times formulating detailed plans behind the scenes.\n\nBecause she always acts with people's hearts in mind, she has saved countless souls in the past, and will continue to save more in the future.

A dramatist whose work is much celebrated on another continent. He was summoned here by Rugis to collaborate.\n\nIt is said that any and all matters of diplomacy that Rugis could not see to himself was handled deftly by Mineji.

Ryoma's mentor. A cunning strategist who specializes in banishing darkness. He is also a fierce warrior who can charge the enemy alone and take the hill if need be.\n\nHis favorite quote is, "Old soldiers do not die, they just fade away."
A man who paves paths of travel.\n\nWhen lacking necessities, he is always able to procure them on his own somehow. You can wager that his pack will be full of items on his next journey.
I have listened to this song countless\ntimes. It evokes many memories.
It's unfortunate, but I can't sell this\nto you.
A man who, through his unique vision, has breathed life into countless distinctive individuals.\n\nThe people of Orsterra owe their existence to his accomplishments.
A craftsman that oversees the ways, means, and flow of battle.\n\nThrough rain, sleet, and snow, his earnest demeanor has supported others in immeasurable ways.
A man whose sincerity brings both richness and order to his tales.\n\nHe first weaves the path that a good story must follow, then carefully watches the travelers who walk it.
A dramatist whose passion infuses intense heat into every story.\n\nWithout Escon, the tale of this journey would have never seen the light of day.
A jack-of-all-trades who always handles sudden requests from Myano swiftly and efficiently.\n\nHis travels have left an indelible mark in this world; in the towns, the people, and the story.
An inventor of theatrical contraptions that shine light upon the world.\n\nThe light she brings is at times gentle and at times harsh, but it always provides color to the world.
A builder that has constructed roads, towns, and even caves.\n\nOne can only guess how many memories and thoughts reside within a mind that created the entire continent.
An architect that brought reason to the world.\n\nHeeding the teachings of his predecessors, he carefully crafted the continent into its present form.
A Knight Ardante who was tasked with escorting the group along the right path.\n\nHis kind words have encouraged many and guided them towards the light.
A dramatist who infused life into the town and its residents.\n\nBy giving purpose to the everyday folk, he provided more weight and truth to the world.
A playwright who brought love and emotion into the world.\n\nOnly a hand that could fully grasp the human heart and bare it for all to see could have brought such truth to this tale.
A veteran dramatist who has supported this tale for a very long time.\n\nFor every evening she spent with us, a new drama was born.

A scholar and playwright who uses tried and tested (if not dubious) methods to solve various predicaments.

He agreed to join this journey even at the sacrifice of his ailing back.

He is owed a debt that Rugis, Delan, and Myano can never fully repay.
Thank you for traveling with us.

You who seek wealth will begin your journey
in the verdant forests of the Woodlands.

There, you will cross paths with
Herminia, "the Covetous Witch."

Her all-consuming desire has stained
the town of Valore with darkness and depravity.

You who seek fame will begin your journey
in the grassy meadows of the Flatlands.

There, you will cross paths with
Auguste, the genius playwright.

In Theatropolis, city of the arts, many a poor soul
has been led astray by his beguiling words.

You who seek power will begin your journey
in the snow-swept frontiers of the Frostlands.

There, you will cross paths with
Tytos, the hero.

Leader of the Scarlet Wings, a band of former
criminals, he rules Emberglow with an iron fist.
Tale of the Princess and the Knight

Go to Emberglow in the Frostlands.
Meet up with Taviani at the Snowbloom Ruins.

Search for the key to the locked door.

Backed by the might of his private army, the Scarlet Wings, the so-called "hero" Tytos rules Emberglow with an iron fist. But there are those who would resist his quest for greater and greater power...

In Theatropolis, city of the arts, lives Auguste, the most famous playwright in all the realm. His works are praised and loved by all. They are said to even change the hearts of his adoring fans...
Schwartz, a man whose wife was murdered by Auguste, seeks revenge against the mad playwright. He visits Valore in hopes of finding a means to kill his hated foe...
In the name of writing a tragedy, Auguste has tortured poor Francesca and her child. And so Schwartz sets out for the amphitheatre to snuff out the playwright's fame...and his life.
So did <chara_name> defeat the three greatest villains in the realm—the masters of wealth, power, and fame—and bring peace to their hometown. It is then that a Knight Ardante appears before them.
Go to Rippletide in the Coastlands.
Meet the ring maidens and seal away the rings.

Go to the Edoras Army's main encampment.

Power-hungry King Pardis III invades neighboring nations with no regard for the wishes of his subjects, his soldiers, or even his daughter. So it is that General Mahrez is sent to war against the Westerlings.
Travel to Clearbrook in the Riverlands.
Framed for the assassination of General Mahrez, <chara_name> has no means to approach the mad King Pardis. Meanwhile, a proud swordsman sets off on a journey to save a nation...
Meet up with Charles in Sunshade.

It is revealed that the mysterious swordsman El is actually Princess Elrica of Edoras. To save her from the clutches of King Pardis III, Princess Alaune hatches a daring plot...
Meet with your ally in the Riverlands.

Vowing to carry on the mission of her ill-fated sister, Elrica, Princess Alaune resolves to stand against the mad King Pardis. She vows to fight side by side with <chara_name>, and begins preparations for the coming battle...

Together with Prince Richard, <chara_name> and Princess Alaune defeat the jester Gonska, who had taken control of the Kingdom of Riven. But as the brave princess faces the decisive battle, she is pierced by a single arrow...

Tips for the Traveler: Part I
The damage you deal with standard attacks is based upon the physical attack value of your equipped weapon.
Damage dealt by skills that specify weapon type is based upon the physical attack value of that weapon.
Damage dealt by elemental skills is based upon the highest elemental attack value of all that character's equipped weapons.
The potency of healing skills is based on the user's elemental defense attribute.
Tips for the Traveler: Part II
Depending on your progress in the game, there may be times where you cannot access\nthe menu or use certain features. Continue with the story to restore these features.
The success rates of Path Actions are based on your influence, which can be increased\nthrough such actions as progressing through the story.
Viator—a warrior skilled in the arts of provocation and evasion.
Reime—a warrior who overpowers enemies with a flurry of attacks.
Ludo—a merchant capable of weakening foes while dealing plenty of damage.
Carinda—a merchant with a wide range of versatile abilities.
Celsus—a thief who baits enemies into easily avoided attacks.
Heidne—a thief who steals from enemies and weakens them with her talents.
Esperre—an apothecary adept at curing status ailments and reviving incapacitated allies.
Macy—an apothecary who cripples foes with status ailments.
Phenn—a hunter ready and willing to act as protector to allies in need.
Delitia—a hunter with a keen eye for exploiting enemy weaknesses.
Pius—a cleric equally skilled at healing wounds and dealing damage.
Xerc—a scholar who commands both ice and wind.
Laurana—a cleric versed in the arts of regenerative healing and resurrection.
Alexia—a scholar who has mastered lightning and dark magic.
Goodwin—a dancer who wields lightning to hinder the enemy's actions.
Saoirse—a dancer whose skills can strengthen allies.
Olberic—a warrior capable of fighting with either swords or polearms.
Tressa—a merchant with a rather unorthodox fighting style.
Therion—a thief who can hold his own in combat.

Thank you for downloading the demo!
A few things to know before you set out on your journey...
In this demo, you can play for three hours from the start of the game (as recorded in your saved data file).
You can choose your own protagonist, just as in the full version of the game.
You will also be able to carry over your saved data and use it in the full game.
In this demo, prepare to kick off an expansive adventure spanning across the continent. Happy travels!

This saved data cannot be loaded, as it has
reached the maximum play time of three hours.

*You can carry this data over to the full game.
The adventure will continue in the full game...
But you are still free to roam the world for the remainder of the three hours!
Help Wishvale's restoration or embark on other adventures!

Three hours of play time have passed, which brings us to
the end of this demo. But not the end of your adventure—
you can carry your saved data over to the full game to
pick up where you left off!

Meet over thirty travelers and build a town of your own
as you continue the epic saga of the divine rings!

Embark on an adventure of your own creation.

Save your completed game?

*Once saved, you will be unable to load this file
in the demo version, but you may carry it over to
continue playing in the full version of the game.
*This quest cannot be played in the demo.

Remember...

You can become anyone. You can go anywhere.

As long as there is a flicker of hope in your heart...
The protagonist's childhood friend. A strong-willed girl who's always helping her father, Lavore, who works as a carpenter.\n\n"We're in this together."

A member of Wishvale's town watch and a skilled hunter.

He's like an older brother to the protagonist with his friendly and encouraging ways.

"For the future of Wishvale!"

A deacon who manages Wishvale's church with her grandmother, Priestess Yaya.

She's kind and thoughtful, and is like an older sister to the protagonist.

"May the Sacred Flame ever shine its warmth upon Wishvale..."
A renowned merchant who operates in the Sunlands. Though many make light of him due to his youthful appearance, his business acumen is not to be underestimated.\n\n"Business as usual."

A man working as a bodyguard at the tavern in Valore. His knife skills are second to none. He is extremely devoted to the Flamebringer and prays every single day.\n\n"By the way... Would you say faith is important to you?"
An itinerant apothecary who is generally easygoing, but becomes deadly serious when it comes to combatting disease.\n\n"Allow me to tend to your health. It would be my pleasure!"
A scholar who hails from the Royal Academy of Atlasdam. She is currently journeying across the land to research ruins and compile her findings and observations.\n\n"Come, come! The truth awaits us!"

You plannin' on goin' up against\nthe witch, too?
Doesn't matter how much people\nhate her—her wealth only grows.

Horrifying... A witch who knows\nnothing but her own desires.
Mercilessly crushing anyone who stood in her way,
People whispered of her with fear, saying...
Lady Herminia. The preparations\nfor the ball are complete.
You're really thinkin' of takin' her\non alone, huh?
Actually, a buncha half-wits like\nyou are gatherin' on main street.
You should go join 'em, assuming\nthey'll accept you, that is.

How do you plan to pull this off?
We'll never even make it through\nthe front gate!

Huh... Seems like you have\na grudge against her.
Hold on, Bargello.\nCan we really trust them?

We're a new family around here.\nOur goal is to take the town back\nfrom Herminia.
I don't know what business you\nhave with her, and I'm not going\nto ask.
But if you want to work with us,\nwe need proof that we can trust\nyou.
Exactly. We have no idea\nwho you are.
For all we know, you could be\nmore of a hindrance than a help.
Haha, sorry 'bout that.\nWe like to play coy.
Pretty steep for a single flower.
Who put you up to this?
Nothing good will come of this.\nGet out while you still can.
But they said that if I don't\nsell this...
What do you think you're doing,\nmeddling in our business!?
Those shabby clothes make\nit clear you're no customers.
You'd be wise not to judge\na book by its cover.
...And you'd be wise to\nkeep your mouth shut.
How did I know this was coming?
Heh! At least we get to have\nsome fun.
Instead of a flower, looks like\nwe've picked ourselves a fight.
Hah! Don't tell me you were\ntrying to join us when you can't\neven hold your own in a fight!
You know what they call people\nlike you?
Let's see what you can do.
Using children to peddle their\npoison, are they now?
Those greedy bastards!\nAlways looking down on us!
I should have stuck my dagger\nin their smug faces!
Settle down, Pierro. Look at this.
Even smug bastards are\ngood for something.
I just happened to find this\nin one of their pockets.
A ball for fat cats with fat purses...
You really helped us out\nback there.
We were born and raised in\nthis town,
and we've watched it go to shit.
...But no more watching.\nWe're going to take it back.
You can fight, and you seem like\nyou have your own business with\nthe Witch.
Well, you've proven we can\ntrust you.
So we've got no more objections.
Do you still want to join\nour cause?
Don't be a chicken!\nI promise we're not so bad!
...Think carefully, friend.\nA chance like this doesn't\ncome along every day.
All right!\nA new addition to our ranks!
Sorry for doubting you. I'd be\nhappy to treat you to some pasta,\nif you want.
Anyhow, we got what we came for.\nLet's leave before we make a scene.
We'll be waiting for you there.
Look at the size of this place!
They say Lady Herminia started\nout as a small-time criminal.
Then she took control of the\npowder operation...
Rumors say she's so rich she\ncould buy the whole continent...
A palace built on powder, eh?
Not just that. Word has it she'll even\nsell her body...for the right price.
They say she's so gorgeous that even\nclergymen are reduced to dogs in heat.
...And that she's slept with the\nwealthiest men in the realm.
Wait a minute, Pierro. You're\nsaying Lady Herminia's a harlot?
I've never met a harlot who lives\nin a mansion like this.
Heh. Even prostitution can pay\nif you choose your clients right.
All right. Enough yapping from\nyou lot.
Now listen. Some of the fattest\ncats from the underworld will be\nat this banquet.
...Some of whom might be able\nto help us get what we want.
Use who we can use, right?
We're going to make a name\nfor ourselves here tonight.
Looks like this party's already in\nfull swing!
All right!\nTime to have some fun!
She's looking to expand her\nclientele, no doubt.
After all, no one can resist\nLady Herminia's "product."
My servants have prepared a feast\nof the finest delicacies in the realm.
I want a piece of the action!
Looks like you're the only one\nwho remembers what we came\nhere for.
But try to contain your fury,\nor else we'll be found out.
You are familiar with\nmy product, yes?
One whiff and you'll be transported\nto a world of sheer bliss.
Why waste your time struggling\nthrough a dreary existence?
And I promise that all your\ndreams will be beautiful...
Do feel free to indulge some more.
But watch out.\nYou may never want to return...
Envelop yourselves in rapture and\nbliss, my dear guests!
He used to sell his product here\nuntil Lady Herminia took over.
...He can't be too happy about\nthe current situation.
Lord Taviani, I presume?\nI've heard so much about you...
And you, Lady Herminia, are\neven lovelier than the tales say...
I can scarce imagine what beauty\nlies beneath that mask...
Forgive me. My line of work\nhas earned me many foes...
...And your riches are the greatest\nin the realm.
You are kind to say so.
In truth, I have found myself\na troublesome foe as well.
For many years, my house held\nsway in these lands.
...Until a greedy witch stole from\nus what was rightfully ours.
A word of advice, Lord Taviani.
You'd be wise to burn your\nneighbor's house before your\nown bursts into flame.
Sage advice, indeed.\nI thank you, my lady.
Your sense of humor is most\nimpressive.
But has the tension in the air\nparched your throat?
Mayhap milord would enjoy\na glass of my finest wine?
As a toast to our newfound\nfriendship...
...Forgive me, my lady. Spirits\nhave never agreed with me.
Is there no one here who will\ndrink this wine in my stead!?
Come forth, now! We cannot\ndisrespect the lady of the house!
G-get back here!\nHave you lost your wits!?
It would be my honor to partake\nof this fine vintage, my lady.
I cannot resist a good wine—\nespecially one with such a...\ndangerous aroma.
...Oho! Might I ask your name,\nyoung man?
An excellent vintage, I must say.\nFrom the Wold region, no doubt?
You've got nerve, my boy!\nI like that!
You're a fascinating young man,\naren't you?
Is there something on my face?\nA mosquito, perhaps?
You know how Lady Herminia\nmakes her "product," right?

Fields stretching out for\nleagues and leagues...

Well? I would hear your report.

N-no, my lady. Some of us\nhave fallen sick, you see...

Maybe you should work harder\ninstead of making pathetic excuses!
Did you get blood on my dress?
This dress was made from the\nfinest silk of the merino worm...
Do you know how many of you\nI could buy for the price of this\ndress?
Your pathetic groveling won't\nmake me any money!
You make me sick...all of you.
Why? <i>Why</> do these dirty slaves\nbleed the same color as I!?
This time, bleed white.\nBleed <i>white</>, I say!
I cannot take this...\nI cannot take this anymore!

Yes... Scatter like the insects\nyou are.

Hehe. We should come back sometime.

Heh... You look like a strong one.

Yeah, right. You're just a rabid dog\nslavering for coin.

We did it!\nServes the bastard right!

We're going to make it big...
Mark my words... We're going to\ntake everything from her...
We're going to take back\nthis town...
...And make it a real place to\nlive again.

Your time has come and gone...
You really helped us out\nback there, <chara_name>.
We took a bit of a detour,\nbut we should be getting back.
This path should lead us straight\ninto town.
Let's meet up again in front of\nTaviani's place.

Lady Herminia's too powerful.\nThere's no way we can fight her\nhead-on.

We'll play the role of the dog until\nwe get close enough to bite her.
Ruff! Arf!\nYeah, I can do this!

...But I know I can count on you.

she rules Valore with money and fear.

...Is Lady Herminia.\nIsn't that so, old man?

in hopes of bringing down the witch.

Pierro, you'll deal with Tomazzo\nand his men.
